title: AI Developer / Full-Stack Developer for Mobile App & Web Platform
duration: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: We’re looking for a senior-level AI developer / Full-Stack Developer to build an AI-heavy mobile app and web platform focused on a coaching solution for a specific niche (details shared privately). The product will include both a mobile app (mobile-first) for iOS and Android and a web application, built as a scalable platform with strong focus on performance, security, and user experience. Cross-platform / hybrid development (e.g. React Native or Flutter) is preferred. This is an MVP development project with complex AI integration, including smart recommendations, personalized responses, and automation logic. Experience with advanced AI systems (e.g. LLMs, RAG, or similar architectures) is highly valuable. Key requirements: Strong full-stack development and backend development experience Building and integrating REST APIs / API integration Experience with AI systems and scalable architecture Authentication / login / user roles and secure data handling Clean, modern UX & UI across mobile and web Handling real-time data / updates where needed Building a robust and scalable SaaS platform Clear communication and ability to work independently We’re looking for someone who can take ownership, think in systems, and deliver a high-quality AI-driven product. Long-term collaboration is possible after the MVP phase.

title: Lead Full Stack Developer (React.js + Node.js)
description: We are looking for a highly experienced Lead Full Stack Developer who can take ownership of end-to-end development across frontend (React/Next.js) and backend (Node.js). This is a high-commitment role (12 hours/day) suited for someone who can operate as a technical leader as well as an execution-focused engineer. You will be responsible not just for coding, but also for architecture, decision-making, mentoring, and delivery ownership. --- Commitment: - 12 hours/day (mandatory) - Long-term engagement (3–6+ months, extendable) - Overlap with IST or US time zones preferred --- Responsibilities: - Lead full-stack development using React.js / Next.js + Node.js - Architect scalable and maintainable systems - Define and enforce best practices across the codebase - Work closely with product stakeholders to finalize requirements - Design APIs and collaborate on backend integrations - Handle performance optimization and scalability challenges - Mentor junior developers and conduct code reviews - Ensure timely delivery with high-quality output --- Required Skills: - 8–12+ years of experience in software development - Strong expertise in: - React.js (hooks, performance optimization, architecture) - Next.js (SSR, SSG, SEO) - Node.js (Express / API design) - Solid understanding of: - System design & scalable architecture - REST APIs and async communication - Databases (MongoDB / SQL) - Experience with: - CI/CD pipelines - Git workflows - Testing (Jest, Cypress or similar) --- Good to Have: - Experience with AWS (EC2, S3, DynamoDB) - Knowledge of AI integrations / LLM-based systems - Experience handling distributed teams - Startup experience (fast-paced execution) --- Ideal Candidate: - Can own projects end-to-end without hand-holding - Strong problem solver and decision maker - Comfortable working long hours and delivering consistently - Communicates clearly with stakeholders - Has prior experience in lead or architect roles

title: Next.js Full Stack Developer (AI-Powered)
description: About the Role: Are you a passionate full-stack developer who thrives on tackling complex challenges with cutting-edge AI tools? Do you dream of building innovative app solutions that revolutionize industries like healthcare, fintech, and blockchain? If so, Topflight Apps wants you! We are looking for a fast and efficient Full Stack Developer with strong AI tool skills. You'll be working on projects using Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, Drizzle, shadcn/ui, and TailwindCSS in a fast-paced environment. What You'll Do As our ideal Full Stack Developer, you will: - Efficiently develop new features tailored to project requirements - Leverage AI tools to accelerate development and ship features rapidly - Maintain and enhance existing codebase by fixing bugs, optimizing performance, and managing dependencies - Collaborate with team members to design and implement scalable, maintainable front-end and back-end systems What We're Looking For - Strong expertise in Next.js, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, and SQL databases - Proven expertise in leveraging AI code generation tools to accelerate development - Hands-on experience with shadcn/ui or similar UI libraries - Proficiency in Supabase or similar platforms (e.g., Firebase, PocketBase) - Solid experience with Drizzle or other ORMs for building efficient data structures - 4+ years of experience building production-level web apps - An independent "hacker" mindset with the ability to suggest and execute plans to significantly reduce development timelines - The ability to independently overcome roadblocks and "figure things out" - Accountability for deadlines, coupled with proactive communication regarding adjustments - The mental fortitude to handle a high-stress environment with tight deadlines and rapid development cycles Bonus Points - Prior experience working with agencies - Previous experience building applications for healthcare organizations or handling health data - Experience integrating LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) into applications Important Notes - We are a fully remote company with a global team. Flexibility with time zones and excellent communication skills are crucial - All new Topflight Apps team members undergo a one-month trial period to ensure smooth onboarding and alignment on expectations How to Apply Please ensure all outreach and applications are submitted through the designated job posting platform. Note that any use of alternative platforms will lead to automatic disqualification.

title: Full Stack Developer Needed to Build Scalable SaaS Dashboard + Backend System
description: We’re looking for a strong full stack developer to build a SaaS-style web application with a focus on clean architecture, scalability, and smooth user experience. This is not a simple frontend project — we need someone who can think in systems and build a reliable backend along with a responsive, intuitive UI. 🔧 Scope of Work: -Build a web application with user authentication and role-based access -Develop a structured backend (APIs, database, workflows) -Create a clean dashboard interface with multiple user flows -Implement onboarding (multi-step forms, user setup) -Integrate third-party APIs (if needed) -Ensure scalability and performance for growing user base 🎯 What We’re Looking For: -Strong experience with full stack development (React + Node or similar) -Experience building SaaS platforms, dashboards, or automation systems -Understanding of system design, data structures, and scalability -Ability to take ownership and make technical decisions ⭐ Bonus: -Experience with Supabase / Firebase / PostgreSQL -Experience with queue systems, automation, or high-volume systems -Ability to think about product + UX, not just code

title: Senior Full Stack Developer (React, Next.js + Supabase) for Real-Time Enterprise Quoting Tool
description: Summary Migrate a high-fidelity React/Vite prototype to a production-grade Supabase architecture. This is a complex internal quoting tool featuring real-time collaboration (Google Sheets style) and high-volume data search. The Role: You will be the Lead Developer working directly with the CIO (me). There is no Project Manager. You must be able to take a detailed technical specification and execute it end-to-end with high autonomy. The Stack: * Frontend: React 18 (Vite), TypeScript, AG Grid Enterprise, React Router v6. *Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Edge Functions). * Database: PostgreSQL with pg_trgm and Supabase Wrappers * Auth: Supabase Auth connected to Azure AD (Entra ID). Key Challenges (You must have experience with these): * Real-Time Collaboration: Implementing multi-user editing on a grid with row-level locking and presence indicators (using Supabase Realtime). * High-Volume Search: Architecting a sub-100ms fuzzy search for a 700k SKU catalog using PostgreSQL Trigram indexes. * "Split-Brain" Data: Managing a data layer where the Catalog is synced via SQL Wrappers, but Pricing is fetched live via secure Edge Functions (BFF pattern). * AG Grid: Managing thousands of quote lines with various API calls. 95% of user interaction will be with quote lines using this. Requirements: *5+ years Full Stack experience. * Deep experience with Supabase (specifically Edge Functions, and Realtime). * Proven experience with AG Grid in complex applications. * Ability to be present for weekly check-in meetings sometime between 9am and 4pm (GMT+8). * English fluency. How to Apply (Please Read Carefully) I have attached a detailed Scope of Works document to this job post. You must review this document before applying. In your proposal, please provide: 1. Rough Estimate: Based on the attached Scope of Works, provide a rough estimated timeline (in weeks) and total hours you think this migration would take. I understand this is just an initial estimate. 2. Past Experience: Briefly describe a project where you implemented real-time collaboration or handled large datasets in Supabase. 3. Answer this specific question: "For the 700k SKU search, how would you configure the PostgreSQL index to ensure a user typing 'Dewalt Drill' matches a row named 'Drill Dewalt 18v' in under 100ms?"

title: 🛠️ Full Stack Engineer Needed – Debug & Fix App Built on No-Code Platform (anything.com)
description: About the Project I have a web application that is approximately 90% complete and currently has active users. The app was built using anything.com, a no-code/low-code platform. I am at a stage where I need a skilled technical engineer — not a no-code hobbyist — to come in, diagnose issues, and manually fix the code directly within the platform's code editor. The AI-assisted editor within the platform has become unreliable at this stage of development (hallucinating fixes, introducing new bugs, and struggling at scale), so all work must be done by manually editing the code — not by prompting the chat/AI interface. What You'll Be Doing - Auditing and fixing broken or misfiring API calls throughout the application - Implementing billing functionality including soft gate and hard gate features (restricting access based on subscription/payment status) - Reinforcing app security — identifying vulnerabilities and ensuring best practices are in place - Reviewing and improving the app's architecture for scalability as the user base grows - Troubleshooting and resolving miscellaneous bugs and UI/logic errors across the platform - All work to be done via manual code editing directly within the anything.com platform editor — not through the AI chat interface. You Are a Great Fit If You: - Are a trained software engineer with full stack experience (not just a no-code user) - Have hands-on experience with anything.com or similar no-code/low-code platforms that expose underlying code - Can read, write, and debug JavaScript, HTML/CSS, and relevant back-end logic - Have experience implementing billing systems and access control (soft/hard gating) - Are comfortable working inside a platform's native code editor without relying on AI-generated suggestions - Have strong debugging and problem-solving skills — you can look at broken code and figure out WHY it's broken - Communicate clearly and can explain issues in plain language You Are NOT a Fit If You: - Only know how to use drag-and-drop or AI chat to build — I need someone who can write and fix code manually - Have never worked inside anything.com or a comparable platform - Cannot troubleshoot without relying on AI-assisted code generation Project Scope This is an ongoing engagement. The immediate priority is stabilizing the app — fixing API issues, implementing billing gates, and resolving current bugs. Once the app is stable, there is additional feature development work available for the right person. The app has active users so reliability and speed matter. To Apply, Please Include: - Your experience with anything.com or similar platforms (be specific) - Examples of bugs or complex issues you've debugged in past projects - Any experience with billing implementation and access gating - Your approach to manual code editing within a constrained platform environment Looking for someone who takes pride in clean, working code and can hit the ground running. This app has real users depending on it — quality and reliability matter.

title: Full-Stack Developer (React) for Hotel/Wellness project Support & Development

We’re looking for someone reliable who can take ownership and contribute to the system’s continuous growth.

title: Full Stack Developer (React + .NET + MongoDB)

This phase focuses on: Expanding KPI tracking (biodiversity, water, soil, social) Building site & project-level dashboards Supporting multi-site programmes Enhancing mobile-based data collection Scope of Work: *Develop REST APIs using .NET (ASP.NET Core) *Build React dashboards & WebGIS map interface *Implement RBAC, data processing & file uploads *Design and optimize MongoDB schemas (incl. geospatial data) *Build reporting APIs & audit logs *Support deployment on cloud infrastructure Tech Stack React | .NET Core | MongoDB | WebGIS ✅ Requirements Strong experience in React + .NET full stack Hands-on with MongoDB & performance optimization Experience with data dashboards / GIS (preferred) Ability to work independently

title: Full-Stack Developer for Customer Support System (React + Mobile App)
description: We’re looking for a senior-level Full-Stack Developer to build and scale a customer support platform with both web and mobile app / mobile-first experience. The system will include a React-based web application and a cross-platform mobile app (preferably React Native or Flutter) targeting iOS and Android smartphones. This is an MVP development project with long-term potential, focused on building a scalable, secure platform that handles customer communication efficiently across channels. Core features include: Ticketing system with statuses, priorities, and internal notes Real-time chat and real-time data / updates between users and support agents Push notifications for new messages and status changes Full authentication / login / user roles system (admin, agent, user) Admin dashboard / admin panel for managing tickets, users, and workflows Clean, modern UX & UI across both web and mobile Strong backend development with well-structured REST APIs / API integration Optional Stripe integration for subscription-based support plans You should have strong experience in full-stack development, including backend architecture, API design, and mobile app integration. Experience with Expo (React Native + EAS) or similar tooling is a plus. We’re looking for someone who understands product thinking, can ensure scalability and security, and communicates clearly throughout the project. title: Full Stack Software/AI Engineer
description: We are seeking a skilled full stack software/AI engineer to maintain, improve and develop a web application for our startup. The ideal candidate will have a strategic mindset, curious, experience in both front-end and back-end development, as well as AI integration and experience with frameworks such as langgraph/crewai agentic workflow building and and AI chatbots. Responsibilities include designing and implementing user interfaces, developing server-side logic, QA, and integrating AI models to enhance user experience. The candidate should be able to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: # Senior Full Stack Developer (AI-Assisted Development) YOU MUST BE WILLING TO WORK (REMOTELY) 9:00 - 5:00 New York Time 5 days a week **Company:** Caffeine **Type:** Full-Time **Experience Level:** 5+ Years --- ## About Us Caffeine is a development agency that builds and maintains a diverse portfolio of web applications — from enterprise .NET platforms and financial services portals to AI-powered tools and client WordPress sites. AI-assisted development isn't a buzzword here — it's how we operate. Our team uses Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and other AI tools daily to architect, build, debug, and ship faster. We're looking for a senior developer who has already integrated AI into their workflow and can help define best practices for the team. ## The Role We're looking for a Senior Full Stack Developer who leverages AI tools to multiply their output and wants to help shape how an entire development team uses AI effectively. You'll architect new systems, modernize legacy codebases, mentor developers on AI-augmented workflows, and ship production features across .NET, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Python projects. This role combines deep engineering experience with a forward-thinking approach to AI-assisted development. ## What You'll Do - Architect and build full-stack applications using **.NET (C#)**, **Vue 3**, **React/Next.js**, and **Node.js** - Use **AI coding tools** (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT) to accelerate development — from scaffolding architecture to writing complex business logic, generating tests, debugging production issues, and refactoring legacy code - Design and maintain **SQL Server** databases with Entity Framework Core and Dapper - Lead modernization efforts: migrating .NET Framework 4.6.1 apps to .NET 9, leveraging AI tools to analyze and transform legacy codebases efficiently - Build and integrate **AI/LLM features** into client applications: chatbots, RAG pipelines, vector search, multi-provider integrations (OpenAI + Anthropic) - **Define AI-assisted development best practices** for the team — prompt patterns, code review standards for AI-generated code, when to trust vs. override AI suggestions - Own payment and e-commerce integrations (AuthorizeNet, Kount fraud detection) - Implement authentication and security: JWT, Okta MFA, ASP.NET Identity - Mentor junior developers on effective AI tool usage — teaching them to be faster without sacrificing code quality or understanding - Manage work across GitHub and internal GitLab repositories ## What We're Looking For ### Required - **5+ years** of professional full stack development experience - Strong proficiency in **C#/.NET** — both .NET Framework and modern .NET (6+) - Production experience with **ASP.NET MVC**, **Web API**, and **Entity Framework** (Core and/or EF6) - Strong experience with **SQL Server** (query writing, schema design, performance) - Proficiency with at least one modern JS frontend framework: **Vue 3** or **React** - Experience with **TypeScript** - **Active, sophisticated use of AI coding tools** — you use Claude Code, Copilot, or similar daily and have opinions on how to use them well - Ability to **evaluate AI-generated code critically** at a senior level — catching subtle bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and architectural mismatches that AI tools produce - Understanding of when AI accelerates work vs. when it creates technical debt - Solid understanding of **REST API design** and decoupled frontend/backend architecture - Experience with **authentication and authorization** (JWT, OAuth, Identity frameworks) - Ability to own projects end-to-end: scoping, architecture, implementation, deployment - Strong Git workflow and code review practices ### Required Technical Depth - **Backend:** C#/.NET 6-9 (Clean Architecture, DDD), ASP.NET MVC/Web API, Entity Framework Core, Dapper, SQL Server, Hangfire - **Frontend:** Vue 3 (Composition API, Vuex, Vite) and/or React 18/19, Next.js, TypeScript

title: Senior Full Stack Developer (SaaS | .NET + React + Stripe/Twilio)
description: We are building a robust, high-performance SaaS platform that requires strong architecture, scalability, and clean execution. We are looking for a Senior Full Stack Developer who can take ownership of both backend and frontend development while contributing to system design and long-term scalability. Scope of Work: You will be responsible for developing a scalable system that handles concurrent users, real-time interactions, and third-party integrations. This includes backend architecture, API development, and frontend implementation. Key Responsibilities: Design and develop backend services using .NET (C#) with a focus on performance, scalability, and maintainability Build and optimize APIs for frontend and third-party integrations Implement and manage integrations with Stripe (payments, subscriptions, webhooks), Twilio (SMS, messaging workflows, OTPs), and Shopify. Develop modern, responsive frontend applications using React + Next.js Handle complex application logic including concurrency, multithreading, and asynchronous processing Optimize system performance under high load and ensure reliability Collaborate with designers and stakeholders to translate requirements into technical solutions Write clean, well-documented, and testable code Participate in code reviews and enforce development best practices Technical Requirements: 5+ years of experience in full stack development Strong expertise in .NET / C# (ASP.NET Core preferred) Solid experience with React and Next.js Hands-on experience integrating Stripe (payments, subscriptions, billing flows) Hands-on experience integrating Twilio (SMS APIs, messaging workflows) Strong understanding of multithreading, concurrency, and asynchronous programming Experience with RESTful APIs and microservices architecture Experience with Entity Framework or similar ORM tools Proficiency in TypeScript and JavaScript Strong understanding of database design (SQL-based systems preferred) Architecture & System Expectations: Experience building scalable SaaS systems Ability to design systems that handle high concurrency and real-time interactions Understanding of API rate limits, queue systems, and background job processing Familiarity with caching strategies and performance optimization Must to Have: Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or similar) Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps workflows Experience building SDKs or developer tools Understanding of security best practices in payments and authentication What We’re Looking For: Someone who can own features end-to-end, not just execute tasks Strong problem-solving mindset and attention to detail Ability to work independently and make technical decisions Clear communication and documentation skills

title: Part-Time Full-Stack Engineer — Angular/Firebase SaaS (with growth path)

The platform includes on-demand video, live sessions, and a timestamped annotation system. The core platform is built and in active use — we're looking for someone to own ongoing feature development, improvements, and maintenance. Familiarity with AI-assisted development (Claude Code or similar) is preferred. To apply, please share relevant work samples or clients.

title: Claude Code Power User for AI-Native Development. Full Stack
description: We need a full-stack developer who already builds production apps in Claude Code every day. Not someone who's "exploring AI tools" or ran through a tutorial once. Someone who's actually shipping real software through agentic workflows and can prove it. Also, we are looking to make this close to full time if it works out so please refrain from applying if you wont be able to get to 30 hours a week or more sooner than later. You'll need to show us real Claude Code experience. We're going to ask you to walk through projects you've built with it, talk about your workflow, how you manage context, and what you do when it breaks. If that conversation would be uncomfortable, this probably isn't the right fit. The work: You'll be building and shipping web applications from scratch through production. React/Next.js frontends, Node.js or Python backends, database design, API integrations, third-party services. You own the whole thing: architecture, code, testing, deployment, maintenance. What you need to know: Claude Code as a daily driver. Not a side experiment. You should understand prompt engineering in a dev context, context window management, CLAUDE.md setup, and how to actually stay productive in agentic coding sessions. React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS on the frontend. You can build clean, responsive UIs that don't look like a hackathon project. Node.js and/or Python on the backend. REST APIs, auth flows, business logic, background jobs. PostgreSQL, Supabase, or something equivalent for data. Schema design, migrations, query optimization, row-level security. GitHub Actions or a similar CI/CD setup. Automated testing, environment management across dev/staging/production, deployment pipelines that actually work. Hosting on Vercel, Railway, AWS, or similar. You know how to get an app live, keep it live, and handle it when traffic shows up. Git and GitHub, for real. Branching strategies, pull requests, code review, release management. This isn't optional. Things that would make you stand out: Supabase experience (auth, edge functions, realtime). MCP server integration and tool-use patterns. Anthropic API experience beyond just Claude Code. Any work on multi-agent or AI-orchestrated systems. Vercel + Next.js deployment pipelines. What matters more than your resume: Show us what you've built with Claude Code. A repo, a screen recording, a Loom walkthrough, whatever. Tell us about a time Claude Code broke something and how you figured it out. Walk us through how you actually structure a coding session, manage context, and stay productive across a long build. This is a contract role with potential for ongoing work. Hours are flexible but we expect real output. We move fast and ship constantly.

title: Full-Stack Developer — AI-Powered Content Automation SaaS (Node.js, Next.js, Claude API, BullMQ)
description: IMPORTANT: Do not contact me outside of Upwork. I work exclusively through the Upwork platform. Any contact via LinkedIn, email, or any other channel will be disqualified. OVERVIEW We're building a content automation platform that generates bespoke social media content in each client's unique voice, runs it through a campaign strategy engine, and publishes it automatically on a schedule. This is not a simple CRUD app. It is a scheduling system, a messaging system, and a publishing system that happens to produce content. We need a developer who treats reliability and observability as first-class requirements, not afterthoughts. We have a complete product specification and developer build proposal covering data models, API contracts, queue architecture, user stories with acceptance criteria, and full edge case handling. You are not being asked to design the system. You are being asked to build it precisely and well. WHAT YOU'LL BUILD (PHASE 1) Voice profile engine. Structured client voice built from website scraping, social post scanning, and onboarding survey. Stored as a versioned text file. Monitored for drift over time. Campaign and content generation pipeline. Monthly campaign objects, 4-week arc enforcement, posting strategy ratios (Jab Jab Punch / 4-1-1), memory rules, hook type rotation, semantic similarity deduplication via pgvector. AI image generation. Style preset system, content moderation, 5-option image slideshow, pluggable provider interface. Approval system. Email magic link approve/decline with no login required, inline decline reason tags, 72-hour auto-post with guard checks, first-post explicit client confirmation. Publishing layer. Abstracted publish() interface over a relay API, idempotent publish worker with external_id checking, blame-assigned failure logging, exponential backoff retry. Queue system. BullMQ + Redis, 4 job types (generate, schedule, publish, notify), single worker per type at launch, designed to scale by config not code change. Pipeline inspector. Internal admin screen showing full per-post stage timeline including voice version, campaign object, prompt inputs, generated output, approval state, and publish result. Weekly digest email. Performance trend, top post, campaign progress, UTM-tracked click data. Stripe billing. $50 and $199 plans, 14-day free trial, self-serve signup. Phase 2 (blog, content clusters, WordPress publishing, email newsletter) and Phase 3 (Agency reviewer dashboard) will follow for the right developer. TECH STACK Frontend: Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS Backend: Node.js, tRPC or REST Database: PostgreSQL with pgvector (Supabase or Railway) Queue: BullMQ + Redis LLM: Anthropic Claude API — primary generation and classification model Email: Postmark Auth: Clerk or NextAuth Billing: Stripe Storage: S3-compatible (Supabase Storage or Cloudflare R2) Image generation: TBC — provider is abstracted and confirmed at project start Publishing relay: TBC — must hold LinkedIn Marketing Developer Platform approval Monitoring: Sentry YOU ARE THE RIGHT DEVELOPER IF You have built queue-based systems before and understand what idempotency means in a publish context — not just the concept but the implementation. You are comfortable working directly with the Anthropic Claude API including structured outputs, multi-turn prompts, confidence scoring, and embedding-based similarity checks. You use Cursor or a similar AI-assisted development environment as your primary workflow. This project is designed to be built with AI tooling and we expect it. You have handled LinkedIn OAuth and/or X OAuth including token refresh, permission scopes, and the edge cases that come with them. You write observable code. Every stage of a pipeline stores its output, failures are logged with context, and a non-technical person can understand what went wrong without reading logs. You can read a detailed spec and implement it faithfully without adding features that weren't asked for. You have shipped a SaaS product end-to-end, not just contributed to one. YOU ARE NOT THE RIGHT DEVELOPER IF You need the architecture designed for you. You treat logging and observability as things you will add later. You have not used the Claude API or a comparable LLM API in a production context. You are not comfortable with queue systems and async job architecture. You underestimate LinkedIn's OAuth quirks. ENGAGEMENT Phase 1 is fixed price or milestone-based, scoped to the build proposal. A detailed developer build proposal covering data models, API contracts, 14 user stories with acceptance criteria, and guardrails will be shared after initial screening. Expected Phase 1 timeline is 5 to 6 weeks at full-time pace using AI-assisted development tooling. Phases 2 and 3 are available for the right developer after Phase 1 delivery. TO APPLY Answer the following questions in your proposal. Applications without these answers will not be reviewed. Describe a queue-based system you have built. What job types did you use and how did you handle idempotency? Have you built with the Anthropic Claude API or OpenAI API in production? Describe what you built and what the hardest part was. Have you integrated LinkedIn OAuth in a production app? What edge cases did you run into? What AI-assisted development tools do you use day-to-day and how do they fit into your workflow? What is your honest Phase 1 estimate given the scope described above? BUDGET We are looking for a senior developer or small team. We are not looking for the cheapest option — we are looking for the right one. Include your rate and Phase 1 estimate in your proposal. Lowball estimates are a red flag, not a positive signal.

title: Full Stack TypeScript Developer (React + Node) – API Integration Module (Fixed Price)

Scope (Fixed Deliverable) Implement a PrintProvider integration with the following: 1. Quote Endpoint Input: product type, quantity, specs (size/finish), artwork URL Output: pricing breakdown (cost, sell price), lead time 2. Order Endpoint Create order with provider Return order ID + confirmation details 3. Status Endpoint Retrieve order status (pending, in production, shipped, etc.) 4. Error Handling Invalid inputs Provider failures Clear structured responses 5. Documentation Short README: endpoints request/response examples setup notes. Technical Constraints TypeScript only (strict typing expected) Node.js + Express (no framework changes) Follow modular/service-based structure No direct UI work or hacks No changes to unrelated parts of the codebase Clean, readable, production-style code Provided Repo access (restricted) Interface definition (PrintProvider) Example patterns from existing services Time Expectation 1–2 days focused work Delivery within 2–4 days max Evaluation Criteria Code structure and clarity Adherence to interface API integration quality Error handling completeness Communication and delivery reliability Requirements Proven experience with: React + TypeScript Node.js + Express External API integrations (REST/webhooks) Must provide: GitHub examples of similar work brief explanation of one API integration you’ve built Notes Multiple candidates will be hired for this task Only top performer(s) will continue to ongoing work No hourly contracts for this phase To Apply Provide: GitHub profile Relevant API integration example Brief outline (3–5 lines) of how you would structure this module

title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer for AI Platform
description: I am looking for a Senior Full-Stack Developer to build the MVP for VibeEngine AI, a next-generation "Vibe Coding" platform. The goal is to create a seamless, hands-off experience where users describe a website or web app in natural language, and the platform automatically generates, renders, and deploys it. I am not looking for a simple wrapper. I need a robust architecture that handles real-time code streaming and automated deployments. Technical Requirements & Stack: • Frontend: Next.js 14/15 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + Framer Motion. • AI Orchestration: Vercel AI SDK to manage streaming from Claude 3.5 Sonnet / Gemini 1.5 Pro. • Real-time Preview: Integration of Sandpack or WebContainers for an instant side-by-side "live-coding" feel. • Automated Deployment: Deep integration with Vercel API or Netlify API for "One-Click Publish" functionality (programmatic site creation). • Backend & Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, and Edge Functions). • Payments & Credits: Stripe integration with Webhooks to manage a credit-based system and automated subscription tiers. Key Features to Implement: 1. Split-Pane Interface: AI Chat on the left, Live Interactive Canvas on the right. 2. Streaming Code Generation: UI must update in real-time as the LLM streams the React/HTML code. 3. Context-Aware Iteration: Users must be able to ask for follow-up changes (e.g., "make it more dark-mode friendly") and the AI should update the existing codebase. 4. Automatic "Hands-Off" Deploy: Upon payment, the system must trigger a production-ready deployment to a unique subdomain. Project Milestones & Payment Schedule: • Milestone 1: Environment Setup & AI Core ($20%) Authentication (Supabase) and connection to LLM API via Vercel AI SDK. Basic chat interface returning structured code. • Milestone 2: The Live Sandbox ($30%) Implementation of the real-time previewer (Sandpack). Code generated by the AI must render instantly in the browser without page refreshes. • Milestone 3: Automated Deployment Engine ($30%) Integration with Vercel/Netlify API to programmatically deploy sites. Stripe Webhooks setup for credit-based triggers. • Milestone 4: Final Polishing & UI/UX ($20%) Mobile responsiveness, error handling for AI outputs, and final hand-off. Selection Criteria (The "Filter" Questions): 1. Have you ever worked with Vercel AI SDK or built a Live Code Editor before? 2. How would you handle LLM "hallucinations" when the AI generates broken code that crashes the preview? 3. Are you familiar with programmatic deployments via Vercel/Netlify API? Please describe a past project where you used them. My budget for the MVP is around $4,000, but I'm flexible for the right talent. I'm looking for a clean, scalable architecture because this is the foundation of a business. I prefer paying by milestones to ensure we are aligned at every stage of the development. Thanks and Good Luck 👍

title: Full Stack Developer Needed — Build CreatorOS (Revenue-Ready Web App)
description: I’m building CreatorOS — a money dashboard for content creators that tracks brand deals, expenses, and taxes in one place. I already have a fully designed HTML prototype with all screens, flows, and UI complete. I need a developer to turn it into a real working web app with a backend — fast. This is NOT a design project. The design is done. I need someone who can build and ship. ⸻ WHAT YOU’LL BUILD • Convert my existing HTML prototype into a fully functional web app • User authentication — sign up, log in, password reset • Dashboard with real data — income tracking, expense tracker, tax estimator • Brand deal tracker — add deals, set status (paid, pending, overdue) • Stripe payment integration — subscription and one-time payment • User database — store and retrieve each user’s data • Mobile responsive — must work perfectly on phone browsers • Fast, clean, and simple — no bloat ⸻ TECH STACK (flexible — suggest what you’re strongest in) Frontend: React or Next.js Backend: Node.js or Python (FastAPI / Django) Database: PostgreSQL or Firebase Auth: Supabase or Firebase Auth Payments: Stripe Hosting: Vercel or Railway ⸻ WHAT I’M LOOKING FOR ✅ Strong portfolio of web apps you’ve shipped — not just designed ✅ Experience with Stripe integration and user auth ✅ Clean, readable code — another developer should be able to pick this up ✅ Communicates clearly and hits deadlines ✅ Available to start immediately ⸻ TIMELINE I want a working MVP live within 3–4 weeks. This is a real business with paying customers already. Speed matters. ⸻ BUDGET Fixed price. I’m looking for quality over cheapest option. Please include in your proposal: • Your fixed price for the full scope above • Your estimated timeline • 2–3 examples of similar apps you’ve built and shipped • One sentence on why you’re the right person for this Proposals without examples will not be considered. ⸻ ABOUT THIS PROJECT This is phase 1 of a larger build. If you do great work here, there is ongoing work — iOS and Android app, new features, and additional products in the same portfolio. I work fast, I pay on time, and I give good feedback. I’m looking for a developer I can build a long term relationship with. Looking forward to seeing your proposal.

title: Senior Developer (Full Stack) MUST BE IN AUSTRALIA NEWZEALAND OR SE ASIA
description: Senior Developer (Full Stack) WordPress Experience mandatory Good to Great Schools Australia Limited About the Organisation Good to Great Schools Australia (GGSA) partners with schools and school systems to support school improvement along a journey from Poor to Fair, Fair to Good and Good to Great. GGSA is modern and innovative, and we are well set up to support roles from being carried out at home. About the Role We are looking for a Senior Developer who can create engaging and interactive online learning experiences using PHP, WordPress and Learn Dash. You will be responsible for developing and maintaining our website, customising themes and plugins, and integrating various e-learning features and functionalities. You will lead the team and review code before development goes through production. As part of a collaborative and forward-thinking team, you will design and deliver cutting-edge web applications that underpin our school and teacher support programmes. This role demands a developer with expertise across front-end and back-end technologies, particularly within cloud-based environments, who thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic setting. Key Responsibilities Strong knowledge of PHP, especially in WordPress development and debugging (PHP 8.1+). Experience with headless CMS architecture, specifically with NextJS. Experience with WordPress themes and plugins, including Learndash, WooCommerce, Divi, etc. Familiarity with front-end technologies, such as HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, Bootstrap, and experience with TypeScript, React/NextJs, SCSS. Familiarity with web development tooling, including phpcs, phpmd, phpstan, phpunit, codecept, Docker BuildKit, TS config, eslint, prettier, postcss, tailwind, and advanced webpack configuration. Experience with containerisation using Docker/compose, including managing dependencies with composer and npm/yarn/pnpm. Experience with GraphQL APIs, particularly using Hasura. Knowledge of database management systems, particularly MySQL/MariaDB (10+) Understanding of Git workflow and familiarity with CLI/terminal commands. Ability to create responsive and adaptive web designs that work across different devices and browsers Understanding of web development best practices, such as SEO, accessibility, performance, security, etc. Good communication and collaboration skills, as well as the ability to work independently and manage multiple projects. Experience leading a development team, providing guidance and ensuring code quality. A portfolio of previous web development projects that demonstrate your skills and creativity. Contribute to team knowledge sharing, mentor junior developers, and participate in code reviews. Work independently while maintaining a strong, continuous flow of communication within a remote-first team. Desirable Skills Experience with other e-learning platforms or tools, such as Moodle, SCORM, LMS, etc. Knowledge of web design principles and tools, such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, etc. Familiarity with web development frameworks or libraries, such as Laravel, React, Vue, etc. Background in education technology or experience in an Agile development environment. Knowledge of DevOps practices and experience with CI/CD pipelines. Benefits Fully remote position, offering flexibility and a balanced work-life structure. Opportunity to lead a greenfield project and work with modern technologies. A collaborative and supportive team, contributing to meaningful work in the education sector. Preferred Qualifications Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field, or equivalent experience. How to Apply Applicants need to submit a resume and an application letter that addresses the essential selection criteria above. GGSA does NOT consider applications who do not address the selection criteria. If you are looking for the next stage of your career and have a desire to contribute to a not-for-profit organisation that supports Australian schools, we'd love to hear from you! **All applications will be reviewed for authenticity, and responses may be checked using AI-detection tools as part of our screening process.**

title: Senior Full Stack Shopify Engineer for Growing E-commerce Stores
description: We’re looking for a very strong full stack engineer to help support and improve a portfolio of Shopify e-commerce stores. There is a high volume of work across both frontend and backend, so we need someone who is technically solid, detail-oriented, fast, reliable, and easy to work with. This is not a basic theme-tweak role. We want someone who can take ownership, execute cleanly, spot issues early, and help keep multiple stores running smoothly. You should be comfortable working across Shopify storefront customization, frontend UI and UX improvements, theme edits, backend workflows, integrations, debugging, product data flows, automation, and general technical problem-solving. Strong Shopify experience is important, and it is a big plus if you also have experience with Liquid, Shopify APIs, metafields, webhooks, React, TypeScript, and Node.js. We care a lot about attention to detail, clean implementation, good product judgment, and smooth communication. You should be able to work independently, move fast without being sloppy, and handle multiple live projects without creating mess. When applying, please send a short intro, a few strong Shopify or e-commerce examples, what parts of the stack you are strongest in, one example of a messy technical problem you solved well, your weekly availability, your hourly rate, and put the word DETAIL at the top so I know you actually read this.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for AI-Powered Ad Management SaaS (Technical Lead)
description: We're building an AI-powered Facebook ads management platform that automates campaign optimization, creative management, and performance reporting for clients in high-ticket verticals. Starting with wholesale real estate, expanding from there. This is not a concept. We already have: - A working AI agent (Python) managing a live ad account with Meta Graph API, HYROS attribution, and Calendly integrations - 2 years of ad performance data and pattern intelligence - Full product vision with clear architecture decisions made - A comprehensive knowledge base of ad strategies, SaaS frameworks, and competitive research already organized for AI-assisted development What we need is a senior developer who can take this from internal tool to scalable SaaS product. THE ROLE: You'll be the technical lead helping architect and build the platform. This starts as a consulting/build engagement and could grow into something bigger as we scale. What you'll work on: - Design the system architecture (backend, frontend, data pipeline) - Build the core platform: user dashboard, campaign management, automated optimization engine - Integrate with Meta Marketing API, payment processing, and reporting - Help make smart technical decisions: what framework, what database, what scales and what doesn't What you bring: - 5+ years building web applications, ideally SaaS products - Strong backend (Python preferred, open to Node/Go) - Experience with React or Next.js on the frontend - You've worked with third-party APIs extensively (ad platforms, payment processors, etc.) - Bonus: experience with AI/ML integration, Claude/OpenAI APIs, or ad tech - Bonus: you've built or helped build a SaaS from early stage to paying customers This is a founder-led project. You'll work directly with me. I move fast, communicate directly, and value people who think about the product, not just the code. If you're the type of developer who asks "why are we building this?" before writing a line, we'll get along. To apply, tell me: 1. A SaaS or platform you helped build from early stage. What was your role and what did you ship? 2. Your experience with Meta/Facebook APIs or any ad platform APIs 3. Your preferred tech stack for building a SaaS from scratch and why 4. Your availability (hours/week) and hourly rate No generic proposals. If your first sentence is "Dear Hiring Manager" I'm not reading it.

title: Full Stack Developer — NestJS/TypeScript + Frontend (Online Ordering Platform) | 6+ Month Contract
description: ****DO NOT MESSAGE ME ON LINKEDIN, SOCIAL MEDIA OR EMAIL AS YOUR APPLICATION WILL BE DECLINED INSTANTLY AND YOU WILL BE REPORTED**** We're an Australian fintech company that builds POS and payment solutions for hospitality merchants. This starts as a 6+ month contract but we're specifically looking for someone who wants to grow with us — with a clear path to a permanent role as we scale our external development team. If you're looking for a one-off gig, this isn't the right fit. What you'll be working on: - Backend services using Node.js, NestJS, and TypeScript - Frontend interfaces for online ordering, checkout, and order management using React or Vue.js - REST APIs for customer-facing and internal platform functionality - PostgreSQL database work including schema design and query optimisation - Integration with third-party services and our existing mobile app APIs - Deployment and infrastructure using Docker and Terraform We have existing systems in production — this is not a greenfield project. You'll be extending and improving what's already built while delivering new features on tight deadlines. Tech stack: Node.js, NestJS, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Docker, Terraform, REST APIs, Git, CI/CD Requirements: - 2+ years experience with Node.js and TypeScript - Strong NestJS or similar backend framework experience - Frontend development with React or Vue.js (responsive, user-friendly interfaces) - PostgreSQL or similar relational database experience - Comfortable with Docker and CI/CD workflows - Available 35-40 hrs/week with some overlap with Australian business hours (AEST) - Strong communicator who works well in a small team - Can start immediately Strong preferences: - UI/UX sensibility — ability to create clean, intuitive customer-facing experiences - Experience with online ordering, e-commerce, or checkout flows - Terraform or infrastructure-as-code experience - Fintech or payments industry background ****DO NOT MESSAGE ME ON LINKEDIN, SOCIAL MEDIA OR EMAIL AS YOUR APPLICATION WILL BE DECLINED INSTANTLY AND YOU WILL BE REPORTED**** To apply, please share: 1- A relevant full stack project you've built (ideally involving ordering, e-commerce, or payment flows) 2- What specific parts of the project did you personally build (frontend, backend, or both)? 3- Your availability and earliest start date 4- Why you're interested in a long-term role, not just a short contract 5- Your hourly rate

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (TypeScript/Next.js) — Clinical Research Study Portal
description: About ViSignal ViSignal is building a non-interventional clinical research platform. Our consumer app captures guided facial scans, videos, and questionnaires—all stored in AWS S3. We need a Study Portal built: an internal research tool where physician reviewers label clinical issues, adjudicate disagreements, lock datasets, and evaluate AI predictions against physician ground truth. The Project A full-stack web portal with 4 role-based dashboards (Reviewer Doctor, Tie-Breaker Doctor, Principal Investigator, Admin), a MongoDB-to-PostgreSQL ingestion pipeline, presigned S3 media access, blinded dual-review workflows, tie-break adjudication, dataset locking, AI evaluation metrics, PDF report generation, and immutable audit logging. We have a complete 80+ page specification document. Every screen, button, workflow, API rule, data model, field mapping, and business logic decision is defined. A feasibility assessment confirmed all 140 requirement areas are covered. This is an execution project, not a design project. Tech Stack (Locked — Non-Negotiable) TypeScript | Next.js 14+ (App Router) | Prisma | PostgreSQL (AWS RDS) | NextAuth.js | AWS S3 presigned URLs | Tailwind + shadcn/ui | AWS ECS Fargate | mongoose (read-only MongoDB ingestion) Contract Details Fixed-price: $3,000 USD. Timeline: 25 working days, hard deadline. Milestones: 30% (Day 7) / 40% (Day 16) / 30% (Day 25). Daily 15-minute standups. Full spec provided after NDA. Start immediately. Milestone 1 (Day 7, 30% — $900) Deliverables: Auth + RBAC, DB schema + Prisma migrations, MongoDB ingestion pipeline, presigned S3 URLs, basic Reviewer UI. Acceptance: — All Milestone 1 test cases pass. — Audit logging active for all auth events (login success, login failure, logout) and all ingestion events (case created, case assigned, media access). — Design document delivered covering: database schema (ERD or Prisma schema diagram), API route map, auth flow diagram, ingestion pipeline architecture, and environment setup guide. Milestone 2 (Day 16, 40% — $1,200) Deliverables: Complete Reviewer + Tie-Breaker dashboards, adjudication logic, Admin panel (users, config, audit logs). Acceptance: — All Milestone 2 test cases pass. — Audit logging active for all reviewer actions (case open, draft save, final submission), all tie-break actions (case open, adjudication submitted), and all admin operations (add/edit/activate/deactivate user, password update, reviewer role replacement). — Design document updated with: reviewer workflow state machine, adjudication logic flowchart, admin permission matrix, and complete API endpoint documentation for all implemented routes. Milestone 3 (Day 25, 30% — $900) Deliverables: PI dashboard, AI evaluation + metrics, PDF reports, CSV exports, AWS deployment, end-to-end testing, bug fixes. Acceptance: — All Milestone 3 test cases pass. — Audit logging active for all PI actions (dataset lock, study report generation, study report download, ground truth export, AI evaluation run, evaluation dataset download, AI performance report generation, AI performance report download). — Design document finalized with: complete API documentation, deployment architecture diagram, AI evaluation data flow, report generation pipeline, full environment configuration guide, and a handoff/maintenance document so a new developer can take over the codebase without assistance. Acceptance Criteria (All Milestones) Each milestone must meet ALL of the following before payment is released: Test Cases Pass. All test cases provided for that milestone must pass. This includes end-to-end workflow tests, role-based access tests, audit trail completeness tests, and submission immutability tests. No exceptions. Audit Logging Complete. Every user action defined in the spec must be logged by the time it is implemented. Logs must be append-only, immutable, timestamped, and include user ID, role, action, case ID (where applicable), and details. No milestone is approved if any implemented feature is missing audit log coverage. Design Documentation Delivered. Each milestone must include an updated design document maintained in the project repo. This is not optional — it is a deliverable equal in importance to the code itself. Milestone 1 establishes the foundation (schema, auth, ingestion architecture). Milestone 2 adds workflow and adjudication documentation. Milestone 3 completes the full system documentation including deployment and handoff guides. Code Quality. Clean, typed TypeScript. No "any" types. Meaningful variable names. Consistent file structure. Comments on complex business logic (adjudication, dataset versioning, audit immutability). AI Coding Tools — Mandatory We expect heavy use of AI coding tools throughout development. This is not optional. You must actively use tools like Cursor, Claude Code, or GitHub Copilot to accelerate development. The 25-day timeline assumes AI-assisted development throughout the entire sprint. If you do not use AI coding tools daily, you will not hit this deadline. In your proposal, describe exactly which AI tools you use, how you use them, and what percentage of your coding workflow they cover. Along with the 80+ page specification, we will provide a complete Prisma database schema and a full API endpoint table. A feasibility assessment using Claude AI reviewed the full specification and concluded that with this technical package—including the spec's detailed field mappings, schemas, workflow definitions, and business logic rules—AI coding tools should generate approximately 80% of the codebase directly from the provided documentation. The remaining 20% — security hardening, deployment configuration, edge case handling, and end-to-end testing — is where your engineering judgment matters. (See Attached Image) Must-Have Skills 5+ years full-stack development | Strong TypeScript (not just JavaScript with types) | Next.js (App Router, API routes, server components) | Prisma + PostgreSQL | AWS (S3, RDS, ECS/EC2) | Presigned S3 URLs via AWS SDK | MongoDB read integration | Backend-enforced RBAC | PDF generation in Node.js | Daily active use of AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, Claude Code) | Full-time availability (8–10 hrs/day) for 25 consecutive working days | Immediate start. Nice-to-Have Clinical trial, healthcare, or research portal experience | Audit trail / immutable logging patterns | NextAuth.js experience | shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS | @react-pdf/renderer or Puppeteer | Understanding of blinded review or adjudication workflows. To Apply — Your Proposal Must Include: Confirmation that you accept the $3,000 fixed price. Confirmation of full-time availability for 25 consecutive working days starting immediately. One example of a Next.js + TypeScript portal/dashboard you built (link or screenshots). One example of AWS integration (S3, RDS, or presigned URLs). Describe what you built. Which AI coding tools you use and how they fit into your daily workflow. Be specific — "I use Copilot" is not enough. Describe how. Your timezone and preferred daily standup time. Generic proposals, auto-generated cover letters, and applications that don't answer the screening questions will not be reviewed.​

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer — React Native + Laravel | Mobile App Performance & AWS Architecture

title: AI/Full-Stack Engineer Build an AI Agent Platform & Chatbot (Urgent)
duration: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: We're building a consumer-facing AI agent platform — think Manus.im or Genspark, but white-labeled with custom context, trained data, and domain-specific workflows. We already have an MVP dashboard built on AnotherWrapper.com and need an engineer who can take ownership of the AI layer end-to-end. Own the AI system long-term — agent logic, prompt engineering, model updates, infra scaling. Collaborate directly with the founder on product direction and technical architecture. Stay current with the rapidly evolving AI agent ecosystem and bring improvements to the table. Ideal scenario: You've already built something agentic that works — show us. A demo, a repo, a product. That matters more than a resume. Remote · Long-term collaboration · Competitive compensation based on experience

title: Sr Full-Stack Developer – Gamified SaaS, Referral System, High-Conversion UI, SMS/Email Automation
description: Senior Front-End Content Funnel Developer (SaaS / High Conversion / Landing Pages + Social Ads)(subscription offering presentations. Overview We are building a national sweepstakes SaaS platform that allows creators to launch compliant $5,000 sweepstakes and monetize their audience. We are looking for a conversion-focused Front-End Funnel Developer who can study and replicate the highest-performing subscription models and landing structures (Amazon, Shopify, Google Fi) and adapt those principles into our platform. Skill stack REQUIRED (non-negotiable) Your hire must understand: 1. Conversion UI (CRITICAL) Knows how to design flows that drive action Not just “make it pretty” Think: progress bars checklists CTA placement friction reduction 2. Gamification systems Like: entries rewards unlocks referrals Similar thinking to: Duolingo DraftKings 3. Built-in engagement loops They keep users active with: nudges reminders milestones You need this for: entrants creators merchants 3. Clean dashboard clarity Everything revolves around: what do I do next Your current risk: Users enter your platform and go: Now what How this applies DIRECTLY to 50fifty.win You’re not building: a giveaway tool You’re building: a behavior engine Your version Earnings Journey User sees: Step 1: Enter giveaway +1 entry Step 2: Verify phone +5 entries Step 3: Invite 3 friends +10 entries Step 4: Buy merch +20 entries Step 5: Share your link unlimited entries This = gamified progression system You do this for money + rewards status leaderboard What a STRONG candidate will show Look for past work like: CRM systems SaaS dashboards Referral platforms Subscription platforms Marketplaces The killer question Send this to EVERY applicant: If we want users to invite 3 friends to unlock more entries, how would you design both the backend logic and frontend experience Weak answer: We can add a referral feature Strong answer: Each user gets a unique referral ID, we track successful conversions, trigger rewards at thresholds, and reflect progress in a dashboard with real-time updates That’s your hire Scoring system Rate each dev 1–5: UX thinking Do they guide users Systems Logic over visuals Dashboards Clear + actionable Events Trigger-based thinking Communication Clear + structured Anyone below 4 skip Reality check Most Upwork devs: Can build features Cannot build behavior systems You need: someone who builds user flows that drive money This is NOT a graphic design-only role. You are managing back end programmer to implement front end presentation and create video and still ads targeting creators and individuals to incentivize viewer to comment, subscribe, share, or purchase to receive entries to win sweepstakes prizes or promote their own sweepstakes and make money selling entries on YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Rumble, etc. This is a revenue-driving role focused on conversion, funnels, and growth execution. Core Objective Your task is to: Analyze and replicate high-converting SaaS/subscription structures Amazon, Shopify, Google Fi style Build high-converting landing pages and pricing funnels Create social media ad landing variations Design onboarding flows Help produce tutorial and walkthrough experiences Responsibilities 1. Conversion and Optimization Implement A/B testing structure Improve bounce rate and engagement Structure upgrade pages Optimize for TikTok, Meta, YouTube traffic 3. Social Media Ad Support Create landing page variants Design micro landing pages Structure ad hooks Assist ad creative layouts 4. Tutorial and Support Experience Build onboarding tutorial flows Create support pages Design dashboard walkthroughs Structure FAQ Required Skills Strong HTML5 CSS3 JavaScript React, Next.js or modern framework Experience with SaaS subscription funnels Stripe integration Conversion psychology understanding Mobile-first design Landing page optimization Preferred Experience Studied high-performing subscription flows Built onboarding funnels like Shopify or Amazon SaaS growth or creator platforms Ad landing pages for TikTok or Meta What We Care About Conversion rate Clean pricing presentation Funnel clarity Fast loading Clear messaging We do NOT care about: Overdesigned visuals Fancy animations Agency fluff Project Scope Phase 1 Homepage Creator signup funnel Subscription pricing page Upgrade page Ad landing pages FAQ page Thank-you page Basic dashboard UI Onboarding flow To Apply Please include: 1. 3 SaaS landing pages you built 2. Example subscription funnel 3. Frameworks you use 4. Availability 5. Answer: How would you structure a SaaS landing page to maximize paid traffic conversion Important: Begin your proposal with the word Conversion Budget Performance-based bonus possible. Long term available if you have ad management and social skills Performance-based bonus possible. Long term available if you had ad management and social skills

title: Full Stack Developer Team Needed for Marketplace Platform
description: We are seeking an experienced full-stack development team (frontend / backend) to build a production-ready, AI-powered marketplace platform. title: Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed to Build Referral & Promoter System
description: Looking for senior automation architect / full-stack developer to build a scalable referral / promoter engine (Airtable + Eventbrite + Make + Custom Backend + Klaviyo) We are building a scalable referral / promoter engine for large-scale event operations across 100+ cities, with multiple events per city for each holiday campaign. We already have a detailed system design and Airtable-based data model scoped out. This is not a greenfield project. We need an experienced systems builder to validate the architecture, tighten weak points, and implement it properly. This is not a simple automation project. The hardest part is building reliable attribution, order matching, reward logic, and fraud prevention in a way that holds up across many cities, many events, refunds, chargebacks, and changing campaign structures. System overview The system includes: Promoter signup and onboarding One unique referral code per promoter per holiday (example: NYE0001) Auto-generation of referral links across multiple events per city Eventbrite integration for tracking ticket purchases Order matching logic using Event ID + referral code Reward system including VIP unlocks, promoter unlocks, and commission logic Klaviyo integration for automated messaging Promoter dashboard in Softr or a lightweight custom frontend What we need Validate and improve the current architecture Build the backend logic using Airtable, APIs, Make, and a custom backend where needed Ensure accurate referral tracking and attribution Implement fraud prevention including self-referrals, suspicious behavior, and invalid conversions Build scalable automation for: code assignment event link generation order ingestion and matching reward triggers reconciliation of refunds / status changes Optionally build or improve the promoter dashboard Preferred stack Airtable as the core database / admin layer Make.com for automation and orchestration Custom backend for critical business logic, matching, validation, and reliability Eventbrite API / webhooks Klaviyo API for email automation Optional: Softr or custom frontend dashboard Important constraints 100+ cities per campaign Multiple events per city including Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and shadow events One referral code per promoter per holiday That code must work across all events in that holiday The system must only count valid tickets: paid not refunded not charged back Commission must not trigger on refunded, invalid, duplicate, or suspicious orders The system must be scalable and not require heavy manual work every time a new city, event, or campaign is added What success looks like We are looking for someone who can build this as an attribution and workflow infrastructure system, not just connect tools together. That means: clean and deterministic matching logic reliable handling of refunds, reversals, and delayed status changes auditability and reconciliation protection against duplicate processing or misattribution a structure that can scale across many campaigns without becoming fragile Deliverables Phase 1: Architecture review and improvements Identify weaknesses in tracking, attribution, and fraud prevention Define the final system structure before scaling Phase 2: MVP build including core referral tracking, order ingestion, matching logic, and reward logic Phase 3: Automation and scaling for multi-city, multi-event support Phase 4 (optional): Dashboard and analytics improvements To apply Please answer the following clearly and specifically: Have you built a referral, affiliate, attribution, or rewards system before? If so, explain the setup. How would you track attribution across multiple links, events, and cities using one code per promoter per holiday? How would you prevent self-referrals, duplicate credits, or fake conversions? How would you structure this system if Eventbrite is the checkout platform? If Eventbrite does not reliably expose the referral or tracking value in the order / webhook payload, how would you design fallback attribution? What would you build in the first 2 weeks? Which parts would you keep in Airtable / Make, and which parts would you move into a custom backend? Only apply if you have real experience building similar systems or other attribution-heavy / commission / transaction logic systems at scale. Bonus if you have experience with: Eventbrite Airtable at scale Make.com Klaviyo referral / affiliate / ambassador systems webhook ingestion, reconciliation jobs, and idempotent processing We are open to milestone-based delivery. For the right person, we care more about getting the architecture and matching logic right than rushing into a fragile build.

title: Full-Stack Technical Lead — Laravel / React Native · NYC Neighborhood Classifieds & News CMS
description: We need an experienced technical lead or small agency to customize proven off-the-shelf software (LaraClassifier / Laravel for classifieds, Ghost or WordPress/Newspack for editorial), integrate NYC-specific geo data, and ship web plus React Native mobile apps for iOS and Android. What You Will Build • Classifieds (LaraClassifier / Laravel): Custom theme, NYC category structure, Stripe listing tiers (Free / Featured / Premium), listing lifecycle, and admin moderation panel. • Neighborhood localization: NYPD precinct boundary GeoJSON (NYC Open Data, 77 precincts) loaded into PostGIS. Point-in-polygon tagging on every listing. Google Maps autocomplete + precinct overlay. Auto-generated SEO landing pages per precinct. • Trust & verification: Persona.com government ID + selfie check with badge write-back. Twilio SMS phone verification. Google Business Profile OAuth + Places API star rating display. Three-tier badge (Email / Phone / ID Verified) on listing cards and profiles. • In-app messaging: Real-time WebSocket threads (Socket.io or Ably), message requests, email notifications, block/report, verification badges visible in every thread. • React Native apps (iOS + Android): Browse, listing, messaging, and verification flows. FCM push notifications. Native Google Maps with precinct overlay. Camera/photo upload. App Store and Google Play submission. • News CMS (Ghost or Newspack): Install, brand, and configure on agreed subdomain. Neighborhood-tagged articles tie to precinct landing pages. Email newsletter integration. Sponsored content zones. Required Skills — Non-Negotiable Do not apply if you cannot show production examples of the following: • Laravel / PHP: LaraClassifier or equivalent Laravel classifieds platform experience. Show us what you built. • PostGIS / spatial SQL: GeoJSON ingestion, point-in-polygon queries, geometry indexing. We will test this. • React Native: At least one app live on the App Store and Google Play. Include links in your proposal. • Google Maps Platform: Maps JS API, Places Autocomplete, Maps SDK for React Native, GeoJSON overlay rendering. • Stripe + Twilio + Persona.com: Production integrations with all three (or direct equivalents). • Ghost or WordPress/Newspack: CMS deployment, custom theming, newsletter integration. • DevOps: Laravel Forge / DigitalOcean or AWS, GitHub Actions CI/CD, staging + production environments. Engagement Details • Type: Fixed-price, milestone-based (7 milestones, 14–16 weeks). • Team: Small agency (2–4 devs) or senior freelancer with subcontractors. One point of contact required. • Communication: Weekly video check-in, async written updates. English fluency required. • IP: All custom code transfers to nyc.news on final payment. • Start: Immediate upon SOW signature. Discovery begins within 5 business days. How to Apply Generic proposals will not be reviewed. Include the following: 1. Your LaraClassifier (or Laravel classifieds) experience — what you built and how you customized it. 2. App Store / Google Play links for at least one React Native app you shipped. 3. Your approach to the NYPD precinct / PostGIS data model and point-in-polygon lookup. 4. Fixed-price quote broken down by milestone, with total hours and hourly rate equivalent.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer — Shopify App (API + Architecture + Dashboard)
description: We're building a B2B SaaS Shopify app — an analytics dashboard that helps merchants track and improve a specific performance metric using AI. Backend API, database, and core logic exist but need an experienced developer to own the full technical stack: review and improve the architecture, extend the API, and build the frontend on top. What's Already Done - Backend API — FastAPI (Python), ~30 endpoints, deployed. Works but needs a senior set of eyes. - Database — Supabase (Postgres), ~20 tables, in production. - Scoring engine + async job pipelines — Built and functional. - LLM integrations — OpenAI API already integrated. - Figma designs — Complete for all pages. What You'll Own 1. Architecture & API (the important part) - Deep review of the existing FastAPI codebase, DB schema, and deployment setup - Identify issues: performance, security, structure, scalability - Refactor and improve where needed - Extend the API as the frontend requires it - Own the backend going forward 2. Shopify App + Frontend - Shopify app setup (OAuth, App Bridge, embedded experience) - Build ~7 screens from Figma: onboarding, auth, dashboard overview, prompts + detail views, recommendations, settings - API integration across all views - Public shareable dashboard URL - Shopify App Store submission This is not a frontend-only role. We need someone who's as comfortable debugging a FastAPI endpoint or optimizing a Postgres query as they are building a React page. We're looking for an individual developer, not an agency. Requirements - Strong backend experience with Python and FastAPI (or equivalent) - Solid understanding of relational DB design and query optimization (Postgres) - Published at least one Shopify app (non-negotiable — Shopify APP, NOT Shopify Website) - React/Next.js skills for the frontend layer - Experience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini) and async job patterns - Able to review someone else's code, form an opinion, and improve it - Works independently, ships without hand-holding Budget & Scope - Timeline: ~6–8 weeks - Commitment: 30-40 hrs/week - Location: Remote, async-friendly - Long-term potential after v1 To Apply 1. Link to a Shopify app you published 2. Example of backend/API work you've done 3. Your rate and availability 4. When you can start
