title: Senior Full-Stack Developer — Next.js 15 + Supabase + Checkout.com Rental Platform
description: We are building a luxury multi-vendor short-term rental marketplace built exclusively for Dubai's weekly and monthly furnished accommodation market. Think Airbnb, but engineered from the ground up for Dubai's DET (Department of Economy & Tourism) compliance requirements, UAE payment infrastructure, and the specific needs of three vendor types: Independent Homeowners, Property Management Companies (PMCs), and Hotel Apartment operators. We have completed an exhaustive 200+ decision discovery process with a full System Requirements Specification (SRS v2.0), a CTO Audit Report, and an investor-grade technical architecture brief. We are NOT looking for someone to figure out what to build. We are looking for someone to build it to a precise, fully-documented specification. REQUIRED: You must be willing to read the full SRS (attached) before quoting. Proposals without evidence of SRS review will not be considered. 1.1 What Makes This Project Different • Full SRS v2.0 already written — 14 sections, 200+ decisions locked. No discovery phase needed. • CTO Audit Report identifies exactly which components are critical vs deferred. • UI wireframes already designed — homepage, search results, listing detail, mobile all prototyped. • Tech stack already decided — Next.js 15, Tailwind, Supabase, Checkout.com, Sanity CMS. • Dubai Math Engine already coded — Tourism Dirham formula with 30-night cap. You implement, not design. • You focus on execution, not architecture. The architecture is done. 2. Tech Stack — Fixed, Non-Negotiable NOTE: Do not propose alternative stacks. These have been selected after extensive technical review. Proposals suggesting Firebase, WordPress, or other stacks will be rejected. Layer Technology Your Role Frontend Next.js 15 (App Router) Build all pages, components, and layouts Styling Tailwind CSS Implement design system per wireframes Database & Auth Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage) Schema, RLS policies, Edge Functions Payment Gateway Checkout.com (single merchant) Webhooks, idempotency, refund logic BNPL Tabby via Checkout.com Checkout integration CMS Sanity.io Schema setup, admin content editing Email Resend.com Transactional email templates SMS Twilio SMS notifications WhatsApp WhatsApp Business API Primary notification channel — all templates Maps Google Maps Platform Search map, area pins, price markers Deployment Vercel CI/CD, preview deployments, Edge Functions Accounting Xero API Phase 2 — basic integration 2.1 AI Builder Workflow We will use Lovable.dev or Bolt.new for rapid UI scaffolding of non-critical pages. Your role is to: 1. Review and refactor AI-generated code for production quality 2. Implement all business logic, Dubai compliance code, and payment integration from scratch — never AI-generated 3. Set up Supabase schema, RLS policies, and Edge Functions — never AI-generated 4. Own all security-critical code: authentication, payments, document vault, RLS 3. Scope of Work — 6-Week MVP 3.1 Week-by-Week Deliverables Week Deliverable Milestone Payment Week 1 Supabase schema + RLS policies + Auth (all 4 methods) + Checkout.com account setup 20% of total Week 2 Vendor onboarding flow + property/unit CRUD + DET permit upload + 3 property types 15% of total Week 3 Guest search + Filters modal + map toggle + listing detail page 15% of total Week 4 Complete booking flow + Dubai Math engine + Checkout.com webhooks + notifications 25% of total Week 5 Admin panel + vendor dashboard + guest dashboard + CMS setup 15% of total Week 6 QA audit + Dubai Math test suite + penetration test prep + soft launch 10% of total 3.2 Critical Technical Items — Must Implement REQUIRED: These are non-negotiable technical requirements. Each must be implemented correctly before milestone payment is released. A. Dubai Math Engine Tourism Dirham calculation with 30-night cap. Formula: AED 10 x bedrooms x min(nights, 30). Must be server-side only — never calculated in frontend. Must include automated test suite with 7-night, 30-night, and 45-night test cases. B. Booking Race Condition Prevention Concurrent booking protection using PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE within a database transaction. Two guests cannot book the same unit simultaneously. This is a day-1 requirement — not optional. C. Webhook Idempotency Every Checkout.com webhook handler must check a processed_webhook_events table before processing. Duplicate webhook delivery must be a no-op. Required to prevent double bookings and double payouts. D. Supabase RLS Policies Every table must have Row Level Security enabled and tested. Vendor A must never be able to query Vendor B's data. Guest documents must only be accessible to the booking vendor and admin. Must be tested with two separate vendor accounts before milestone release. E. Financial Audit Trail booking_events table capturing every state change with actor, timestamp, and payload snapshot. INSERT-only — no UPDATE or DELETE. Required for UAE regulatory compliance. F. Guest Document Vault Supabase private storage bucket with signed URL access (1-hour expiry). Document numbers stored as SHA-256 hash only. Raw document number never stored in database. G. WhatsApp Notification System All 9 notification templates submitted for Meta approval simultaneously on week 1. WhatsApp delivery confirmation via webhook. SMS fallback on failed WhatsApp delivery. Notification preference per user. 4. What We Provide to the Developer CONFIRMED: Full SRS v2.0 (14 sections, 200+ locked decisions) — attached to this post CONFIRMED: CTO Audit Report with all architecture decisions, gaps, and code samples for the 3 critical fixes CONFIRMED: Complete UI wireframes — homepage, search results (desktop + mobile), listing detail, booking flow, dashboards CONFIRMED: Dubai Math formula with test cases — you implement, not design CONFIRMED: Database schema decisions including all table structures and RLS policy requirements CONFIRMED: Payment flow diagrams — full money flow, T+1 payout logic, refund scenarios CONFIRMED: Notification template content for all 9 WhatsApp templates CONFIRMED: Checkout.com will be set up before week 1 starts — API keys ready CONFIRMED: Sanity.io account and schema requirements ready for week 5 CONFIRMED: Daily async communication via WhatsApp — founder available for questions same-day 5. Developer Requirements — Non-Negotiable 5.1 Must-Have Skills Skill Minimum Level Proof Required in Proposal Next.js (App Router, SSR) Expert — 3+ years Portfolio link with Next.js App Router project Supabase (PostgreSQL + RLS + Edge Functions) Expert — must understand RLS Example of RLS policy you wrote Tailwind CSS Proficient Portfolio — design quality will be assessed Checkout.com or Stripe webhook integration Proficient Describe a payment webhook you built with idempotency TypeScript Proficient All code must be TypeScript — no JavaScript API integration (WhatsApp, Maps, email) Proficient List APIs you have integrated Git / GitHub Proficient GitHub profile required PostgreSQL (transactions, locks) Proficient Explain SELECT FOR UPDATE in your proposal 5.2 Nice-to-Have (Will Prioritize) • Previous OTA (Online Travel Agency) or rental marketplace experience • Experience building UAE or GCC-market products • Sanity.io CMS experience • Supabase Edge Functions and pg_cron experience • Experience with WhatsApp Business API and Meta template approval process • Checkout.com specifically (vs Stripe) — marketplace payment experience 5.3 Disqualifying Factors REQUIRED: Do NOT apply if any of these apply to you: • You propose to use Firebase, MongoDB, WordPress, or any stack other than the one specified • You propose to build a native iOS/Android app — PWA only for this phase • You cannot commit to UAE timezone overlap (GMT+4) for at least 4 hours per day • You have never implemented Row Level Security in Supabase or PostgreSQL • You propose to use AI code generators for payment or security logic • You cannot provide code samples demonstrating webhook idempotency or database transactions • Your portfolio shows no production Next.js App Router projects 6. How to Apply — Read This Carefully We will review proposals within 48 hours. We receive many proposals and reject any that do not follow this structure. This is intentional — attention to detail is the first test. 6.1 Your Proposal Must Include — In This Exact Order 5. The code word 'DET-VERIFIED' at the top of your proposal. This confirms you read the full job post. 6. Your fixed-price quote for the 6-week MVP. Itemise by milestone (not a single total). 7. Your availability confirmation: how many hours per day, what timezone, and your UTC+4 overlap window. 8. One paragraph explaining how you would implement the booking race condition fix. This is a technical filter — vague answers will be rejected. 9. One paragraph explaining how you would implement Supabase RLS to ensure Vendor A cannot read Vendor B's bookings. 10. One real example of a webhook handler you built with idempotency. Code snippet preferred. 11. 3 portfolio links — specifically Next.js App Router projects you built. Describe your role on each. 12. Your Supabase experience: how many production Supabase projects, and which features you have used (RLS, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage). 13. One question you have about the SRS after reading it. This confirms you actually read the document. 6.2 Budget Guidance NOTE: The budget range is USD 4,000 + for the 6-week MVP. We expect quotes in this range. Quotes above USD 10,000 will not be considered. Quotes below USD 3,000 suggest the developer has not read the scope. We will pay a fair rate for excellent work. Milestone Scope Payment % Milestone 1 — Week 1 Schema + Auth + Checkout.com setup 20% Milestone 2 — Weeks 2-3 Vendor flow + search + listing detail 30% Milestone 3 — Week 4 Booking flow + payments + Dubai Math + notifications 25% Milestone 4 — Week 5 Dashboards + CMS 15% Milestone 5 — Week 6 QA + soft launch 10% 6.3 Selection Process 14. Application review — we read every proposal that follows the structure above 15. Technical screening call — 30 minutes, GMT+4 timezone, we will ask you to explain your RLS approach live 16. Paid test task — fixed — implement the booking race condition fix in a clean Supabase project 17. Contract — Upwork fixed-price contract with milestone releases 7. Working Arrangement Item Detail Communication WhatsApp for async updates. Weekly video call (30 min). Response within 4 hours during UAE business hours. Code repository GitHub — private repo, founder has full access at all times Code review All PRs reviewed by founder + CTO advisor before merge Testing Unit tests for all Dubai Math functions. Integration tests for booking flow. RLS tests with dual vendor accounts. Documentation All Supabase Edge Functions must have JSDoc comments. README must be kept current. Ownership All code is 100% client-owned. Full IP transfer on final milestone payment. Phase 2 Preferred developer will be offered Phase 2 contract (Operations Module, Channel Manager integration, Arabic language) immediately after successful MVP. NDA Standard NDA will be signed via Upwork before SRS is shared.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for Multi-Tenant QA/QC SaaS (Commercial Grade
description: Project Overview We are building the Ladnek QC Platform, a multi-tenant Quality Assurance and Service Verification system. While the initial rollout is for our own facilities firm, the system is being built as a commercial SaaS product for the Caribbean service contractor market. We need a developer who is fast, precise, and experienced in building subscription-based software. The Tech Stack (Strict Requirements) Frontend: Next.js 14/15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui . Backend: Node.js (NestJS or Next.js Route Handlers). Database & ORM: PostgreSQL with Prisma. Infrastructure: Redis/BullMQ for background jobs and S3/R2 for photo storage. Auth: Auth.js or Clerk with robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). Core Deliverables (Phase 1 MVP) 1. Multi-Tenant & Subscription Architecture Organization Isolation: Logical data separation using organizationId across all tables. Monetization Engine: Integration with Stripe (or equivalent) for subscription management. Tiered Access: Logic to gate features (e.g., limit the number of "Inspector" seats or "Sites" based on the plan) . 2. Service Hierarchy & Site Setup Full CRUD for Customers → Contracts → Sites → Buildings → Areas . Support for QR code values at the Area level for future service validation. 3. Dynamic Inspection & Scoring Engine Template Builder: Weighted scoring forms with multiple response types (Pass/Fail, 1-5 Rating, Photo required, N/A toggles) . Execution: Mobile-responsive audit forms with offline-save capability, GPS capture, and auto-calculated scores . 4. Automated Corrective Actions (Ticketing) System must auto-generate tickets when an inspection item fails or falls below a threshold. Centralized queue for assigning, tracking, and closing issues with before/after photo evidence . 5. Analytics & Exportable Reporting Performance dashboards for QA Managers and read-only portals for Clients. Server-side PDF generation for formal site inspection reports. Developer Requirements (How to Apply) We are looking for a developer who can move from the provided Prisma schema to a coded MVP in 6 weeks . To be considered, please answer: Multi-Tenancy: How do you ensure one tenant cannot see another tenant's data at the API/Query level? SaaS Experience: Have you integrated a subscription/billing system (like Stripe) into a multi-role app before? Speed: We have a full Developer Package (Prisma schema, UI wireframes, and API specs) ready. How soon can you begin Sprint 1 (Auth & Org Setup)?

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (Platform)
description: About Us: ValiantAI is a fully autonomous marketing platform that handles the entire lifecycle, from strategy and content creation through publishing, optimization, and reporting, across email, ads, and social. We're a fast-growing team of 20 with high standards for the people we work with and the product we ship. The work is technically deep: multi-channel autonomy, continuous learning across industries, and real-time optimization at scale. We're looking for people who thrive on that kind of challenge. If that sounds like you, apply below. About the Role: Salary: $60,000 per year for the first 60 days, then $72,000 per year Hours: Full-time Location: Remote (UK, US, Canada, Europe) Perks: Quarterly team offsites, performance-based milestones, and the opportunity to work on meaningful, high-impact challenges alongside a sharp team As a Senior Full-Stack Developer, you’ll be responsible for building and enhancing the systems that sit at the core of our platform. This isn’t about shipping isolated features or maintaining internal tools no one uses. It’s about improving the parts of the product that shape outputs, power execution, and make the platform more useful, more reliable, and more scalable. You’ll work across both backend and frontend systems, helping us improve AI outputs, execution features, and the user-facing product experience. That includes everything from the logic and infrastructure behind the platform to the interfaces our users interact with every day. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering, product, and design to move quickly, solve real problems, and ship work that has a direct impact. Your success is measured by one thing: how much better the platform becomes because of what you build. What You'll Do: - Build and improve core product features across both frontend and backend - Develop systems that enhance AI outputs and make them more reliable, useful, and scalable - Build execution features that help the platform take action more effectively across workflows - Contribute to the frontend of our web application, improving usability, speed, and overall product quality - Help build and maintain websites, landing pages, and other user-facing frontend experiences where needed - Work across APIs, databases, business logic, and UI to ship full product features end to end - Collaborate with product and design to turn ideas into clean, production-ready implementations - Identify bottlenecks, fix issues, and continuously improve system performance and maintainability - Help shape internal architecture and engineering standards as the platform scales Who You Are: - You hold yourself to a high standard. Some people might call you a perfectionist. You call it baseline. - You have energy and drive. You're drawn to big problems, not easy ones. Obsession is a feature, not a flaw. - You're sharp. You learn fast, think clearly, and can connect dots others miss. You solve problems with first principles, not gut feelings. - You communicate directly. You can articulate your thinking with clarity and you don't hold back. - You care. About the team, the customers, and the work itself. You're not the type to ship sloppy code or leave rough edges in the product. The details matter to you because the outcome matters to you. - You have deep expertise. Not surface-level familiarity, but real, narrow, deep competency in your craft. You understand your discipline at a microscopic level and can reason about it from the ground up. - You're a good person. Ethical, principled, not overly judgmental. You are nice to work with and are understanding when things don’t go as planned. You'll thrive here if you: - Love camaraderie, team offsites, and actually enjoying the people you work with - Care about product quality, systems thinking, and building things that genuinely work - Get excited about working across unique industries and cutting-edge technology Nice-to-haves: - Experience in startups or fast-moving environments - Experience building AI-powered products or internal tools - Strong frontend instincts and an eye for product quality - Experience working across both user-facing interfaces and backend systems - Comfort owning features end to end without needing heavy oversight How to Apply: Please submit a 1-2 minute Loom video along with your application. In your video, please cover the following: - Who are you? Quick background on your journey - How did you get into full-stack development? What experience do you have across frontend and backend? - Rate yourself from 1-10 as a full-stack developer and why. What makes you that number? - What have you built? Walk us through products, systems, or features you’ve worked on - Have you worked on AI products or automation systems before? If so, tell us about them - What technologies do you prefer to use? Walk us through your stack - What are you currently working on? Job, freelance, or personal projects - Why this role? What interests you about building and improving the core platform at ValiantAI?

title: Full-Stack Developer (Next.js/Supabase) – AI-Augmented Development & CRM Feature Expansion
description: I am looking for a skilled Full-Stack Developer to continue the development of iOpt_CRM, a custom CRM solution. This project was built using Claude Code (Anthropic’s CLI tool), and I am looking for a developer who is comfortable working with AI tools to maintain and expand the codebase rather than performing a manual rewrite. I value speed and efficiency. This project utilizes AI for rapid prototyping and feature deployment. Your role is to act as the "pilot"—using AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot) to generate code, while providing the expert oversight to ensure security, performance, and logical consistency. Key Responsibilities Feature Development: Build out new modules (Leads, Pipeline management, Reporting) within the existing framework. AI Orchestration: Use AI coding assistants to accelerate development while ensuring the output matches the existing project structure. Database Management: Manage and optimize Supabase (PostgreSQL) schemas, Row Level Security (RLS), and edge functions. UI/UX: Implement clean, responsive interfaces using Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui. Code Quality: Review AI-generated code for technical debt and ensure it integrates seamlessly with the current architecture. Required Skills Frameworks: Next.js (App Router), React, and TypeScript. Backend/Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage). Styling: Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components. Tooling: Experience with Claude Code, Cursor, or deep proficiency in prompting AI for complex coding tasks. Git: Strong version control habits.

title: Lead Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
description: We are Topflight Apps! We are a team of innovative product managers, founders, designers, and developers. Together our mission is to build products that help millions of lives by tackling some of the biggest problems in healthcare and fintech. Our company was founded in 2017 in Irvine, California, and each year since, we have roughly doubled, reaching the $4 million revenue mark last year and raising our first seed fund as well. Due to our expansion, we’re looking for a high-growth Senior Full-Stack Developer ready to transition into their first Lead role. We need a modern architecture enthusiast and AI-augmented power user who is ready to lead our projects and team by example. Responsibilities: - AI Strategy: Lead the team in leveraging AI code generation tools (e.g., Cursor, Copilot, LLMs) to accelerate development and ship features at record speeds. - Discovery & Estimation: Work closely with PMs and POs to provide technical roadmaps and rapid prototyping estimates. - Technical Architecture: Enforce and evolve the company’s modern stack (React, React Native, Convex, Supabase, Drizzle) across projects. Strategic Problem Solving: Act as the primary technical support for PMs, identifying "hacker" shortcuts and workarounds to overcome roadblocks without sacrificing quality. - Code Quality & Mentorship: Review code for performance, type safety (TypeScript), and maintainability. Mentor developers in adopting AI-augmented workflows. - Scope Management: Manage the project's technical scope and handle deployment communication with DevOps/Vercel/Supabase environments. - Full Stack Execution: Lead by example by building complex features, managing database schemas with Drizzle, and crafting polished UIs with shadcn/ui. Requirements: - 5 to 8 years of experience building production-level web and mobile applications. - Mastery of the Modern Stack: Strong expertise in Next.js (App Router), React, React Native, and Node.js. - Advanced TypeScript: Deep understanding of type-safe development across the entire stack. - Database & ORM: Strong skills in PostgreSQL and hands-on experience with Supabase and Drizzle ORM. - UI/UX Excellence: Expert-level command of TailwindCSS and shadcn/ui (or similar headless UI libraries). - AI-Native Workflow: Proven expertise in using AI tools to significantly reduce development timelines. You should be "AI-first" in your coding approach. - Architectural Vision: Sound understanding of MVC, serverless architectures, and modern design patterns. - Communication: Ability to participate in client calls, simplifying complex technical matters for stakeholders. - The "Hacker" Mentality: An independent drive to "figure things out" and the mental fortitude to handle rapid development cycles and tight deadlines. Bonus Points: - Industry Experience: Previous work with healthcare (HIPAA) or fintech organizations. LLM Integration: Proven experience integrating OpenAI, Anthropic, or LangChain APIs into functional software. - Agency Background: Prior experience working in a fast-paced agency en+vironment. - Cloud Infrastructure: Knowledge of AWS, Vercel, and CI/CD pipelines. - 0 to 6 months experience as Tech Lead. Important Notes: - We are a fully remote company with a global team. Flexibility with time zones and excellent communication skills are crucial - All new Topflight Apps team members undergo a one-month trial period to ensure smooth onboarding and alignment on expectations How to Apply: Please ensure all outreach and applications are submitted through the designated job posting platform. Note that any use of alternative platforms will lead to automatic disqualification.

title: Python Full Stack Developer / Python AI Developer
description: We are looking for an experienced Python Full Stack Developer / Python AI Developer to join our team and help build scalable web applications, intelligent automation tools, and AI-powered product features. This role is ideal for someone with strong backend expertise in Python, solid frontend experience with React, JavaScript, HTML, HTML5, and CSS, and hands-on knowledge of modern architectures, databases, and integrations. You will work on full-cycle product development, from backend services and database design to responsive user interfaces and AI-driven workflows. We are looking for a specialist who can build reliable systems with Django or Flask, create and maintain API integrations, develop automation tools and Python Script solutions, and support data-focused tasks such as Data Scraping. Experience with Node.js or PHP is also valuable for integrations, legacy systems, or multi-stack environments. Responsibilities: Build and maintain full stack applications using Python Develop backend services with Django and Flask Create responsive frontend interfaces using React, JavaScript, HTML, HTML5, and CSS Design and maintain API integrations and internal services Work with SQL, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB Develop automation tools, Python Script solutions, and Data Scraping workflows Use Docker for development and deployment environments Support projects that may include Node.js or PHP components Requirements Strong experience with Python (3+ years) Hands-on experience with FastAPI (or strong willingness to adopt it) Experience building LLM-based systems (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) Solid understanding of vector search (pgvector, Supabase, Pinecone, etc.) Experience with LangGraph / LangChain / similar AI orchestration tools Practical experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization Strong database knowledge: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB Experience with Supabase or similar backend-as-a-service platforms Nice to Have Experience with RAG system design in production Knowledge of embedding strategies and semantic search optimization Experience with real-time systems or streaming architectures Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or similar) We are looking for a reliable developer who communicates clearly, takes ownership of tasks, and delivers high-quality work

title: Senior Full-Stack / AI Product Engineer for Commerce Intelligence MVP
description: We are building a founder-facing commerce intelligence product for Amazon-led brands. This is not a generic dashboard project. We need a senior builder to create the first MVP of a long-term global platform. The system will ingest cached commerce data from third-party sources, standardise it into a clean internal model, apply deterministic scoring, and surface decision-ready outputs through a lightweight founder portal and Telegram digest. This first contract is for the MVP only. There is a larger roadmap behind it. This is a long-term global build, not a one-off freelance task. We already have the product direction defined, and larger follow-on budget is planned after this MVP is successfully delivered and approved. What we need in this MVP 1. Data layer Connect third-party commerce data sources into a warehouse, ideally BigQuery or Postgres Pull an initial set of cached commerce data Build a lean canonical model for: account daily performance marketplace daily performance product-level daily performance advertising daily summary 2. Rules / scoring layer Build deterministic logic to detect: sales drops CVR drops efficiency issues stock risk where data is available Store alerts in a structured way 3. MVP product layer Build a simple founder dashboard that shows: yesterday sales MTD sales best / worst market top product snapshot active alerts recommended next action 4. Delivery layer Telegram daily digest clean daily summary output from the system 5. AI layer Implement only 2 narrow AI workflows in this MVP: daily founder summary anomaly explanation We do not want an over-engineered multi-agent build in Stage 1. We want a clean, working MVP with strong foundations. What success looks like By the end of this contract, we want: data flowing into the warehouse correctly clean core tables built basic scoring / alerts working founder dashboard home working Telegram digest working 2 narrow AI outputs working code and structure clean enough to continue into future phases Why this project matters This is the first stage of a much larger build. We are creating a global product layer around commerce intelligence, designed to scale across multiple brands and marketplaces. The right person will not just be building a dashboard. They will be helping shape the foundation of a serious long-term platform. We are looking for someone who can prove themselves through a focused MVP, then continue with us into larger follow-on phases. What we are looking for You should be strong across most of the following: full-stack product development data modelling and warehousing API integrations LLM workflow implementation clean MVP thinking dashboard UX for decision-making products ability to ship fast without making a mess Strong preference for experience in: commerce data founder dashboards BigQuery or Postgres Telegram bot integrations AI product features long-term product builds, not just one-off freelance tasks Not a fit This is not for: junior developers generic dashboard freelancers pure prompt engineers bloated agency teams We want a strong senior builder who can ship an MVP properly and potentially grow with the project. Budget Fixed price: $3500 for Stage 1 MVP This is the budget for the first MVP only. Further budget and future phases will be approved only after Stage 1 is completed and accepted. Engagement structure This is intended to become a long-term engagement for the right person. We are building this in stages. Stage 1 is the proof point. After that, the roadmap expands materially.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer – Multi-Tenant SaaS Website Builder (Next.js + Plasmic)
description: We are seeking a high-calibre Senior Full-Stack Developer to architect the core infrastructure for a niche vertical, proprietary website builder and performance platform designed for a specific creative professional audience. We are moving away from a white-label platform to a custom headless solution that offers 100% brand ownership and elite performance. The Role You will build a multi-tenant Next.js application that serves as the shell for a white-labelled website building experience. You will work closely with our Head of Creative to turn high-fidelity Figma designs into a functional, scalable SaaS. Vercel is the chosen infrastructure layer and is not negotiable — it is an architectural decision central to how multi-tenancy is implemented in this project. Key Technical Requirements Multi-Tenancy via Vercel Edge Middleware The entire multi-tenant routing layer is built on Vercel Edge Middleware. Incoming hostnames are resolved at the network edge, tenant data is looked up, and requests are rewritten to the correct page tree — all in under 5ms globally. You must have hands-on experience with this pattern or a demonstrably equivalent implementation. Wildcard domain support (*.ourdomain.io) and programmatic custom domain addition via the Vercel API are both required. White-Labelled Visual Editor Integration Embed Plasmic Studio (Content Creator Mode) into our custom dashboard. Users must be able to edit their sites within our UI without ever seeing third-party branding. Stripe Billing Engine Implement a 14-day free trial (credit card upfront) using Stripe Billing. Logic must include automatic renewal at Day 15 and immediate site suspension via webhooks if payment is declined. Domain Automation Integrate Entri or Approximated.app for one-click DNS configuration. Custom domains are added to the Vercel project programmatically via API — no manual DevOps at any point. Figma-to-Code Pipeline Support our design team in syncing complex, animated Figma components into Plasmic and ensuring they are production-ready. Component Library & Slot Architecture Build a centralised Component Registry where our Head of Creative can publish Plasmic components that automatically appear in every user's editor sidebar. Implement slot-based architecture allowing users to drag and drop pre-approved sections into a blank canvas while maintaining the global design system. Blank project provisioning must be available via Plasmic API — not manually. Technical Stack Frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS Backend: PostgreSQL via Supabase, Prisma ORM, Node.js Editor: Plasmic (Headless/Embedded mode) Payments: Stripe Billing + Webhooks Infrastructure: Vercel — Edge Middleware, wildcard domains, custom domain API, preview deployments, Speed Insights, ISR Auth: Supabase Auth / NextAuth Storage: Supabase Storage / Cloudflare R2 Requirements Proven experience building multi-tenant SaaS platforms from scratch — please provide a live example or GitHub repo Demonstrable experience with Vercel Edge Middleware for multi-tenant routing specifically Deep expertise in Stripe Webhooks — idempotency, signature verification, and full subscription lifecycle handling Experience with Plasmic or a comparable headless visual builder is highly preferred Next.js App Router proficiency — Edge Middleware, dynamic routing, and ISR TypeScript fluency throughout — no JavaScript fallbacks Availability for weekly video syncs and async communication throughout the build Ability to work under a strict NDA To Apply Please provide: a brief description of the most architecturally similar project you have built, your specific experience with Vercel Edge Middleware for multi-tenancy, your Plasmic (or equivalent) experience, your approach to Stripe webhook idempotency, your proposed timeline for Phase 1, and your rate. Applications without these specifics will not be progressed.

title: Lead Full-Stack Developer – B2B SaaS
description: About the project We are building an AI-native Marketing Operating System for B2B companies — a platform that helps Marketing Managers centralize, automate, and optimize their entire marketing workflow in one place. We are pre-launch with 15 active paying beta users and a waitlist of 50+. The product is real, the team is committed, and we are now looking for a lead developer to take technical ownership. What you will own Architecture and hands-on development of the core SaaS platform (React frontend, Node.js backend) AI feature integration (LLM APIs, automation pipelines, intelligent content modules) Driving MVP delivery from beta to production — clean code, scalable structure Direct collaboration with our CTO and founding team — you shape the product, not just execute tasks What we are looking for 3+ years of production experience with React and Node.js Demonstrated experience building SaaS products — not just client work, but products with real users Experience integrating AI/LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar) into production applications Strong ownership mindset — you communicate proactively, flag issues early, and deliver reliably Fluent written English — German is a plus but not required Nice to have Experience with B2B marketing tools or marketing automation platforms Prior work in an early-stage startup or as a technical co-founder Knowledge of the DACH market or European SaaS landscape

title: Full-Stack Developer Needed — AI-Powered Solana DeFi Security Platform
description: We're building MagicVest, a real-time DeFi security terminal focused on the Solana blockchain. Think of it as a Bloomberg Terminal for crypto safety — AI-powered token analysis, whale tracking, rug pull detection, and smart contract auditing. The entire frontend (7,300+ lines of React JSX) is already built and fully functional with mock data. We need a developer to build the backend and connect everything to real APIs. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ THE PROJECT ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ MagicVest has 13 pages/features already designed and coded in the frontend: • Live Scanner — Real-time Solana token list with price, volume, holders, security scores • Magic Radar — Auto-detection of new tokens with pump potential • AI Safe Picks — Claude AI-curated token recommendations with badges • Social Hype Tracker — Aggregated social sentiment (Twitter, Telegram, Reddit) • Whale Tracker — Monitor 100+ known Solana whale wallets with PnL tracking • Contract Scanner — AI-powered smart contract audit (mint/freeze authority, LP status) • Rug Alerts — Real-time rug pull detection with 2 second notification • LP Monitor — Liquidity lock surveillance (Raydium, Orca, Meteora pools) • Scammer Database — Registry of known scam wallets with community reports • Token Profile — Detailed page combining all data for a single token • Portfolio/Watchlist — Personal token tracking with alerts • Holdings — Connected Solana wallet portfolio (via Wallet Adapter) • Notifications — Push alert system (WebSocket + FCM) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ TECH STACK ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ • Frontend: React (JSX), already built — responsive, dark theme, animations • Backend: Node.js (Express or Fastify) • Database: MongoDB Atlas • Cache: Redis (Upstash or similar) • AI: Anthropic Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) • Blockchain: Solana only (SPL tokens) • Real-time: Socket.io • Auth: Firebase Auth + Solana Wallet Adapter • Payments: Stripe (subscription model)

title: Full Stack Developer (Node.js, React, Next.js)
description: We are looking for an experienced Full Stack Developer to join our team and help build, improve and scale modern web applications. The ideal candidate should be confident working across both frontend and backend, with strong expertise in Node.js, React, JavaScript, TypeScript and Next.js. In this role, you will develop responsive, high-performance web applications, create clean and maintainable code, integrate third-party services, and contribute to both product functionality and user experience. You should have solid knowledge of HTML, HTML5, CSS, CSS 3, Bootstrap, jQuery, Responsive Design, Web Design, MongoDB, and API Integration. Responsibilities: Build and maintain frontend and backend features for web applications Develop scalable server-side logic using Node.js Create modern, responsive interfaces with React, Next.js, HTML5, CSS 3 Work with JavaScript and TypeScript to deliver clean, reliable code Handle database operations and backend logic with MongoDB Implement and maintain API Integration with third-party services Ensure strong performance, cross-browser compatibility, and Responsive Design Collaborate with the team on UI improvements and overall Web Design We are looking for a reliable developer who can take ownership of tasks, communicate clearly, and deliver high-quality work.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for Lean SaaS MVP (Next.js / Supabase / Stripe / Clay webhook)
description: Title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for Lean SaaS MVP (Next.js / Supabase / Stripe / Clay webhook) Job post: We’re looking for a strong senior full-stack developer to build a lean MVP for a sales data SaaS product. This is a simple web app for sales reps. Users will be able to sign up, receive a small number of free trial credits, buy more credits, create and manage lists, paste LinkedIn profile URLs into a list, and receive returned results from Clay. Important: All enrichment logic and mobile lookup waterfall logic happen inside Clay. This app is the user workspace around that process. What the app needs to do user signup / login free trial credits for new users admin can change the number of free credits given to first-time users Stripe payments for buying more credits list management: create rename archive delete user can paste LinkedIn profile URLs into a list app sends records to Clay app receives Clay callback/webhook results show returned statuses in the UI deduct 1 credit only when Clay returns a successful result allow user to add call outcomes and notes CSV export of results simple admin controls Phase 1 only We want a clean, fast MVP. We are not building the following in phase 1: CRM integrations Chrome extension dialler / calling integration complex team workspace features advanced analytics Preferred stack Next.js TypeScript Node.js Supabase Stripe experience with webhook / callback integrations We’re looking for someone who has experience with: SaaS products admin panels / dashboards Stripe payments credit / usage logic webhook-driven workflows secure, practical MVP builds Please include in your proposal: Your fixed-price quote for phase 1 Estimated timeline Relevant examples of similar SaaS / internal tools / dashboard apps you’ve built Your recommended architecture for this build What you see as the riskiest part of the build Whether you would build this solo or with a small team We already have a detailed MVP spec and will share it with shortlisted candidates. We are looking for someone practical who can ship quickly, communicate clearly, and avoid overengineering.

title: Experienced Full-Stack Developer — AI-Integrated SaaS Web App
duration: 1 to 3 months, Hours to be determined
description: We are building a multi-screen SaaS web application where AI is central to the product — not an add-on. The platform includes a structured onboarding experience, personalized dashboard, AI-generated document outputs, a real-time conversation interface, and a full user profile and auth system. The product is designed for a non-technical consumer audience and must be polished and production-ready. A complete Figma design will be shared with the selected developer. We are currently at the quoting stage. Skills required (production experience, not just familiarity): Next.js / TypeScript, Supabase (Postgres), LLM API integration (Claude or GPT-4), prompt engineering, streaming chat UI (SSE/WebSocket), user authentication flows, Figma-to-code implementation, Tailwind CSS, Vercel deployment. Important: Do not apply if your AI experience is limited to chatbots or basic API calls. This project requires structured AI workflows where outputs are personalized from user data. Strong applicants have shipped at least one SaaS product with meaningful LLM integration — please describe it in your proposal. Estimated effort: 250–345 hours total across all modules. To apply, please include: 1. A SaaS or AI product you have shipped (link or description) 2. Your specific experience with LLM APIs in production 3. Your hourly rate or estimated fixed-price range for a 250–345 hour project 4. Your availability and timeline Start your proposal with the words: "I've built AI products." Proposals that do not include this will not be reviewed.

title: Full-Stack Developer (React Native Expo + NestJS + NextJS) — Early-Stage SaaS, Remote - Full time
description: We are filling a full time position Do not email us We're Borradh, an Irish SaaS company replacing marketing agencies for aesthetic and beauty clinics. One platform that handles Meta advertising, an AI-powered lead follow-up chatbot called Claire, and booking automation. Clinic owners pay us instead of an agency and get better results at a fraction of the cost. 40+ paying customers live on the platform across Ireland and the UK. Just raised $110k in pre-seed funding. Currently targeting a $1.6M seed round for August 2026. US market expansion launching this month. This is not an idea or an MVP. It's a live product with real paying customers and real revenue. Where we're heading next: This is the exciting part. We're investing heavily in our React Native mobile app — it's how most of our clinic owners actually run their business day-to-day, and it needs to feel as polished as anything Instagram or Linear ship. We're building an in-app video editor so clinic owners can create ad content directly from their phone without external tools. We're shipping push notification infrastructure, a completely redesigned mobile client dashboard, an in-app messaging system replacing WhatsApp, and deeper integrations with major booking systems like Fresha, Phorest, and Pabau. We're also building Claire into a much more advanced AI system that handles the entire lead journey from first message to booked appointment, and scaling the whole thing on AWS to handle 500+ clinics globally. The product roadmap is aggressive and the work is meaningful. Every feature you build goes live to real customers immediately. What you'll work on: React Native (Expo) mobile app — this is the core of the role. Redesigned mobile client dashboard, in-app video editor for ad creative production, push notification infrastructure (APNs + FCM), in-app client communication system replacing WhatsApp, offline handling, and app store release management. You'll also work across the stack on the NestJS API and Next.js web app that power the mobile experience — Claire AI chatbot enhancements, Meta Graph API integration improvements (token management, error handling, account diagnostics), booking system integrations, and AWS infrastructure scaling. Tech stack: Mobile: React Native (Expo, EAS Build, expo-router) Frontend: React (NextJS) Backend: Express (NestJS) Cloud: AWS APIs: Meta Graph API, Cal.com, various booking system APIs Must have: 3+ years full-stack development experience Strong React Native (Expo) proficiency — production apps shipped to the App Store and Play Store Strong React and NextJS proficiency NestJS or Express backend experience AWS experience (deployment, scaling) Third-party API integration experience Strong written English Self-directed — you ship without being micromanaged Nice to have: EAS Build / EAS Submit / OTA updates experience Native module integration (expo-modules, custom dev clients) Push notification infrastructure (APNs, FCM, Expo push service) Meta Graph API or Messenger Platform experience LLM/AI integration experience n8n or similar workflow automation tools Previous startup experience Multi-tenant SaaS experience Details: Remote (Brazil preferred) Contract, full-time $1,000 to $1,200 USD/month (approximately R$5,700 to R$6,800/month) Working directly with the CTO every single day You're not joining a massive team where your work disappears. There are 2 founders and you. You will have direct input on architecture decisions, product direction, and how things get built. This is a ground-floor opportunity at a company that is actively raising its next round and scaling internationally. ⚠️ DO NOT APPLY WITHOUT THE FOLLOWING — applications without these will be ignored: 1. A short video (2 to 3 minutes max) of yourself on camera explaining one project you have built. Walk us through what it does, what technology you used, what your role was, and one technical challenge you solved. Screen share is fine but we need to see your face and hear you explain it in your own words. Upload to YouTube (unlisted), Loom, or Google Drive and include the link. 2. Your GitHub profile link. We want to see real shipped code, not tutorial repos. If your best work is in private repos, explain what you built and your contribution in a few sentences. 3. One or two links to React Native / Expo apps you have shipped to production — ideally with App Store or Play Store links. We want to see a real mobile app you built, not a web app. If your best mobile work is under NDA, explain what you built and your contribution in a few sentences, and link your strongest web/SaaS work as a secondary sample. If you send a generic cover letter without these three things, we will not respond. We're a small team hiring carefully and we need to see that you can communicate clearly and build real things.

title: Build a Full-Stack Tools SaaS (PDF + Docs + Images) — Next.js / MERN

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (AI-first) — SaaS para terapeutas
description: (English Version) Hi, how are you? I’m Arnau, based in Barcelona. I’m looking for a senior Full-Stack developer who is highly autonomous, pragmatic, and AI-first minded to help us finish and launch a SaaS for therapists that is already quite advanced and has real users. We’re a small, fast team with very little bureaucracy. You’ll be touching code from day one and making decisions that directly impact the product. A lot of freedom, but also a lot of responsibility. We work with Claude Code MAX accounts, so it’s important that you’re comfortable using AI in a CLI environment and, more importantly, that you have the judgment to use it well. The product brings together everything a therapist needs in one place: scheduling, patient management, document signing, video sessions, payments, calendar sync, and team collaboration. The goal is to replace multiple tools with one that actually works. Your work will be straightforward: • Fix real bugs (frontend, backend, database) • Finish incomplete features • Improve and unify UX/UI • Build new features end-to-end You’ll also touch architecture when needed, simplify things that have become overly complex, and help make the system stable, fast, and well-structured. A key part of the role is making the app truly usable on mobile. We’re working with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL (Drizzle), along with Stripe and S3/R2 storage. We care less about knowing the stack and more about your ability to solve problems with good judgment. We’re looking for someone who: • Has worked on SaaS products in production • Can work independently without constant supervision • Has strong technical and product judgment • Speaks Native Spanish • Is available full-time Compensation is $2,000 USD/month, with a long-term collaboration in mind and a salary review if there’s a good fit. To apply, please include: • A short introduction • Relevant projects or repos • How you use AI in your workflow • Your availability If you enjoy getting into real systems and making them actually work, we should talk. Thanks for your time, Arnau (Versión Española) Hola, ¿qué tal? Soy Arnau, de Barcelona. Estoy buscando un/a desarrollador/a Full-Stack con experiencia, muy autónomo/a y con mentalidad AI-first para ayudarnos a terminar y lanzar un SaaS de gestión para terapeutas que ya está bastante avanzado y tiene usuarios reales. Somos un equipo pequeño, rápido y sin burocracia. Aquí vas a tocar código desde el primer día y a tomar decisiones que impactan directamente en el producto. Bastante libertad y bastante responsabilidad. Trabajamos con cuentas de Claude Code MAX, así que es importante que tengas experiencia usando IA en CLI y, sobre todo, criterio para sacarle partido. El producto centraliza el día a día de un terapeuta en un solo sitio: agenda, pacientes, firmas, videoconsultas, pagos, calendario y trabajo en equipo. La idea es sustituir varias herramientas por una que realmente funcione. Este SaaS centraliza todo lo relacionado con el día a día de un terapeuta en un solo sitio: agenda, gestión de pacientes, firmas de documentos, videoconsultas, pagos, calendario y trabajo en equipo. La idea es sustituir varias herramientas por una que realmente funcione. Tu trabajo consistirá en: - Arreglar bugs de la versión actual (frontend, backend, base de datos…) - Terminar funcionalidades pendientes - Mejorar y unificar criterios de UXUI - Construir nuevas features end-to-end También tocarás arquitectura cuando sea necesario, simplificarás cosas que se han complicado y ayudarás a que todo sea estable, rápido y bien estructurado. Parte importante será dejar la app usable de verdad en mobile. Trabajamos con Next.js, React, TypeScript y PostgreSQL (Drizzle), además de Stripe y storage tipo S3/R2. No buscamos a alguien que “conozca el stack”, sino que sepa resolver problemas con criterio propio (esto seguramente es lo más importante!) Buscamos a alguien que: - Haya trabajado en SaaS en producción - Sea capaz de moverse sin supervisión constante - Tenga buen criterio técnico y de producto - Hable español nativo - Pueda trabajar full-time El salario mensual es de $2.000 USD/mes, con idea de colaboración a largo plazo y revisión si hay buen encaje. Para aplicar, envíanos: - Breve presentación - Proyectos o repos relevantes - Cómo usas IA en tu día a día - Disponibilidad Si te encaja nuestra propuesta, nos encantará conocerte. Muchas gracias por tu tiempo y un saludo! arnau

title: URGENT IMMEDIATE HIRING - Full-Stack Developer Needed to Convert Extension into Web App in 3 hours
description: We are seeking a skilled full-stack developer to transform our existing browser extension into a fully functional web application. The ideal candidate will possess strong development skills and experience in both front-end and back-end technologies. You will be responsible for ensuring a seamless transition, maintaining user experience, and enhancing features during the conversion process. If you're passionate about developing web applications and have a keen eye for detail, we want to hear from you!

title: Full-Stack Developer (Rails + Next.js), AI-Native, Русскоязычная команда, EdTech Platform
description: We're hiring a full-stack developer to join a small, high-output team for an EdTech platform that's been operating for 15 years and has thousands of active users. Russian speaker preferred! Our technical team communicates primarily in Russian. Native or fluent Russian is a strong plus. Ukrainian speakers equally welcome. The role: You'll be picking up tickets that span our entire stack: Rails API, Next.js frontend, and infrastructure config, and shipping complete solutions without someone else catching what you missed. This is not a "build a component and hand it off" role. You own the ticket from read to deploy. Our team is three people: a technical lead, a frontend/payments developer, and a QA engineer. We move fast, we communicate asynchronously in Linear, and we expect clean PRs with clear descriptions. If you're someone who reads half the ticket and ships half the solution, this isn't the right fit. What you'll work on: - Feature development across Rails and Next.js (new quiz features, student-facing tools, admin functionality) - Bug fixes that touch multiple layers: a single ticket might involve an Nginx config change, a Rails controller update, and a Next.js page fix - Infrastructure work as we migrate hosting to Render - Security hardening and deployment pipeline improvements - Writing tests and catching your own edge cases before QA does What we're looking for: - 2–5 years of professional experience with Ruby on Rails and React/Next.js - Deployed to managed PaaS platforms: Render, Vercel, Railway, or similar. We're not looking for someone whose only deployment experience is SSH-ing into a VPS. - AI-native workflow. You should be using Cursor, Claude, GitHub Copilot, or similar tools as a core part of how you write code, not as an occasional convenience. We'll ask you to demonstrate this. - Clean communication. You read the full ticket. You ask clarifying questions before you start, not after you've shipped something incomplete. Your PR descriptions explain what changed and why. - Self-directed. You don't wait to be told what to do next. You pick up the next ticket, you flag blockers early, and you ship. What we're NOT looking for: - Frontend-only or backend-only specialists - Developers who need daily standups or heavy management to stay on track - Anyone who treats AI tools as "cheating" or hasn't integrated them into their workflow To apply: Skip the generic cover letter. Instead, tell us: - A brief description of a recent project where you worked across both backend (Rails or similar) and frontend (React/Next.js) - Which AI coding tools you use daily and how they fit into your workflow - Your availability and timezone We'll send a short paid screening task to promising candidates before scheduling an interview.

duration: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: We are seeking a skilled Full Stack Developer to join and a take a lead in the team management. This will start as a part-time engagement of 30 hours per week, with the opportunity to increase with strong performance. If you are passionate about coding and have a keen eye for detail, we would love to hear from you!

title: Full Stack Developer Needed — Waitlist Web App (Next.js + Supabase)
duration: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: Hi, I'm building the waitlist landing page and user system for XXXX — a fintech app that lets workers access their earned pay before payday. No loans, no interest, no waiting. I need an experienced full stack developer to build the complete waitlist system from scratch. This includes user signups, a coin-based rewards system, referral tracking, automated emails and SMS, user dashboards, and an admin panel. --- 📋 SCOPE OF WORK I will provide a full, detailed Build Scope & Handoff Document that covers every feature with step-by-step instructions, diagrams, database schemas, API specs, email templates, and a prioritised build order. You will not need to figure out what to build — everything is documented. Your job is to execute it cleanly and professionally. The system includes 17 features: 1. Waitlist Onboarding Flow — email signup, auto coin credit, magic link dashboard access 2. Referral Share Hub — copy link, WhatsApp, Instagram, Email, SMS sharing 3. Re-engagement Nudge Email — automated email after 7 days of inactivity 4. Profile Completion Prompt — name + phone collection for bonus coins 5. Waitlist Dashboard Home — coins, rank, check-in, referral link 6. Dashboard with Profile Reminder — persistent prompt for users who skipped profile 7. Admin Command Center — full user table, filters, search, export CSV 8. Mass Email + SMS System — segmented campaigns with smart variables 9. Quick Check-In Gate — landing page check-in button handling new and existing users 10. Leaderboard — collapsible dropdown showing top earners in real time 11. Referral Confirmation Email — automated notification when a referral converts 12. Coin Activity Log — full transaction history per user 13. Magic Link Expiry Handler — clean UX for expired dashboard links 14. Unsubscribe / Email Preferences Page — CAN-SPAM and GDPR compliant 15. OG Social Share Meta Tags — branded link previews for WhatsApp, iMessage, etc. 16. Rate Limiting & Abuse Prevention — protect coin credits and API routes 17. Launch Notification System — one-time email + SMS blast to full waitlist at launch Estimated total: 70–90 hours --- 🛠️ REQUIRED TECH STACK — YOU MUST KNOW ALL OF THESE Please do not apply if you cannot demonstrate experience with every item below. I will ask for examples or a GitHub link. • Next.js — full stack app with API routes (not just frontend) • React — mobile-first, component-based UI • Supabase — auth (magic links / passwordless), PostgreSQL database, row level security, edge functions • PostgreSQL — relational schema design, queries, joins • Tailwind CSS — utility-first styling, responsive and mobile-first layouts • Resend or SendGrid or Postmark — transactional email delivery • Twilio — programmatic SMS sending at scale • Vercel — deployment, environment variables, serverless functions • TypeScript — strongly preferred over plain JavaScript --- ⭐ BONUS SKILLS (nice to have — mention in your application) • Supabase Edge Functions or similar serverless/cron job experience • React Email — building HTML email templates in code • Upstash or Redis — rate limiting on API routes • Zod — input validation • Experience building referral systems or points/rewards systems • Experience building passwordless / magic link authentication flows --- 📁 WHAT I NEED TO SEE FROM YOU To be considered, your application must include: 1. A link to a live project OR GitHub repo where you built a user authentication flow (magic links, passwordless, or similar) 2. A link to a live project OR GitHub repo where you built any kind of rewards, points, referral, or gamification system 3. Confirmation that you have used Supabase in a production project 4. Confirmation that you have sent transactional email and/or SMS programmatically 5. Your honest estimate of how long this project will take you Applications without examples will not be reviewed. --- 💬 ABOUT WORKING WITH ME I am a non-technical founder. I will provide you with a complete, professional Build Scope & Handoff Document that leaves very little ambiguity. You will have clear instructions, feature diagrams, database schemas, copy, and a build order before you write a single line of code. I am looking for someone who: • Communicates clearly and proactively • Asks good questions early rather than delivering surprises late • Takes ownership of their work and flags issues before they become problems • Can work independently with a detailed spec --- 💰 PROJECT DETAILS Project type: Fixed price or hourly (open to discussion) Estimated hours: 70–90 hrs (2 weeks maximum) Timeline: Looking to start as soon as the right person is found Communication: Weekly check-ins + async updates via Upwork messages --- If this sounds like a fit, introduce yourself, tell me which part of this project excites you most, and send your examples. Looking forward to building something great together. — XXXX
