description: We are filling a full time position Do not email us We're Borradh, an Irish SaaS company replacing marketing agencies for aesthetic and beauty clinics. One platform that handles Meta advertising, an AI-powered lead follow-up chatbot called Claire, and booking automation. Clinic owners pay us instead of an agency and get better results at a fraction of the cost. 40+ paying customers live on the platform across Ireland and the UK. Just raised $110k in pre-seed funding. Currently targeting a $1.6M seed round for August 2026. US market expansion launching this month. This is not an idea or an MVP. It's a live product with real paying customers and real revenue. Where we're heading next: This is the exciting part. We're building out Claire into a much more advanced AI system that handles the entire lead journey from first message to booked appointment. We're developing an in-app video editor so clinic owners can create ad content directly inside the platform without needing any external tools. We're building push notification infrastructure, deeper integrations with major booking systems like Fresha, Phorest, and Pabau, a completely redesigned client dashboard, and scaling the whole thing on AWS to handle 500+ clinics globally. The product roadmap is aggressive and the work is meaningful. Every feature you build goes live to real customers immediately. What you'll work on: Claire AI chatbot enhancements including multi-language support, US tone adaptation, and conversation analytics. In-app video editor for ad creative production. In-app client communication system replacing WhatsApp. Push notification infrastructure. Booking system integrations. Client-facing dashboard improvements. Meta Graph API integration improvements including token management, error handling, and account diagnostics. Mobile app features via React Native. AWS infrastructure scaling. Tech stack: Frontend: React (NextJS) Backend: Express (NestJS) Mobile: React Native (Expo) Cloud: AWS APIs: Meta Graph API, Cal.com, various booking system APIs Must have: 3+ years full-stack development experience Strong React and NextJS proficiency NestJS or Express backend experience AWS experience (deployment, scaling) Third-party API integration experience Strong written English Self-directed — you ship without being micromanaged Nice to have: Meta Graph API or Messenger Platform experience React Native (Expo) n8n or similar workflow automation tools LLM/AI integration experience Previous startup experience Multi-tenant SaaS experience Details: Remote (Brazil preferred) Contract, full-time $1,000 to $1,200 USD/month (approximately R$5,700 to R$6,800/month) Working directly with the CTO every single day You're not joining a massive team where your work disappears. There are 2 founders and you. You will have direct input on architecture decisions, product direction, and how things get built. This is a ground-floor opportunity at a company that is actively raising its next round and scaling internationally. ⚠️ DO NOT APPLY WITHOUT THE FOLLOWING — applications without these will be ignored: 1. A short video (2 to 3 minutes max) of yourself on camera explaining one project you have built. Walk us through what it does, what technology you used, what your role was, and one technical challenge you solved. Screen share is fine but we need to see your face and hear you explain it in your own words. Upload to YouTube (unlisted), Loom, or Google Drive and include the link. 2. Your GitHub profile link. We want to see real shipped code, not tutorial repos. If your best work is in private repos, explain what you built and your contribution in a few sentences. 3. One or two links to live projects you have worked on that are relevant to this role. SaaS platforms, API integrations, dashboard-heavy apps, anything that shows you can build and ship production software. If you send a generic cover letter without these three things, we will not respond. We're a small team hiring carefully and we need to see that you can communicate clearly and build real things.

title: Senior Full-Stack TypeScript Engineer for Web Data Platform MVP
description: Title: Senior Full-Stack TypeScript Engineer for Web Data Platform MVP Description: We are building an MVP of a web data access platform that provides a single UI and orchestration layer on top of three existing APIs: - Scraper API - SERP API - AI Search API Important: the external APIs already exist. We are NOT looking for someone to build a scraping engine from scratch. We need a strong senior engineer who can build the product/platform layer on top of those APIs. This role is for one person who can take ownership of the MVP end-to-end: frontend, backend, integrations, run orchestration, normalization, storage, and usage tracking. Core product scope: - User authentication - Secure API key storage and validation - Create and run jobs manually from the UI - Support batch runs (multiple URLs/queries in one run) - Run lifecycle management: created / queued / running / processing / completed / partial_completed / failed / cancelled - Retry and cancellation - Integrations with existing external APIs - Normalize different API responses into one internal schema - Generate result outputs in JSON / CSV / Markdown / HTML - Store results and make them downloadable in the UI - Real-time status updates - Usage/request tracking Suggested stack: - Frontend: Next.js, React, TypeScript - Backend: Node.js, TypeScript - DB: PostgreSQL - Cache / queue: Redis, BullMQ or similar - Storage: S3-compatible storage - Docker for local/dev setup What we need from you: - Build the MVP end-to-end - Make pragmatic technical decisions - Keep the architecture clean enough for future expansion - Be comfortable working with external API integrations and non-trivial backend flows - Communicate clearly and move fast Required experience: - Strong commercial experience with TypeScript - Strong experience with Next.js / React - Strong backend experience with Node.js - Experience with PostgreSQL and Redis - Experience with queues, retries, async job execution - Experience designing APIs and backend workflows - Experience with S3 or similar object storage - Experience shipping real products, not only prototypes Nice to have: - Experience with workflow/orchestration systems - Experience with SSE / WebSockets - Experience with API metering / billing / usage tracking - Experience with scraping, SERP, or search-related products - Experience with Docker and production deployments Please apply with: 1. A short note on similar systems you built 2. Your exact role in those projects 3. Stack used 4. Links to relevant GitHub / portfolio / case studies 5. Your availability 6. Expected hourly rate Screening questions: 1. Have you built an integration-heavy product with async jobs, retries, and multiple external APIs? 2. Have you built both the frontend and backend of a production product yourself? 3. What would you use for queue/orchestration in this MVP and why? 4. How would you design the run lifecycle and make retries safe/idempotent?

title: Title: Full-Stack Developer to Deploy Suite of Small Web Apps (Cloudflare / Vercel / Supabase)
description: **Title:** Full-Stack Developer to Deploy Suite of Small Web Apps (Cloudflare / Vercel / Supabase) **Project overview** I run a growing digital products business in the cat behavior and bonding niche. I’ve been using AI builders to create a suite of internal tools and customer-facing tools, and now I need a developer to **properly deploy and harden** them so they’re reliable enough to sell and scale. Most tools are already built as browser-based apps; I’ve done light deployments (e.g., on Cloudflare Pages), but I want a more robust, well-documented setup. **Tools to deploy (initial batch)** These are separate but related apps: 1. **Bonding Companion Tool** – interactive implementation helper for a cat bonding program 2. **Blog Content Manager Tool** – internal editorial planner and content operations tool 3. **First-Party Analytics Tool** – internal analytics + attribution app for blog → CTA → checkout funnels 4. **Cat Care Tool** – decision-support / care-planning tool for cat owners 5. **Troubleshooting Tool** – structured diagnostic/triage assistant for common behavior problems 6. **Routine Planner Tool** – schedule/routine builder for daily cat care & bonding 7. **Cat Body Language Decoder** – reference/lookup tool for interpreting cat body language 8. **Cat Personality Test** – quiz-style assessment with result profiles and recommendations I’m not asking you to design the products from scratch; they already exist in working or near-working form. The main job is getting them into a **production-ready, maintainable deployment**. **What I need from you** - Review the existing codebases/exports for each tool - Recommend a **sane, unified deployment approach** (e.g., Cloudflare Workers/Pages + D1, or Vercel + Supabase/Postgres, or similar) - Set up environments (dev/stage/prod as appropriate) - Configure databases / durable storage for tools that need persistence - Implement secure environment variable handling for any secrets or API keys - Set up build pipelines and deployment scripts (light CI/CD is a plus) - Add basic auth/guardrails where needed (these are paid products / internal tools, not public toys) - Ensure solid error handling, logging, and basic monitoring - Document: - how each app is deployed - how to update/redeploy - where data lives and how to back it up - Optionally help with: - domain / subdomain mapping and SSL - basic performance and security hardening **Nice-to-haves (not required on day one)** - Experience with Cloudflare (Pages, Workers, D1, KV) - Experience with Vercel + Supabase/Postgres - Familiarity with first-party tracking and event-based analytics - Comfort working with AI-generated code and tightening it up where needed **About you** - Solid full-stack web development background (JS/TS preferred) - Comfortable reading and improving code you didn’t write - Experience deploying small apps for **real users** (not just prototypes) - Strong at documentation and explaining your decisions in plain English - Respectful of constraints (these tools must stay simple enough that a small team and VA can operate them) **Deliverables** For this first engagement, success looks like: - At least **3–4 of the tools** fully deployed in a production-ready way (we can prioritize which ones together) - Clear written deployment docs for each app - A short architecture note explaining: - chosen hosting and database stack - how the tools could be extended in future (especially the analytics tool) If we work well together, there’s ongoing work to: - Deploy the remaining tools - Iterate on features - Improve performance and reliability as usage grows **How to apply** Please include: 1. A brief summary of your experience deploying small web apps or internal tools. 2. Which hosting stack(s) you’d recommend here and why. 3. 1–2 examples (links or short descriptions) of apps you’ve deployed for clients. 4. Your rough estimate of hours or a ballpark fixed price for getting the first 3–4 tools deployed.

title: Full Stack developer Next.js React Node.js TypeScript AI-development Web Application AWS

title: Full stack development

title: Full-Stack Dev: Finalize CRM App (Next.js + Supabase + Outlook API)
description: We are a boutique M&A advisory firm that has built a custom internal deal pipeline CRM using V0 (Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Supabase). The core app is live and functional at a Vercel URL. We need an experienced developer to take it the last mile — primarily getting the Microsoft Outlook email integration working end-to-end and ensuring the app is production-ready. This is a well-documented project. We will provide the full spec doc, component inventory, state/store spec, and V0 source code access. What's Already Built Live Next.js app deployed on Vercel Supabase backend with full data model (engagements, categories, companies, contacts, activity log) Full pipeline table UI with 3-level hierarchy (category, company, contact) 9-stage status system with advance buttons, inline edit, CSV import/export Microsoft OAuth flow (partially working — authenticates successfully, tokens stored in Supabase) Webhook and manual sync endpoints scaffolded but not fully functional What We Need You to Complete Outlook email sync (primary task) — The Microsoft Graph API OAuth flow connects and stores tokens successfully, but the mailbox read is returning empty results (likely an AppRiver email security layer blocking Graph API access — a support ticket is in progress). Once unblocked, we need you to ensure the sync works correctly: outbound emails auto-increment contact email count and log activity, inbound replies auto-advance contact status to "Responded." Multi-user auth — Add simple email-based login (magic link via Supabase Auth) so 2-3 team members can each log in and share the same live data. No complex permissions needed — all users see everything. Production hardening — Ensure all Supabase RLS policies are correctly configured, environment variables are clean, and the app is stable for daily use by a small team. Minor UI fixes — A small list of known issues to clean up (we'll provide a full list on kickoff). Tech Stack Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Realtime) Microsoft Graph API / Azure AD Vercel (deployment) What We'll Provide Full written spec (data model, component inventory, state/store spec) V0 project access (source code) Vercel project access Supabase project access Azure AD app credentials Access to debug endpoint showing current OAuth/Graph API state Ideal Candidate Strong Next.js and Supabase experience Prior experience with Microsoft Graph API / OAuth 2.0 Comfortable working from an existing codebase rather than starting from scratch Available for a quick video call to review the codebase before starting Scope and Timeline Fixed-price project preferred. Estimated 10-20 hours of work depending on the AppRiver resolution timeline. Looking to complete within 2 weeks of hire. To Apply Please share examples of previous work involving Microsoft Graph API integration or Supabase Auth, and confirm you are comfortable working within a V0-generated Next.js codebase.

You must be able to clearly explain technical decisions, defend your solutions, and communicate fluently with the client in English. • Ability to work autonomously and take ownership Nice to have • Experience with Kafka, RabbitMQ or similar • Experience in regulated environments (finance, health, gov) • Experience integrating AI/ML features ⸻ Working conditions • Full-time (8 hours/day, Monday to Friday) • Flexible schedule, with availability for early meetings (Europe time) • Long-term engagement (multi-year project) • Equipment provided • Final interview with end client (Europe) ⸻ Compensation • Budget: $25 – $32 USD/hour

title: Project Title: Full-Stack Development for VERTEX – Premium Fitness Discovery & Performance WebApp
description: Project Title: Full-Stack Development for VERTEX – Premium Fitness Discovery & Performance WebApp 1. Project Overview VERTEX is a bespoke, digital-first personal training and wellness community. The platform serves two primary stakeholders: Wellness Professionals (Trainers) and Clients. The goal is to provide a seamless "Uber-style" discovery experience for trainers in Dubai, integrated with a high-performance "Live Logger" for real-time workout tracking and wearable data integration. 2. Technical Requirements Platform: Responsive WebApp (Mobile-first design). Frontend: React.js, Next.js, or Vue.js (High-performance UI/UX is critical). Backend: Node.js, Python, or Go.Database: PostgreSQL or MongoDB. Key Integrations: * Payments: Stripe, Apple Pay, GPay, and Tabby (BNPL). Wearables: Integration with Apple HealthKit, Garmin, Oura, Whoop, and Google Fit APIs. Auth: 2FA via SMS/WhatsApp (Twilio or similar). Maps: Mapbox or Google Maps API for custom district-based discovery. 3. Key Feature Modules 3.1. High-End Brand Experience (The "Splash") Animation: A sub-5 second landing animation. A gunmetal VERTEX logo lands in sand, displacing it kinetically. As the sand settles, the steely logo remains visible. Developer Note: Looking for Lottie, WebGL, or high-end CSS animation expertise. 3.2. Dual-Journey Stakeholder PortalsThe Switch: A landing screen with a dual-tab toggle: [PT/Wellness Professional] vs. [Client]. 3.3. Trainer Path (Professional Side)Onboarding: Multi-step registration including CV and Qualification uploads (PDF/Images).Verification Loop: Admin review trigger (submissions sent to VERTEX management).Professional Profile: * 30-second Video Intro (In-app recording/uploading).Core Competency tagging (Strength, Combat, Mind/Body, etc.).Dynamic Fee Setting (Slider: AED 270 – 1,000/hr).Live Logger: Real-time data entry during sessions (Sets, Reps, Weight, Rest).Client Management: Dashboard to view client profiles, injury history, and performance trends. 3.4. Client Path (User Side)Registration: 2FA verification and mandatory financial registration (Credit Card/Tabby/Wallets). The Discovery Engine (Find Your PT): Geolocation & District Map: Custom interactive map of 18 Dubai Districts. Filtering: Multi-criteria search by Goal, Competency, Gender, and Budget. Booking: Integrated calendar showing real-time trainer availability. Performance Dashboard: * Progress tracking via graphs (Volume, Weight, RHR).Photo Vault (Front/Side profile comparisons).Wearable Data Sync. 4. The "Live Logger" LogicA critical component of VERTEX is the real-time interaction during a workout.Start/Stop Functionality: The trainer initiates the session, which starts a timer visible to both.Dynamic Templates: Trainers can load VERTEX-provided templates (e.g., "Strength Training - Chest") and edit them on the fly.Automated Metrics: The app must calculate Total Volume Load using the formula:$$Total Volume = \sum (Sets \times Reps \times Weight)$$Session Summary: An automated training summary is generated post-workout, integrating wearable data (Heart Rate, Calories) with the lifting data. 5. User Interface (UI) Requirements Theme: Premium, Dark Mode, Industrial (Gunmetal, Steel, High-Contrast). Feedback Loops: Incomplete registration boxes remain red; successful submissions in Green. Interactive Elements: Carousel galleries for trainer profiles, scrolling wheels for budget/fees, and toggle sliders for notifications. 6. Scope of Work & Deliverables Discovery & Wireframing: Validating the sitemap and user flow. Database Architecture: Designing a scalable system for high-volume performance data. Frontend Development: Building the animated splash and dual-portal UI. Backend & API Integration: Setting up 2FA, Wearable APIs, and Payment Gateways. Admin Dashboard: A basic internal tool for VERTEX management to approve/reject Trainer applications. UAT & Launch: Beta testing with a core group of trainers and clients. 7. Proposal RequirementsWhen quoting, please provide: Relevant experience with fitness or marketplace webapps. Examples of high-end UI animations (WebGL/Lottie). Your proposed tech stack and why. An estimated timeline for an MVP (Minimum Viable Product).

We work remotely, so all team members must be available when needed to collaborate during 8a-5p Pacific Time (PT). Job Expectations: - We’re looking for a Full-Stack Software Engineer. As with all engineers at Grux, you’ll be cross-trained across our two core projects, Grux and Rook, and work closely with product, design, and engineering teammates. - This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building cloud-native systems from the ground up, values clean architecture, and is comfortable owning features end-to-end. What You’ll Do 1. Design, develop, test, and deploy cloud-native applications 2. Take ownership of backend systems primarily written in Golang, while collaborating on frontend work using Flutter 3. Work across both Grux and Rook, gaining familiarity with all parts of the platform 4. Collaborate with product, UX/UI, and other engineers to ship high-quality features 5. Architect, build, and maintain scalable, reliable, and secure systems on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) 6. Develop and maintain codebases in Golang, Dart, and JavaScript 7. Implement best practices for code quality, testing, CI/CD, and deployments 8. (not just LangChain) What We Provide: Detailed technical specification document (ready now) Existing open-source codebases to fork/build from Working prototype code from our personal trading bots title: Full-Stack Python Engineer for Warehouse Management System (Shopify, Postgres, Shipping API)
description: We’re a fast-growing smart appliance selling direct-to-consumer via Shopify Plus and through retail partners. We need an experienced Python/FastAPI developer to build a custom Warehouse Management System (WMS) from scratch. The Ideal Candidate - Strong experience building production FastAPI applications - Experience with warehouse/logistics/3PL systems - Comfortable with Postgres schema design and migrations - Uses modern AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Claude Code) as part of their daily workflow to move faster and produce higher-quality code - Experience integrating with Shopify APIs and webhooks - Has worked with shipping/carrier APIs - Experience consuming and producing XML APIs - Can build a functional, usable web UI - Good communicator, comfortable working async with a small technical and operations team Deliverables A web-based system that manages our end-to-end order fulfilment pipeline including: - Stock Management: Track inventory levels across multiple warehouses in Postgres. Support receiving, adjustments, and real-time stock updates both manually and via excel import. - Shopify Order Ingestion: Receive and process orders from Shopify via webhooks/API. - Warehouse Routing: Automatically route each order to the optimal warehouse based on stock availability and location. - Carrier Selection: Determine the optimal shipping method for each order. - Label Generation: Generate shipping labels programmatically via carrier APIs. - Warehouse Dispatch: Send confirmed, labelled orders to the warehouse for fulfilment via their XML-based API. - Web UI: A clean, functional dashboard to view and manage products, view and release orders, update stock levels, and view shipment status. The Teck Stack - Python / FastAPI - PostgreSQL - Shopify API & Webhooks - XML API integration (warehouse) - Temporal workflows - Frontend framework of your choice (React, Vue, etc)

In addition to the fixed price, a performance-based bonus will be provided based on: On-time milestone delivery Code quality, scalability, and maintainability Measurable performance improvements (Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse, SEO) Top performers can expect meaningful upside beyond the base project cost. To Apply (Important) Please include: Examples of WordPress → Next.js (Versel or similar) migrations Your proposed tech stack (CMS, architecture, etc.) Your approach to SEO preservation during migration Your availability Start your proposal with the word “Migration” so we know you’ve read this carefully.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer – Multi-Tenant SaaS Website Builder (Next.js + Plasmic)
description: ​We are seeking a high-caliber Senior Full-Stack Developer to architect the core infrastructure for a niche/vertical, proprietary website builder and performance platform designed for world-class tattoo artists. We are moving away from white-label platforms to a custom "Headless" solution that offers 100% brand ownership and elite performance.​The Role: You will build a multi-tenant Next.js application that serves as a "shell" for a white-labeled website building experience. You will work closely with our Head of Creative to turn high-fidelity Figma designs into a functional, scalable SaaS.​Key Technical Requirements:​Multi-Tenancy Architecture: Implement a centralized Next.js (App Router) application that serves custom content across dynamic subdomains and user-connected custom domains.​White-Labeled Plasmic Integration: Embed the Plasmic Studio (Content Creator Mode) into our custom dashboard. Users must be able to edit their sites within our UI without ever seeing third-party branding.​Stripe Billing Engine: Implement a 14-day free trial (credit card upfront) using Stripe Billing. Logic must include:​Automatic renewal at Day 15.​Immediate "Site Suspension" via webhooks if payment is declined.​Domain Automation: Integrate Entri or Approximated.app to allow users to connect their own custom domains with one-click DNS configuration.​Figma-to-Code Pipeline: Support our design team in syncing complex, animated Figma components into Plasmic and ensuring they are production-ready. Component Library & Slot Requirements:​Build a Centralized Component Registry where our Head of Creative can add new Plasmic components that automatically appear in the user’s editor sidebar.​Implement "Slot-based" architecture to allow users to drag and drop pre-approved sections into a blank canvas while maintaining the global design system (typography, spacing, brand tokens).​Ensure "Blank Project" provisioning is available, allowing users to start with a zero-state page and build using our proprietary Block Library.​Technical Stack:​Frontend: Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS.​Backend: PostgreSQL (Supabase or Prisma), Node.js.​Editor: Plasmic (Headless/Embedded mode).​Payments: Stripe Connect / Billing.​Infrastructure: DigitalOcean or Hetzner.​Requirements:​Proven experience building Multi-tenant SaaS platforms from scratch.​Deep expertise in Stripe Webhooks (idempotency, signature verification, and subscription lifecycles).​Experience with Plasmic or similar headless visual builders is highly preferred.​Ability to work under a strict NDA.

description: I'm building a premium facial analysis platform — think Qoves Studio, but faster, cheaper, and more complete. Users upload 6 angled photos + complete a short quiz, and receive a deep facial harmony report with personalized non-surgical protocols, hairstyle morphs, and skincare/aesthetic recommendations. The product is called ProtoMorph (or MorphGlow — TBD). The goal: launch an MVP within 90 days, acquire first 500 users at an intro price, and iterate from there. This is a high-margin, subscription-first business. What You'll Build Landing page — premium design, before/after examples, 60-second explainer video embed, Stripe checkout User onboarding flow — guided 6-photo uploader (front, 45°, side, smile, etc.) + 2-minute intake quiz (age, ethnicity, goals, lifestyle) Admin/ops dashboard — internal queue for the human review team to polish AI-generated reports before delivery User dashboard — private per-user portal with PDF download, interactive before/after morph sliders, biometric score cards, checklist-style protocol, and hair section with shopping links Email drip integration — post-signup nurture sequence ("tips while you wait") + delivery notification + annual renewal reminder AI pipeline integration — connect to facial analysis API (e.g. Face++ or similar) + GPT-4 for report generation. I'll provide the prompt architecture; you implement the pipeline Payments & subscriptions — Stripe: $129/yr base, $49 expedited add-on, $49 annual refresh, $99 intro price coupon logic "Share your morph" viral loop — watermarked share card generator Tech Preferences (Open to Discussion) Next.js / React Node.js or Python backend PostgreSQL or Supabase Stripe OpenAI API / GPT-4 Facial analysis API AWS S3 or Cloudflare R2 SendGrid / Postmark Vercel deployment Ideal Candidate Has built full-stack SaaS products before — you know what "production-ready" means Comfortable integrating AI/ML APIs and building async pipelines (photo → analysis → report delivery) Strong UI/UX sensibility — the product needs to feel premium and modern, not like a template Can take ownership end-to-end and communicate clearly in English Available for a 30-minute intro call before project start Bonus: experience with image morphing/manipulation (face warp, style transfer) or computer vision Deliverables & Milestones Milestone 1 — Landing page + Stripe checkout + photo upload flow (25%) Milestone 2 — AI report generation pipeline + admin queue dashboard (35%) Milestone 3 — User dashboard with morphs, PDF, protocol checklist (25%) Milestone 4 — Email drip, viral share card, QA, deployment (15%) How to Apply Please include in your proposal: 2–3 examples of SaaS products you've shipped (links preferred) Your recommended tech stack and a brief rationale Rough timeline breakdown across the 4 milestones Your fixed-price bid One sentence on how you'd handle the AI report generation pipeline (photo in → report out) Generic proposals will not be considered. Show me you've read this carefully.

title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer (Local-First App, Private AI/Ranking Engine)
description: Job Description Build a Private Intelligence Console (Local Mac App) We are building a data-driven intelligence platform (think “Bloomberg for health intelligence”) that ranks practitioners, centers, and approaches based on real signals—not marketing. We are looking for a senior full-stack / backend-focused engineer to build a founder-only Owner Console that runs locally on a MacBook Pro. 🎯 Project Goal Build a local-first application that: runs entirely on my MacBook Pro (localhost) connects to Supabase to read signal data executes proprietary ranking calculations locally allows me to preview and publish ranking results writes only final outputs back to Supabase The core ranking logic must remain local and private at all times. This is not a typical web app. This is a secure operator system + private intelligence engine. 🧠 What You’ll Build (V1) A local owner console with: 1. Owner Dashboard runs on localhost (browser-based or equivalent) clean, fast UI (not design-heavy, but precise) 2. Ranking Engine Integration local execution of scoring / ranking logic structured, modular code (future-proof) 3. Preview & Publish Workflow “Run Preview” → compute rankings locally inspect results (tables, ranking changes, score breakdowns) “Publish” → write approved results to Supabase 4. Configuration Layer editable scoring parameters (weights, profiles, etc.) stored locally (not in Supabase) 5. Logs & Reliability execution logs error handling ability to re-run and debug 🔐 Core Architecture Principles Supabase = signal warehouse + output store only All ranking logic = local on my machine No proprietary logic stored in Supabase No public exposure of the console Clean separation between: UI (console) local engine data layer 🧩 Tech Stack (preferred) Next.js TypeScript Node.js Supabase Local-first architecture Python is a plus (for data processing), but not required. 👤 Ideal Candidate You are: a senior full-stack or backend-leaning engineer experienced with internal tools / admin systems comfortable building local-first or offline-capable apps strong in system design and clean architecture able to build something practical, not over-engineered Bonus if you have worked on: analytics or ranking systems scoring models / decision engines founder/operator tools data-heavy applications ⚠️ Important This is not a generic frontend/dashboard project. We are building: a private intelligence system with a founder control interface. Architecture and security thinking matter more than visual design. 📦 Deliverables Local architecture plan Working prototype on Mac Full Owner Console (V1) Ranking execution flow Supabase integration Documentation for setup and usage 🧭 Project Structure We will likely work in two phases: Phase 1 architecture + prototype Phase 2 full V1 build 🤝 Working Style direct communication fast iteration cycles focus on usable output first (not perfection) clarity over complexity 🚀 Why This Is Interesting You are not building another CRUD app. You are building the core intelligence layer of a new platform, including: ranking logic decision systems private data processing founder control interface This will evolve into a much larger system over time. 👉 To Apply Please include: Example of a system or tool you built that involved data processing or internal dashboards How you would structure a local-first app that connects to a remote database (like Supabase) Your preferred stack for this project and why 1. Local-First Architecture (Core Question) How would you design a local-first application that runs on a MacBook (localhost), reads data from Supabase, executes proprietary ranking logic locally, and writes results back—while ensuring the ranking logic cannot be reconstructed from Supabase or the frontend? Screening Questions: 1. Please describe: architecture (components) data flow where logic lives what you would explicitly avoid 2. Security & Moat Protection What are the biggest risks that could lead to someone reverse-engineering or copying the ranking algorithm in this system—and how would you mitigate them in your design? 3. Execution Flow & Usability Describe how you would implement the “Preview → Inspect → Publish” workflow for ranking runs in a local console. How would you structure the system so results can be safely reviewed before being written to Supabase? To apply, please start your response with the word “Clockbusters” (exact spelling) so I know you’ve read this carefully and can follow instructions.

title: Full-Stack Developer — Internal App Launcher (React + FastAPI)
description: An internal web-based app launcher — think Adobe Creative Cloud — that unifies all firm applications under a single Google SSO login. Users log in once and see a personalized tile grid of their assigned apps. The project is self-contained: you are building both the React frontend and the FastAPI/Python backend for this launcher, on our existing DigitalOcean server (Ubuntu 24.04, Nginx, PostgreSQL already running). This is not an integration project — you are not connecting to or modifying any existing backend services. The stack is React + Vite + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui on the frontend, FastAPI + PostgreSQL on the backend, and Clerk for Google SSO. You will build three things: (1) the launcher UI — a dynamic, branded tile grid where each user sees only their assigned apps; (2) user and app management — a backend with full CRUD for users, groups, app tiles, and visibility assignments, plus an admin panel in the frontend where admins manage all of this; and (3) silent Actionstep (and other SSO-enabled apps) authentication — when a user clicks the Actionstep tile or other app, they land inside Actionstep or or other app already logged in, via a token-based OAuth redirect flow (no second login prompt). All milestones are contracted and paid individually. To apply, please include a link or screenshot of a similar full-stack internal tool, dashboard, or admin panel you have built, and a short paragraph explaining how you would implement the Actionstep silent authentication (token-based redirect after first-time OAuth authorization). Proposals that do not address this question will not be considered. Strong preference for Top Rated developers with verified React and Python/FastAPI experience. Full technical brief provided before contract.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

title: AI-Native Full Stack Developer (Claude / AI Agents / SaaS Builder)
description: We’re not looking for a normal developer. We’re building a high-performance AI product factory — launching SaaS tools, mobile apps, and automation systems at speed. If you don’t actively use AI tools like Claude, AI coding agents, and automation workflows to 10x your output, this role is not for you. ⚡ What You’ll Be Doing - Build and ship SaaS products, mobile apps, and internal tools - Use AI tools (Claude, AI coding agents, etc.) to move fast and build smarter - Design and implement scalable architectures (you choose the stack) - Build AI-powered systems (chatbots, automations, workflows like n8n) - Debug issues fast and independently (no hand-holding) - Turn rough ideas into production-ready products 🧠 What We’re Looking For You are NOT just a coder. You are a builder + thinker + problem solver. Must-Have: - Strong experience in full stack development - Able to architect systems, not just write code - Deep understanding of debugging and problem solving - Comfortable choosing the best stack/tool for the job AI-Native (VERY IMPORTANT): - You actively use tools like: - Claude / AI coding agents - AI-assisted workflows - Strong in prompt engineering - Experience building AI agents / automations / integrations Bonus (Highly Preferred): - Experience with SaaS products - Experience with mobile apps - Familiar with tools like n8n / automation systems - Good UI/UX sense (you know what looks clean and converts) 🔥 What We Offer - Full-time, long-term opportunity - Work on multiple real products (not boring maintenance work) - Fast-moving environment (we build, test, iterate quickly) 💬 How to Apply (IMPORTANT) To filter serious applicants, include the following: - What AI tools do you use daily in your workflow? - Share 2–3 projects you’ve built (especially using AI) - Describe a time you used AI to speed up development significantly - If I give you a SaaS idea today, how would you approach building it? Applications without these will be ignored. 🎯 Final Note We are building fast. We need someone who can think, build, and execute at a high level using AI. If that’s you — apply.

title: Full Stack Developer for SaaS Platform Development

The ideal candidate is a reliable SaaS developer who can work across both frontend and backend, create clean architecture and deliver high-quality solutions for modern SaaS platforms. This role is a great fit for a Full stack Developer React Node with hands-on experience in building responsive web applications, developing APIs, integrating databases and supporting long-term product growth. You should be comfortable working on both user-facing interfaces and server-side logic, with a solid understanding of performance, maintainability, and product-focused Software Development. Core technologies and skills: Node.js React JavaScript TypeScript HTML5 CSS 3 Vue.js Bootstrap HTML Redux WordPress Software Development Python jQuery Responsive Design Web Design PHP MongoDB CSS Git What we are looking for: experience building SaaS products and web platforms strong frontend and backend development skills ability to work with modern JavaScript frameworks and scalable backend systems confidence in creating responsive, user-friendly, and maintainable applications practical knowledge of database-driven development, version control, and cross-functional delivery Project focus: The main goal is to support SaaS product development with a strong balance of frontend quality and backend reliability. We are looking for a developer who can contribute as a Full stack developer SaaS, think like a SaaS developer and deliver results with the mindset of a Full stack developer React Node.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (Long-Term, High Responsibility, SaaS Platform)
description: We are looking for a highly experienced **senior full-stack developer** to take ownership of an existing, production-critical SaaS platform. This is **not a short-term freelance task**. We are looking for a long-term collaboration with a developer who can grow into a **key technical owner of the platform over the coming years**. --- ### About the Project You will be working on a mature SaaS platform that has been developed over the past 10+ years and is currently used in production. The system is business-critical and requires: * high stability * structured development * strong understanding of existing architecture * ability to take over responsibility from a single original developer --- ### The First 6 Months (Critical Phase) The onboarding will be structured as follows: **Months 1–3** * Work closely with the current developer * Gain deep understanding of architecture, data flows, and integrations * Begin documenting the system (supported by AI tools) * Start handling smaller tasks and fixes **Months 4–6** * Gradually take over responsibility for development and maintenance * Continue documentation and knowledge consolidation * Become operationally independent --- ### Important Context * The current developer will be available until the end of June for onboarding and knowledge transfer * You are expected to **actively use AI tools** (e.g. for code analysis, documentation, and system understanding) * The goal is to reduce dependency on a single developer and build a sustainable long-term setup --- ### Tech Stack (Must Be Comfortable With) Please only apply if you have strong experience with most of the following: **Client / Presentation Layer** * Nuxt 3 * Vue 3 * FlutterFlow **Backend / Application Layer** * PHP 8.3 * Yii2 * Node.js * Puppeteer **API & Integrations** * OAuth2 * WooCommerce (custom plugins / API) **Search** * Elasticsearch **Data Layer** * MySQL Aurora 8.0 **Infrastructure / Cloud** * AWS (CloudFront, S3, AMI, environments) **Security** * AWS WAF * GuardDuty * VPN setups **DevOps / Operations** * Multiple environments (dev/demo/prod) * Snapshots, backups, recovery **External Integrations** * QuickPay (Nets/Clearhaus) * Shipmondo (GLS) --- ### What We Are Looking For We are specifically looking for someone who: * Has **proven experience taking over existing codebases** * Can work **independently and take ownership** * Is comfortable working with **legacy + modern hybrid systems** * Has strong **problem-solving and debugging skills** * Has experience with **complex integrations and production systems** * Is structured in their work and communication --- ### Requirements * Senior-level experience (this is not a junior or mid-level role) * Strong track record on Upwork or similar platforms * Ability to work within **European / Danish working hours (or close to)** * Good written and spoken English * Availability for **long-term collaboration** * Please address your application to "Jensk" to confirm that you have read the job description; applications that do not include this will not be considered --- ### Important: How to Apply To be considered, please include: 1. A short description of **similar systems you have taken over** 2. Your experience with **PHP (Yii2), Node.js, and AWS** 3. How you typically approach **understanding a new codebase** 4. Which **AI tools** you use in your workflow (and how) 5. Your availability (hours per week + timezone) Applications without relevant experience will not be considered. --- ### Final Note This is an opportunity to take ownership of a real, production platform and become a long-term technical partner. We are looking for **quality, stability, and commitment** — not just execution. --- Best regards Jens

title: Develop custom, full stack rate based software for hotel group
description: Looking for a software developer to build a full stack custom tool for setting hotel rates for a hotel group with API integration to our property management system. Nice to have would be scalable product. Backend to handle data and pricing logic needed. Frontend dashboard (web-based). Multi-tenant architecture so each hotel customer is isolated. Pricing logic and rules are ready to go. Candidate in Arizona (Tucson) preferred.

title: AI Inbound Sales AI Agent Specialist (11x + Hubspot + Full Stack Setup)
description: We are building a lean, AI-powered outbound sales engine designed to generate qualified meetings with high-end hospitality clients from day one. At the core of this system is a seamless integration between 11x and Pipedrive, allowing us to automate lead engagement, qualification, and follow-up while maintaining a premium, human-feeling experience. The objective is to ensure that every inbound or outbound interaction is handled instantly, intelligently, and with clear progression toward a booked meeting. This system will be fully connected to a new, conversion-focused website that acts as both a credibility layer and a lead capture tool. All enquiries, whether through forms or direct outreach, will flow directly into Pipedrive, triggering automated workflows via 11x to respond, qualify, and route leads. The entire journey—from first touchpoint to booked call—will be structured, trackable, and optimised, giving us full visibility on performance while reducing manual effort. Ultimately, we are not just setting up outreach campaigns, but building a scalable revenue infrastructure. The goal is to create a system that runs continuously in the background, generating consistent opportunities while allowing the founder to focus purely on closing and strategic growth. Over time, this engine should evolve into a predictable and repeatable pipeline, capable of supporting rapid expansion into new regions and verticals.

title: Full-Stack Developer (Strapi + Nuxt) for Ongoing Website Support & Enhancements
duration: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: We’re looking for a reliable, experienced full-stack developer to support and maintain our existing website built on a modern headless architecture. This is not a build-from-scratch project; we need someone who can confidently step into an existing environment, troubleshoot issues, and implement enhancements across both backend and frontend. The first priority project is working with the SEO team to help improve some elements such as site speed and load speed. Tech Stack: Strapi v4.10.5 (Node.js backend) Nuxt.js (Vue-based frontend) PostgreSQL database (hosted on Railway) Railway (hosting & infrastructure) GitHub (version control) Cloudinary (media storage) Cloudflare (CDN & security) Responsibilities: Maintain and extend Strapi CMS (content types, APIs, configurations) Support and enhance the Nuxt frontend (UI updates, bug fixes, performance improvements) Troubleshoot issues across the full stack (frontend, backend, database, hosting) Manage deployments and environments in Railway Collaborate via GitHub (branches, pull requests, code reviews) Optimize site performance, stability, and scalability CI/CD & Development Workflow We have an established CI/CD and deployment workflow that must be followed. The ideal candidate should be comfortable working within structured development processes. Code is managed via GitHub using a branch-based workflow (feature branches, pull requests, reviews) Deployments are handled through Railway (auto-deploy and/or environment-based workflows) Changes should be properly tested before merging and deployment Experience working with staging and production environments is preferred Ability to troubleshoot deployment issues and environment configuration is important We maintain separate staging and production environments We prioritize clean, well-documented code and maintainable architecture Ideal Candidate: Strong experience with Strapi (v4 preferred) or similar headless CMS Proficient in Nuxt.js / Vue.js Comfortable working in full-stack environments (Node.js + frontend frameworks) Experience with PostgreSQL and cloud hosting platforms (Railway or similar) Solid understanding of Git-based workflows Able to debug independently and communicate clearly Project Details: Ongoing contract (not a one-time task) Flexible hours, but responsive & timely communication is important We’re looking for someone who can become a go-to partner for continuous improvements Please include: Examples of projects using Strapi and/or Nuxt A brief description of your experience with similar stacks Your availability and bandwidth: timezone and how quickly you can jump in to help support Start your proposal with: “Headless Pro” so we know you’ve read this.

title: Senior Full Stack TypeScript Developer (Node.js/React) for Custom E-commerce RMA/Return System
duration: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
description: We have a fully functional, modern Return Management System (RMA) built locally that works perfectly with all API integrations. The system is already running smoothly on your machine with all connections active. Now we need an experienced Full Stack TypeScript Developer to take ownership of the project, get the entire mono-repo live, handle any minor bugs that appear during deployment, perform final end-to-end testing, and then continue maintaining and expanding the system according to our roadmap. The system acts as a highly automated bridge between our Shopify store and nShift/Unifaun logistics, handling everything from customer return labels and email confirmations to advanced analytics and hardware batch tracking. Current status: Everything works 100% locally (Shopify webhooks, return label generation, Resend emails, Prisma + SQLite, full admin dashboard). We now need it deployed and stable in production (Render or similar PaaS). After successful live deployment + final testing we will continue with new features on an ongoing basis. Tech Stack (What you'll be working with): Frontend: React (Vite), modern hooks, high-performance structure, styled-components / Tailwind-inspired CSS Backend: Node.js + Express.js Database & ORM: SQLite via Prisma (easy migration to PostgreSQL if wanted) Language: 100% strict TypeScript (full mono-repo) Key Integrations (all already working locally): Shopify Admin API (products, orders, webhooks) nShift / Unifaun REST API (return shipments & labels) Resend API (transactional emails)

title: Senior Full-Stack Mercenary (React Native / Node.js)
description: Project Overview: We are building the "Digital Black Card" for Dubai's top 1% (UHNWIs, elite influencers, top-tier hosts). Think Raya meets Soho House. This is a strictly invite-only, ultra-luxury lifestyle OS. Our CTO is moving at lightspeed, and we need an elite, "mercenary-style" Senior Full-Stack Developer to join the trenches and ship our V2 (Minimum Lovable Product). The Stack: Frontend: React Native (iOS & Android) Backend: Node.js Language: TypeScript (Strict) Real-time: WebSockets / Socket.io (Crucial for our real-time 72-hour RSVP rooms and micro-interactions). What We Expect From You: Zero Hand-Holding: We don't have time for 2-week onboardings or micromanagement. We hand you a world-class Figma file and a razor-sharp logic flow; you deliver a pixel-perfect, buttery-smooth feature. Formula 1 Execution: You understand high-end UI/UX and micro-animations. The app must feel like a Tom Ford suit, not a cheap SaaS product. Architectural Mindset: You write clean, scalable, and bulletproof code that doesn't crash when 1,000 elite users are active simultaneously. Do NOT apply if: You need a Product Manager to spell out every single button for you, or if you can't work independently. If you are a top-tier killer who wants to build the most exclusive lifestyle app in the Middle East - apply now. Let's build an empire.

title: Senior Full Stack Developer (MVP) Scalable Agricultural Platform
description: We are building a production-grade platform with a clear path to scale designed to organize agricultural supply and demand through verified data, controlled workflows, and scalable system architecture. This is a product build, not a prototype or experiment. We are looking for a high-quality full stack developer to help implement a clean, scalable MVP. MVP Scope (Clearly Defined) The system includes the following core modules: - User authentication (Farmer / Buyer / Admin roles) - Farm registry (structured farm and production data) - Demand request system (buyers submit structured demand) - Rule-based matching engine (supply ↔ demand) - Response tracking system (farmer responses to demand) - Admin dashboard (system oversight and reporting) Technical Direction (Important) We are intentionally building: - Modular monolithic architecture - Clean REST API structure - Well-structured PostgreSQL database We are intentionally avoiding: Microservices AI integrations Over-engineering Complex infrastructure Your Responsibilities - Implement backend and frontend features - Follow defined architecture and standards - Build clean, maintainable, production-quality code - Work in structured weekly deliverables - Collaborate via GitHub (pull request workflow) Tech Stack Backend: Node.js (Express or NestJS) Frontend: React / Next.js Database: PostgreSQL Engagement Model Remote, part-time Weekly deliverables and reviews Task-based workflow (ClickUp) Requirements Strong experience with Node.js and React Experience building SaaS or platform-based systems Strong understanding of database design Experience designing and working with APIs Ability to work independently with clear structure To Apply (Required) Please answer the following: - How would you design the simplest scalable architecture for this MVP? - What would you intentionally NOT build in the first version? - Share a similar system you have built (brief explanation) This platform will be presented to institutional and government stakeholders, so code quality and structure are critical.

title: Full-Stack Engineer Needed for Interview Copilot Reliability Fix
description: We’re hiring a Full-Stack Engineer to build and improve our Interview Copilot. Current stack (MERN) We use Deepgram for real-time transcription and need someone who can implement reliable two-way audio capture and stream it properly for live transcription. Core requirement (must-have) The product must capture two-way audio: Interviewer audio = audio coming from the device / meeting tab / system audio User audio = audio going into the device (microphone) Both must be captured at the same time and transcribed live via Deepgram. Preferred experience Prior work with Deepgram streaming (WebSocket / live transcription) Browser-based audio capture experience (WebRTC / MediaStream / getUserMedia / tab capture) Handling Chrome/Edge reliability + Mac constraints (system audio limitations, tab-only capture approaches, etc.) Mandatory to be considered Please share a Loom video with a live demo of an Interview Copilot (or similar app) you’ve built showing: interviewer audio captured + transcribed user mic captured + transcribed both simultaneously (two-way) No Loom demo of two-way audio capture = not considered. Reply with Your approach to capturing tab/system audio + mic How you stream to Deepgram (single mixed stream vs dual streams) Supported browsers/OS

title: Full-Stack Developer Needed to Build Financial Dashboard (WordPress + React)
description: I run a financial education and investing platform and am building a members-only dashboard that delivers market insights, rankings, and trading signals. This is not a typical website project. We are building a clean, fast, app-like dashboard experience for members, similar to a simplified version of: • TradingView • Bloomberg (lite) • Seeking Alpha dashboards I already have: • A live and long established WordPress site • A membership system in place • A clear wireframe and feature plan I need a developer who can help bring this to life in a clean, scalable way. ⸻ Project Scope (Phase 1) We will start with a focused build, not a massive platform. Core Deliverables: • Create a dashboard-style UI with: • Left sidebar navigation • Top data bar (market indicators) • Main dashboard layout • Build key components: • ETF Leaderboard (sortable/filterable table) • Smartscore stock rankings table • Market overview panels • “New signals” card section • Integrate: • TradingView (or similar) chart embeds • Basic external data sources (API or structured input) • Ensure: • Fast load times • Clean UX (desktop first, mobile responsive) • Simple, scalable structure ⸻ Tech Stack (Preferred, but flexible) • Frontend: React (Next.js preferred) • Backend: Node / API-based (or lightweight alternative) • WordPress: remains as main site + authentication layer Open to suggestions if you have strong reasoning. ⸻ What I’m Looking For You should be: • Strong in React / modern frontend development • Comfortable working with WordPress environments • Experienced building dashboards or data-driven interfaces • Able to think in terms of UX, not just code • Practical and solution-oriented (not overengineering) ⸻ What I’m NOT Looking For • Agencies with layers of project managers • Developers who overcomplicate simple things • “Yes to everything” freelancers who lack opinion • People who disappear mid-project ⸻ Important: This is a phased build This project will expand over time. Future phases may include: • Alerts system • User watchlists • Personalization • Mobile optimization I am looking for someone I can potentially work with long-term. ⸻ To Apply Please include: 1. Examples of dashboards or data-heavy interfaces you’ve built 2. Your preferred stack for this type of project (and why) 3. A brief explanation of how you would approach this build 4. Your hourly rate or project estimate ⸻ Small Test (Required) To make sure we’re aligned, I will ask shortlisted candidates to: • Sketch (or describe) how they would structure: • A dashboard page with a sidebar + data table • OR review my wireframe and suggest improvements ⸻ Budget Open based on experience and approach. Looking for quality and clarity over the lowest price. ⸻ Final Note This project is about building something useful and used daily, not just something that looks good. If you enjoy building clean, data-driven tools that people rely on, this will be a great fit.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (Web + Mobile, AI + SaaS) for Lead Generation Platform
description: We’re building a simple but powerful platform that helps local businesses capture and manage leads more efficiently using AI — across web and mobile. We’re looking for a strong full-stack developer who can do more than just execute tasks. We want someone who can **think, challenge ideas, and help shape the product**. 🚀 Core Features (MVP) * AI-powered intake form (collects project details from users) * Lead capture + storage (database + admin view) * Simple dashboard to manage leads * Mobile-friendly experience (or companion app) * Basic AI integration to structure or summarize inputs 🧠 What We Need * Build a clean, fast web + mobile experience * Integrate AI (OpenAI or similar) * Help simplify features and prioritize what matters * Suggest better ways to build, structure, and scale the product * Think beyond code — contribute to product direction 🛠️ Preferred Stack * Web: React / Next.js * Mobile: React Native or Flutter (open to recommendations) * Backend: Node.js or Python * Database: PostgreSQL / Supabase / Firebase 💡 Ideal Candidate * Has built SaaS products or real-world applications * Strong product mindset (can challenge ideas and improve them) * Comfortable working with AI APIs * Communicates clearly and proactively * Enjoys solving problems, not just completing tickets 🤝 Engagement * Start with MVP (paid) * Strong potential for ongoing work if it’s a good fit 🔥 To Apply Please include: 1. Examples of products you’ve built (web or mobile) 2. Your approach to building an MVP like this 3. One suggestion or idea you would improve in this concept Looking for someone who takes ownership, thinks independently, and helps build the right product — not just the requested one.

title: ZOHO full stack developer
duration: More than 6 months, Hours to be determined
description: We love those who work with us. We are loyal to people who choose to work with us. We've been in business for 45+ years now helping people breathe better without big pharma getting in the way. We have been on zoho one for about 7 years. We took a break from zoho 18 months ago thinking we could find something better but we were wrong. We are looking for someone who is always pushing to know the latest zoho has to offer and wants to share that with the customers you help like us. We are looking for a person who is not afraid of making mistakes along the way as you help us customize zoho We want someone who is about using the latest tactics of AI as well to get more done in less time. We look forward to meeting you and starting a relationship. OUR NEEDS: we want to start fresh and you do a new clean install of zoho. title: Full stack developer for fashion marketplace. Web app + mobile app
description: We are looking for an experienced full stack developer or development team to build a fashion marketplace platform similar in structure and user experience to Grailed or Depop. The project includes three parts: 1. A browser-based marketplace platform 2. A mobile app for iOS and Android 3. A full admin backend / CMS platform for internal operations and platform management The full UI/UX designs are already completed, so this is a build-focused project. We are looking for a strong technical partner who can take approved designs and turn them into a clean, scalable, high quality product across web, mobile, and admin. This marketplace will allow users to create accounts, list items for sale, browse and search products, message each other, make offers, complete purchases, and manage their profiles and listings. On the backend side, the platform should also include a robust admin system to manage users, listings, orders, content, reports, categories, and operational workflows. Project Scope Marketplace Web Platform • Frontend build based on completed designs • User authentication and account management • Buyer and seller profile flows • Listing creation and item management • Product detail pages • Search, filters, saved items, and category browsing • Messaging/chat between users • Offer system • Checkout and payment integration • Order tracking and account management • Notifications Mobile App • Native or cross-platform mobile app for iOS and Android • Buyer and seller functionality aligned with the web platform • Messaging, notifications, listing management, offers, and purchase flow • Smooth and intuitive marketplace UX Admin Backend / CMS • Admin login and permissions • Dashboard overview for platform activity • User management • Seller management and verification workflows • Listing moderation and approval tools • Order and transaction management • Category and attribute management • Content management for banners, homepage sections, editorial or static pages • Reports, flags, disputes, and support tools • Notification management • Analytics and operational visibility • Ability to expand the admin panel over time as the business grows What We Already Have • Complete UI/UX designs • Clear product direction • References and feature inspiration from Grailed, Depop, and similar marketplace platforms What We’re Looking For • Strong experience building marketplace platforms or transactional apps • Ability to build web, mobile, and admin systems • Strong backend and database architecture • Clean frontend implementation with attention to design detail • Experience with chat, offers, payments, and marketplace logic • Good communication and organized project execution • Ability to recommend the right tech stack • Long term scalability mindset Ideal Candidate You have already built or contributed to a marketplace, social commerce platform, resale platform, or similar multi-sided product. You understand buyer and seller journeys, listing systems, chat, offers, checkout, moderation flows, and internal admin operations. You can work from completed design files and deliver production-ready builds. To Apply Please send: • Relevant marketplace or commerce products you have built • Whether you are applying as an individual or a team • Your recommended tech stack for web, mobile, and admin backend • Estimated timeline • Estimated budget range • Your role in the projects you share • Examples of any admin dashboards, CMS systems, or internal tools you have built We are looking for someone serious who can build this properly, not just a basic MVP. Quality, scalability, clean execution, and long term thinking are very important. If you have experience building platforms similar to Grailed, Depop, Vinted, or other marketplace products, please mention that clearly in your proposal.

title: Full-stack developer to build a form-driven report builder with .docx export
description: We're a medical packaging testing lab looking for a full-stack developer to build an internal web application that replaces a manual Word document workflow. What the app does: Staff currently fill in ~30 Microsoft Word templates by hand to produce regulatory-grade test reports. The app should let a report writer select which tests were performed, fill in the results through structured forms, and export a complete, properly formatted .docx report ready for manager review. Core features: - Dashboard for managing multiple reports simultaneously - 30+ form templates, each with a mix of editable fields and locked boilerplate text - Reusable equipment database (LSO ID, manufacturer, model, calibration date) - Auto-populated summary tables (logistics, results and conclusions) - .docx export that preserves all table formatting - Simple user authentication The suggested stack is React, Node.js or Python, and PostgreSQL hosted on Azure, but we are fully open to alternative approaches based on your experience. What matters is that the solution is maintainable and the .docx output matches our existing report format. You should apply if you have experience building form-heavy web apps with structured data, prior work with .docx generation (docxtemplater or python-docx preferred), and the ability to recommend and configure cloud hosting. Fixed-price engagement. Please include your approach to .docx generation and a relevant prior project in your proposal.

title: Full-Stack Developer Needed — Convert AI MVP to Production Beta (React + Backend + AI Integration)
description: ABOUT THE PROJECT We're a GCC-based startup building an AI-powered B2B platform for the MENA+EU market. We have a polished React frontend prototype that's deployed and live — full dashboard with multiple views, data visualizations, and user flows. The UI is production-quality. The problem: everything runs on hardcoded demo data. No backend, no database, no real user accounts, no AI integration. We need a developer to add the backend layer so we can onboard our first 3–4 real users into a closed beta. A detailed developer brief (8 pages) with full specs, database schema, API endpoints, and success criteria will be shared with shortlisted candidates. SCOPE (5 FEATURES) 1. Authentication & User Accounts • Real signup/login (email + password) • Two user roles with different dashboards • Session management, protected routes • Supabase Auth, Firebase Auth, or Auth0 — your choice for speed 2. User Onboarding & Profile System • Multi-step intake form (10–15 fields) that saves to database • Profile edit and completeness indicator • Different profile structures for each user role 3. Seeded Data Import • We provide a CSV of 30–50 records for one side of the platform • You build the schema and import pipeline • Admin CRUD for managing this dataset 4. AI-Powered Matching Engine (Core Feature) • Take user profile data → process against seeded database via Claude API or OpenAI • Produce ranked results with compatibility scores and natural-language explanations • Calculate a composite readiness score based on profile inputs • Results persist in DB and display on dashboard 5. Connect Existing Dashboard to Real Data • 2 of 7 dashboard pages connected to live data from backend • Remaining pages stay as demo — labeled "coming soon" • Navigation and layout unchanged TECH STACK • Frontend: React (Vite) — already built, you're adding backend • Backend: Node.js/Express or Supabase (serverless) — your call • Database: PostgreSQL (Supabase) or Firestore • AI: Claude API (Anthropic) or OpenAI — we provide API keys • Hosting: Currently on Replit, open to Vercel/Railway if you recommend it WHAT WE PROVIDE • Full access to existing React codebase • Live deployed prototype you can explore immediately • Detailed 8-page developer brief with schema, endpoints, and success criteria • Seed data CSV • AI API keys • Responsive, technical founder — fast to unblock you --- PAYMENT (MILESTONE-BASED) • Milestone 1 (30%): Auth + database + onboarding form functional • Milestone 2 (40%): AI matching engine + dashboard integration • Milestone 3 (30%): Beta launch with real users + 1 week bug fixes IDEAL CANDIDATE • Strong full-stack experience (React + Node/Supabase/Firebase) • Has integrated AI APIs (OpenAI, Claude, etc.) into production apps • Can move fast — 3-week sprint, not a 3-month project • Comfortable working inside an existing React codebase • Experience with auth systems and role-based access • Bonus: Built marketplace or matching/recommendation platforms TO APPLY Please include: 1. A brief overview of a similar project (AI integration + auth + dashboard) 2. Your recommended backend stack and why 3. Rough timeline estimate for the 5 features 4. Your fixed-price quote 5. Availability to start this week Full spec document shared with shortlisted candidates only. Serious inquiries only — we're moving fast.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for AI-Powered Platform (Launch + Production Support)
description: We are looking for a senior full-stack developer to help take an AI powered platform from its current build stage through production readiness, launch, and post-launch optimization. The platform is already underway and includes core user flows. We need someone who can step in like a true product-minded engineer — not just code features, but help stabilize, harden, and operationalize the platform for real users. This is not a blank-sheet build. We need someone comfortable auditing an existing codebase, identifying weak points, implementing clean fixes, and helping us launch with confidence. We are looking for a developer who can help with: - Production stabilization pass across the full app - Fixing reliability, data flow, and edge-case issues - Improving security and access control - Tightening payment / package / entitlement logic - Ensuring product specific features work correctly - Improving admin visibility and internal tooling - Preparing the platform for launch Post-launch support: - Bug fixes - Performance improvements - UX refinements - Analytics / tracking improvements - Feature enhancements - General technical partner support as the business grows The current platform was built on Lovable on a modern stack such as: - Next.js - Supabase / Postgres - Stripe - AI / API-based generation workflows - Modern frontend component architecture You do not need to be tied to a specific stack from the start, but you should be very comfortable stepping into an existing full-stack web app and working across frontend, backend, database, auth, payments, and integrations. Responsibilities: - Audit the current codebase and identify production risks - Fix bugs and unstable user flows - Harden authentication, authorization, and feature gating - Improve package / pricing / checkout logic - Ensure correct handling of product entitlements - Review database structure and improve data integrity where needed - Improve logging, error handling, and operational reliability - Help prepare the product for production deployment - Support launch and post-launch iterations - Recommend pragmatic technical improvements without over-engineering Must-Have Experience: - Strong experience as a senior full-stack engineer - Experience shipping and supporting production web apps - Strong frontend + backend troubleshooting ability - Experience with authentication, authorization, and role-based access - Experience with payments / Stripe / webhook handling - Experience with database design, migrations, and data integrity - Experience working in an existing codebase built by others - Ability to independently diagnose root causes, not just patch symptoms - Strong communication and ability to explain what is broken, what was fixed, and what still needs attention Nice to Have: - Experience with Supabase - Experience with Next.js App Router - Experience with AI-powered product workflows - Experience building or improving admin dashboards - Experience with feature gating / entitlements / tiered packages - Startup or product-launch experience - Experience improving app readiness from beta to production

title: Full-Stack Developer (React + TypeScript + Supabase + Crypto) — SaaS Dashboard + Integrations
description: Tech Stack: React TypeScript 5 Supabase (PostgreSQL) JWT Authentication HikerAPI integration Stripe NowPayments for Crypto Payments Overview We’re hiring a Full-Stack Developer to build and improve our SaaS dashboard. This role is for someone who can write clean, maintainable code, work with Supabase/Postgres, implement secure auth flows, and integrate payment providers + external APIs. We are strict about code quality. We do not want rushed or copy-paste work. Scope: Initial Milestone: Your first task will be to complete the QA findings provided by our QA tester. We work on a milestone basis only. If you’re interested, apply and we’ll review the QA list with you in a meeting. This milestone is estimated at ~12 hours, with a fixed budget of $120 USD (approx. $10 USD/hour). Please only apply if you’re comfortable with this rate and milestone structure. Just a quick note on how we structure work: instead of bundling the full ~60-hour QA estimate into one milestone, we break it into smaller milestones. This keeps things clear, fair, and easier to manage on both sides. After each milestone, we’ll do a quick review with our technical auditor, then move on to the next batch. It also gives you a smooth way to ramp up with the codebase, and keeps expectations aligned as we go. Also, once the project is launched and everything is stable, you’ll have the first opportunity to step into the role as our long-term/full-time Full-Stack Developer. That would include offering an ongoing maintenance package (bug fixes, updates, reliability) and being the main developer responsible for building new features requested by our users and internal team. At the end of each week, your work will be reviewed. If your performance meets our quality standards and is verified by our senior auditor, you will receive a $30 weekly bonus. Eligible for profit-sharing after launch based on performance and continued involvement Responsibilities Build and maintain features in our React + TypeScript SaaS dashboard Work with Supabase/Postgres (schema, queries, RLS if needed) Implement and maintain JWT authentication flows (secure access patterns) Integrate and maintain external APIs (HikerAPI) Implement payments (Stripe + NowPayments) including webhooks and subscription logic Fix bugs, optimize performance, and improve code structure over time Write clear PR descriptions and follow our workflow (PR reviews required) What We Expect (Non-Negotiable) You must write clean, readable, maintainable code You must be able to explain your code and decisions clearly (no “AI-only” work) You must use PRs for all changes You must include steps-to-test in every PR You must accept code review feedback and make revisions as needed You must communicate consistently (daily updates / async updates) Deliverables / Workflow Work is delivered through GitHub PRs Each PR must include: What was done + why Steps to test Any edge cases / notes Code must pass review before merge Required Experience Strong React + TypeScript experience (real projects) Supabase/Postgres experience (schema + querying; bonus if you know RLS) Experience implementing JWT auth securely Experience with payment integrations (Stripe required; NowPayments is a bonus) Experience integrating external APIs with rate limits + error handling Comfortable working in an existing codebase and improving quality Nice to Have Experience with subscription billing + webhook reliability Experience building admin dashboards / SaaS products Testing experience (basic unit/integration testing is a plus)

title: Full-Stack Developer Needed - Python, .NET(C#)
description: I’m looking for an experienced US-based developer to help diagnose and fix issues in a desktop application that’s currently running on my local machine.
