title: Full-Stack Developer Needed for Ongoing Projects (React / Node.js / Replit / GitHub)
duration: 1 to 3 months, Hours to be determined
description: I am looking for a dedicated, experienced full-stack developer to work with me across multiple web application projects on an ongoing basis. The projects span internal business tools, product configurators, and operational platforms — all built on the same core tech stack in Replit and managed through GitHub. This is not a one-off gig. I need someone who can become a reliable development partner over the coming months, working from detailed specification documents I provide. Each project has its own set of implementation prompts — structured Markdown briefs (typically 2,000–5,000 words each) covering database schemas, business logic, UI layouts, API integrations, edge cases, and testing requirements. Your role is to execute these specifications precisely, ask smart questions when something is ambiguous, and deliver tested and diagnosed, production-ready code. The Work Across the various projects, you will be building features that include: • Complex data models with relational databases (10–30+ table schemas with joins, aggregations, and calculated fields) • Role-based user systems with profiles, permissions, and activity tracking • Financial and costing logic (job costing, margin calculations, cost allocation engines) • Product configuration tools with real-time pricing • Document generation and delivery pipelines (PDF generation, email integration via Google APIs) • Dashboard reporting with data visualisation (charts, KPIs, filterable tables) • AI/LLM integration for classification, compliance checking, and intelligent automation • File storage and retrieval (S3-based) • Audit logging and immutable record-keeping You will not be designing the system architecture or making product decisions — that is handled on my end. You will be translating well-defined specs into working software with a keen eye for workability and diagnostics How We Work • Spec-driven: You receive detailed written specifications for every feature. No guesswork. • Asynchronous communication: We work across timezones. You receive clear briefs, you ask clarifying questions upfront, you deliver working code with a handover note. • Replit environment: All development happens in Replit. You must be comfortable with Replit's workspace, deployment, and Agent tooling. • GitHub version control: Feature branches, pull requests, clean commit messages. No committing directly to main. • Testing is mandatory: Every feature ships with Vitest tests covering core logic and edge cases. Untested code will be sent back. • Australian context: All projects use AUD currency, metric measurements, and operate in AEST/AEDT timezone. You do not need to be in Australia, but you need to understand these conventions. What I Am NOT Looking For • Developers who skip reading specs and start building based on assumptions • Developers who deliver code without tests • AI-generated portfolio projects with no real deployment or user base • Generic proposals that do not reference the specific tech stack listed below • Someone who needs hand-holding on basic React/Node patterns Required Skills Category Specifics.. Frontend - React 19 (hooks, context, state management), Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui components, responsive design Backend - Node.js, Express, tRPC 11 (procedures, middleware, auth context) Database - Drizzle ORM with MySQL/TiDB, schema-first migrations, relational queries Development Environment - Replit (workspace management, deployment, Agent — must have demonstrable experience) Version Control - GitHub (feature branches, pull requests, code review, clean history) Testing - Vitest (unit and integration tests as standard practice) APIs - Google OAuth 2.0, Gmail API, REST integrations & XERO Styling - Tailwind CSS 4 utility-first approach, dark/light theming, mobile-first responsive Preferred (Not Required) • Experience with manufacturing, ERP, or job-costing business systems • S3 file storage integration • AI/LLM integration in production applications (server-side calls, structured responses) • Chart.js or D3.js for data visualisation • PDF generation pipelines • Experience working from detailed written specifications rather than verbal briefs Deliverables (Per Feature) For each specification prompt you receive: 1. Working implementation deployed and functional in the Replit environment 2. Database migrations pushed and verified 3. Vitest test suite covering core business logic, validation, and edge cases 4. GitHub pull request with clean, descriptive commits and a PR summary 5. Please reply with Replit Build in your response to prove your thoroughness. 6. Handover note — what works, what needs attention, any deviations from the spec and the reasoning behind them

title: Full Stack Developer for Video Streaming Platform (Clips + Live + Events)
description: MasLifeWorld TV is a mobile-first web platform focused on Caribbean Carnival culture. The platform will feature short-form video clips, live streaming, replay content, and event-based organization. The goal is to build a scalable, secure, and modular system that allows full owner control and can later be extended into a mobile app without rebuilding the backend. Core Requirements: 1. Frontend (Website – Mobile First) - Responsive design optimized for mobile devices - Homepage with: - Live section (if active) - Vertical scrolling clip feed - Featured events - Replay section - Navigation: Home, Live, Events, Replays, Profile 2. Backend (API-First Architecture) - Must be API-driven to support future mobile app (iOS/Android) - Clean separation between frontend and backend - All data and functionality accessible via API 3. Events System (Scalable) - Ability to create unlimited events (e.g., Trinidad Carnival 2026, St. Thomas Carnival 2026) - Each event contains: - Clips - Live streams - Replays - Events must be fully manageable from admin dashboard 4. Video System (Modular) - Use Mux or similar provider for video and live streaming - System must be provider-agnostic (modular), allowing future switching without rebuilding - Support: - Clip playback - Live streaming - Automatic replay generation 5. Creator Upload System (Moderated) - Creators can upload videos - All uploads go into “Pending Review” - Admin approval required before publishing - No auto-publishing 6. Admin Dashboard (Full Control) - Upload and manage content - Approve/reject creator submissions - Create/edit events - Manage users and roles - Manage homepage content - Manage sponsor placements - Access logs, reports, and system status 7. Role-Based Access Control - Super Admin (full system control – owner only) - Admin (content management, no system ownership) - Editor (upload and organize content only) - Creator (upload only, no publishing) - Viewer (standard user) 8. User System - Email-based account creation - 18+ age confirmation required - Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acceptance required - System must log acceptance timestamps 9. Email Capture - Collect emails during signup - Optional email capture prompts (pop-up or CTA) - Store emails for future marketing integration 10. Safety & Content Moderation - All creator content must be reviewed before publishing - Report Content feature - Zero tolerance for prohibited content - Admin ability to remove content at any time 11. Copyright / DMCA System - Report form for copyright claims - Admin review queue - Ability to remove content and track repeat violations 12. Logging System (Audit Trail) - Track all key actions: uploads, approvals, edits, deletes, role changes - Each log includes: user, action, timestamp 13. Backups & Recovery - Daily database backups - Secure media storage - Restore capability 14. Infrastructure & Scalability - Must support thousands of videos and users - No hard-coded data (events, categories, etc.) - Use scalable cloud architecture - Optimized for performance and high traffic 15. Tech Stack (Preferred) - Frontend: Next.js - Backend: Node.js - Database: PostgreSQL - Auth: Supabase or equivalent - Video: Mux (or equivalent) - Storage: AWS S3 (or equivalent) - CDN/Security: Cloudflare Ownership & Control: - All accounts must be created and owned by the client (domain, hosting, database, video services, code repository, etc.) - Developer will be granted access but not ownership - Final system must not require developer involvement for daily operation Deliverables: - Fully functional web platform (MVP) - Admin dashboard - Creator upload and moderation system - Live + replay functionality - Documentation for system management Goal: Build a clean, scalable, and secure foundation for a long-term digital platform that can evolve into a full mobile application and media network.

title: Job Title on Upwork: "Full-Stack Integration Engineer – Lovable / Supabase / AI Automation"
description: Here's the full job description: --- **Full-Stack Integration Engineer – Lovable / Supabase / Claude AI** **About the Role** We're a growing digital health company building a suite of AI-powered wellness and clinical platforms. We work at the intersection of neuroscience, preventive medicine, and consumer health technology — and we move fast. We're looking for a hands-on full-stack integration engineer who can take ownership of building, operationalizing, and scaling our products from front-end to backend, with a strong command of modern AI-native development workflows. This is an ongoing part-time engagement with room to grow. You'll be working across multiple live projects simultaneously, so the ability to context-switch, prioritize, and maintain clean architecture across codebases is essential. --- **What You'll Be Doing** - Building and iterating on web applications using **Lovable.dev** as the primary development environment, paired with **Claude (Anthropic API)** for AI-native feature development - Taking Lovable-generated front-ends and making them fully production-ready — clean, scalable, and connected to robust backends - Architecting and managing **Supabase** infrastructure: database schema, authentication, row-level security, edge functions, and real-time features - Designing and implementing **HIPAA-compliant** data systems, including access controls, audit logging, encryption, and BAA-ready architecture - Integrating third-party APIs, payment systems (Stripe), and automation tools (n8n or equivalent) into cohesive product workflows - Building multi-step AI agent pipelines using the **Anthropic API**, including tool use, context management, and structured outputs - Setting up webhook architecture and event-driven workflows across systems - Collaborating closely with non-technical stakeholders — you'll need to translate product vision into working systems, not just execute tickets --- **What We're Looking For** - Demonstrated experience with **Lovable.dev** — this is non-negotiable. If you haven't shipped a project using Lovable, this role isn't the right fit - Strong **React + TypeScript** proficiency (Lovable's output stack) - Deep **Supabase** experience: not just basic queries, but RLS policies, edge functions, and production-grade schema design - Solid **PostgreSQL** fundamentals - Real experience integrating the **Anthropic API** (Claude) — tool use, multi-turn conversations, structured outputs, prompt engineering - Familiarity with **HIPAA technical safeguards** — you don't need to be a compliance officer, but you need to know what PHI is and how to architect around it - Experience with **Stripe** subscriptions, webhooks, and payment flow integration - Comfort with **n8n, Make.com**, or similar automation platforms for building cross-tool AI workflows - Strong communication skills — async-first, clear documentation, no hand-holding required --- **Nice to Have** - Experience building in health, wellness, or clinical tech environments - Familiarity with freemium SaaS product architecture - Experience with BAA vendor coordination (Supabase, AWS, etc.) - Prior work on consumer-facing AI features (not just backend API calls) --- **Engagement Details** - **Type:** Part-time, ongoing - **Hours:** Flexible, roughly 15–25 hrs/week depending on sprint priorities - **Rate:** Competitive — commensurate with demonstrated Lovable + Supabase + Claude experience - **Location:** Remote, global applicants welcome --- **To Apply** Please include in your proposal: 1. A specific example of a project where you connected a **Lovable front-end to a Supabase backend** and integrated an external API or automation tool — describe what you built, what broke, and how you fixed it 2. Your experience with the **Anthropic API** specifically (not just LLMs generally) 3. Any exposure to **HIPAA-compliant** system design *Applications without a response to these three points will not be reviewed.*

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (Next.js / Node) to Build MVP SaaS Tool
description: Please start your proposal with the word “VETERAN” so I know you read the full job description. I’m building a SaaS tool for U.S. veterans that helps analyze VA disability ratings and identify potential secondary claim opportunities. This project is an MVP build, not a long-term enterprise platform yet. The goal is to launch a working dashboard with core functionality and expand features later. The public marketing site already exists on Webflow. The software itself will live on a separate app subdomain (dashboard). This project includes building the authenticated dashboard application and backend logic. MVP Features to Build The MVP will include three core tools: 1. VA Disability Rating Calculator Users enter their current VA conditions and ratings. The system calculates: combined VA disability rating using the official VA math formula. 2. “Am I Underrated?” Audit Using user inputs, the system analyzes possible missed rating opportunities and outputs: estimated potential rating estimated monthly compensation difference 3. Secondary Condition Suggestions Based on a condition relationship dataset we provide, the system suggests possible secondary conditions. Example: PTSD → migraines PTSD → sleep apnea Back pain → radiculopathy This will be powered by a database lookup. Tech Stack (Preferred) Frontend Next.js React Tailwind CSS Backend Node.js Database PostgreSQL (Supabase preferred) Authentication Supabase Auth / Clerk / Auth0 Hosting Vercel preferred Open to suggestions if you have strong reasons. Architecture Current structure: Marketing Site getvetclaimiq.com (Webflow) SaaS App app.getvetclaimiq.com Backend API Node.js service Database PostgreSQL The Webflow site will simply route users to the app dashboard for signup/login. Core Deliverables Developer will build: • user authentication • dashboard UI • rating calculator logic • secondary condition engine • audit results page • database schema • API endpoints • deployment to hosting environment What I Will Provide I will provide: • condition relationship dataset • feature requirements • UI examples • hosting accounts • domain access Timeline Target MVP build time: 4–6 weeks. Budget Project budget: Depending on experience and approach. Requirements Please apply only if you have: • experience building SaaS dashboards • strong Next.js / React experience • backend API development experience • experience with authentication systems • experience deploying production apps To Apply Please include: Links to SaaS apps or dashboards you have built Your preferred stack for this project A short outline of how you would approach this architecture Applications without portfolio examples will not be considered.

title: Senior Magento 2 Developer (Full Stack) - Backend + Frontend | 8+ Years Required
description: Job Description We are a B2B e-commerce business operating a Magento 2 store with 3,900+ products across 33 attribute sets. We are currently executing a structured technical recovery programme and require a senior full-stack Magento 2 developer to join the team on an ongoing basis. This is a hands-on technical role. You will be working directly from a structured task list covering security hardening, navigation fixes, canonical tag remediation, URL rewrite management, schema implementation, and Varnish/Cloudflare performance configuration. Full task scope will be provided once you have been accepted. A full technical audit has been completed on the live production environment. All issues are diagnosed, documented, and ready for execution. You are not walking into an unknown situation you will have a complete picture of the stack, the problems, and the fix sequence from day one. If you are a senior developer who prefers structured, well-defined work over ambiguous discovery projects, this engagement is designed for you. The stack you must know: - Magento 2 (Open Source) / minimum 8 years, full stack - Snowdog Alpaca theme and Frontools (Gulp + Less compilation) - AWS EC2, RDS (MySQL), Docker Compose - Varnish 6.5 FPC, Redis, Elasticsearch 7.10 - Cloudflare WAF, cache rules, transform rules - Magento CLI, PHP PDO, EAV schema, URL rewrite table management - JSON-LD structured data (BreadcrumbList, Product schema) in Magento templates - GTM dataLayer implementation and debugging What we require from applicants: - Answer all five screening questions below / applications without written answers will not be reviewed - Proven Snowdog Alpaca experience or demonstrable hands-on familiarity - Strict staging-first discipline / no production changes without written approval The developer must have demonstrable, hands-on experience with every component of this stack. Do not apply if you require guidance on any item listed below. Platform Magento 2.4.3-p1 (Open Source) PHP 7.4 MySQL / RDS (MariaDB) Infrastructure: AWS EC2 ([aws-region]) RDS (production-restored) Docker (self-managed) Nginx 1.20 Varnish 6.5 Elasticsearch 7.10 Frontend: Snowdog Alpaca Theme Snowdog Frontools (Gulp + Less) RequireJS + jQuery CDN / Security: Cloudflare (WAF, CDN, Bot Management) Redis (cache + sessions) Docker Compose Extensions This is a senior full-stack Magento 2 role. The developer must be able to operate independently across both backend and frontend. Tasks span database queries via PHP bootstrap, Snowdog Alpaca theme overrides, Docker container management, and Magento CLI operations on staging3 before production. The work is structured with explicit change control. No production changes are made without written approval from the site owner. Backend Requirements •Magento 2 module architecture: di.xml, plugins (before/after/around), observers, service contracts •Direct database access via PHP PDO bootstrap (RDS MySQL, no CLI auth) •Magento CLI: setup:upgrade, di:compile, cache:flush, indexer:reindex •URL rewrite table management, EAV schema queries, core_config_data •Snowdog Megamenu (snowmenu_node) database-level configuration •Varnish VCL, Redis configuration, Elasticsearch health monitoring •Docker Compose: container management, log access, volume mounts •AWS EC2 SSH access, file path navigation, environment file handling •RDS connection management, slow query identification, index optimisation •Magento store view configuration, ghost store view removal, canonical tag management Frontend Requirements •Snowdog Alpaca child theme: override structure, inheritance, do not edit core Alpaca files •Less CSS compilation via Snowdog Frontools (Gulp-based, not standard static content deploy) •RequireJS module definition, data-mage-init pattern, jQuery no-conflict mode •Magento layout XML: referenceBlock, referenceContainer, block removal •Phtml template escaping: escapeHtml, escapeUrl, escapeJs •JSON-LD structured data output in Magento templates (BreadcrumbList, Product schema) •GTM dataLayer implementation: push events, debug in Preview mode •Core Web Vitals: LCP, CLS, INP identification and resolution in Magento context •Cloudflare interaction: cache bypass rules, purge after static content deploy Availability to overlap with Australian Eastern Time (AEST GMT+10/11) for approvals, Strong written English for technical briefing documents

title: Full-Stack Developer (Python/React) to Convert Working AppSheet Prototype into Multi-Tenant SaaS
description: I am the domain expert and founder of a legal support operations company. I have built a fully functional, highly complex prototype using AppSheet and Google Apps Script that automates email intake, process server dispatching, and complex invoice generation using AI. I am now looking to commercialize this internal tool into a multi-tenant B2B SaaS platform for other legal support firms. What You Will NOT Have to Do: You do NOT have to guess the business logic. You do NOT have to design the database architecture from scratch (the schema is mapped). You do NOT have to engineer the AI Prompts (the complex JSON output prompts, edge cases, and math chains of thought are already 100% written and tested). What You WILL Build: Backend: Python (FastAPI or Django) to handle heavy PDF manipulation and AI API routing. Frontend: React (Next.js) web dashboard for dispatchers/admins. Database: PostgreSQL with strict multi-tenant architecture (Row Level Security). Integrations: * Mailgun/SendGrid (for automated email ingestion and attachment stripping). Stripe Connect (for SaaS subscriptions and user payment processing). LLM API (Anthropic Claude or OpenAI) for data extraction. A robust PDF generation engine for dynamic Affidavits of Service and Invoices. Phase 2 (Future Scope): A React Native mobile app for process servers in the field (offline capabilities and GPS tracking). For Phase 1, the web app simply needs to be mobile-responsive. The Ideal Candidate: You must have deep experience building multi-tenant SaaS platforms from scratch. You must be highly proficient in Python for PDF/AI manipulation and React for clean UI/UX. Please include the phrase "Summons and Complaint" at the top of your proposal so I know you read this. In your proposal, please briefly outline how you would handle the email ingestion-to-AI pipeline.

description: (English Version) Hello, how are you? We are looking for a highly autonomous Full-Stack developer with an AI-first approach to help us finalize and launch two large SaaS products that are currently in an advanced stage, close to market release. This is not a typical corporate role. We are a small, fast-moving team focused on building real products for real users. We need someone versatile, resourceful, and comfortable working across multiple areas of development. If you enjoy fixing complex problems, closing unfinished parts, improving UX, handling edge cases, and shipping quickly, this role is for you. ⸻ 💻 About the product You will mainly work on Fantasktic, an all-in-one SaaS platform designed to replace scattered tools such as: • Monday.com • Asana • Notion • Trello • Spreadsheets and multiple disconnected apps Fantasktic centralizes project management, clients, documents, automations, and operations in a single platform. The goal is essentially to replace 15 different applications with one system. ⸻ 🧠 Tech stack It is important that you feel comfortable working with modern technologies: • Next.js • React • TypeScript • PostgreSQL • Redis • R2 / S3 (storage) ⸻ 🚀 What you will do You will act as a generalist developer focused on solving real problems. Typical tasks include: • Fixing bugs in both frontend and backend • Completing partially implemented features • Improving UX/UI where needed • Implementing new features • Reviewing architecture and proposing improvements • Ensuring stability, performance, and security • Adapting the platform for mobile usage (responsive) • Refactoring existing code or AI-generated code • Developing and deploying quickly through iterative cycles ⸻ 🤖 AI-First Workflow (REQUIRED) We work with a modern development workflow heavily supported by AI. You must: • Know how to use Claude Code (mandatory) • Use AI as a primary development tool • Maintain your own technical judgment • Manually review work before considering tasks complete • Be productive without constant supervision 👉 We provide Claude Code MAX accounts for the team. ⸻ ✅ Requirements • Solid experience as a Full-Stack developer in modern web applications • Previous experience building or maintaining SaaS products • Strong self-management skills • Problem-solving mindset • Good technical judgment • Fluent written English • Real availability for full-time work ⸻ ⭐ Nice to have (not mandatory) • Experience with project management tools such as Monday.com, Notion, Trello, Asana, etc. • Experience working in startups • Experience launching real products or MVPs • DevOps or security knowledge • Product and UX sensitivity ⸻ 💰 Compensation $1,500 USD / month — Full-time (8h/day) Long-term collaboration with future salary review if there is a good fit. This is an early-stage role with strong growth potential as the product scales. ⸻ 🌱 Why join • Work on an ambitious SaaS about to launch • Direct impact on the product • AI-native development environment • Professional growth alongside the project • Flexible remote work ⸻ 📩 To apply, please include 1. A short introduction 2. Links to relevant projects or repositories 3. Your availability to start ⸻ If you are a pragmatic, fast developer capable of turning complex systems into stable, production-ready products, we would love to talk. Best regards and thank you for your time, Arnau (Spanish Version) Hola, qué tal estás? Estamos buscando un/a desarrollador/a Full-Stack muy autónomo/a y con enfoque AI-first para ayudarnos a rematar y lanzar dos SaaS grandes que están en fase avanzada previa a salida al mercado. title: I need a professional SEO team with full-stack WordPress development capabilities.

I strictly require "White Hat" SEO strategies; furthermore, the bidding team must possess the capability to handle both full-stack WordPress development and ongoing maintenance. Specific requirements are as follows: 1. Analyze my website and products, and provide a comprehensive SEO strategy along with a list of keywords for my selection. 2. Produce a specific quantity of SEO-optimized articles on a weekly basis, and publish them on relevant websites with high Domain Authority (DA) scores. 3. Create and publish high-quality backlinks for my website every week. 4. Review SEO performance reports on a daily basis. 5. Following the daily review of SEO reports, resolve any issues requiring attention (e.g., 403/404 errors, broken backlinks, etc.). 6. Rectify all errors present on my website to ensure it operates in a healthy state and remains fully functional. 7. title: Full Stack Developer– Fix Real-Time Transcription in Interview Copilot (React / TypeScript / AWS)
description: We are seeking an experienced Audio Engineer to diagnose and resolve audio capture and transcription issues within our Interview Copilot product. This role requires deep expertise in audio signal processing, browser-based audio capture, and real-time transcription pipelines. You will be responsible for identifying the root cause of audio inconsistencies, optimizing input/output handling, and ensuring reliable two-way audio transcription in live interview scenarios. Responsibilities: Troubleshoot and fix audio capture issues across browsers (Chrome, Edge, etc.) Improve system audio + microphone input handling Optimize audio stream processing for real-time transcription Ensure compatibility with our React + TypeScript frontend Work within AWS infrastructure to improve transcription reliability Tech Stack: React TypeScript AWS Real-time transcription services (experience with WebRTC, MediaStream APIs, or similar preferred) Requirements: Strong background in audio engineering or audio systems development Experience with real-time audio streaming and transcription Ability to work independently and provide clear technical recommendations Strong debugging skills across browser environments Please reach out with: Relevant experience A short demo or Loom walkthrough of similar work Examples of previous audio or transcription-related projects We are looking for someone detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of delivering a stable, production-ready audio solution.

title: Lead Full Stack Developer Needed to Build Beta Version of a Membership Platform (End-to-End)
description: We are building the beta version of a new digital membership platform designed to test a specific interaction model and economic behavior within a small, controlled community. This beta platform will operate with a limited number of members (approximately 200 users) and includes a few core mechanics centered around time-limited content, member interaction that can extend content lifespan, and periodic paid sessions hosted by guest contributors. The goal of the beta is not to build a full social network, but to create a reliable, production-quality system that allows us to observe member behavior and measure participation in paid sessions. We are looking for a strong full stack developer who can take the defined system architecture and implement the platform end to end. This role includes backend development, frontend implementation, database design, and deployment of the beta environment. Project Overview The platform will support a small membership room where users can post short text contributions that remain visible for a limited period of time. Other members can interact with those contributions in a way that extends their lifespan. The system will also support scheduled paid sessions where members can participate in discussions or content hosted by invited practitioners. The platform must track behavioral activity across the system so we can evaluate engagement and participation patterns during the beta phase. The system will operate with strict constraints such as a fixed membership cap and inactivity rules that affect account status. Core System Features User Identity System • User registration and authentication • Fixed membership cap (approximately 200 active users) • Unique identity assignment for each member • Identity status management (active, inactive, pending transfer, etc.) • Activity tracking to determine member engagement Content Contribution System • Members can post short text contributions • Each contribution has a defined lifespan (time-based expiration) • Contributions automatically expire unless extended by interaction • Interaction mechanism that extends the lifespan of contributions • Tracking of interaction counts and timestamps Activity Feed • Chronological display of active contributions • Visibility of contribution lifespan and remaining time • Ability for members to interact with contributions • Ability to create new contributions Paid Session Module • System for hosting scheduled paid sessions • Members can view upcoming sessions • Payment required to access paid portions of sessions • Stripe payment integration • Access control for session participants Inactivity and Account Status Management • System tracks meaningful user activity • Warning triggers for inactivity • Account status changes after extended inactivity • Administrative ability to manage member status and transfers Behavioral Data Logging The platform must track and log key system events such as: • contribution creation • interaction events • contribution expiration • session participation • payment events • user activity and retention patterns This data will be used to evaluate the success of the beta experiment. Admin and Operations Tools Administrative tools are required for managing the beta environment, including: • member management • identity issuance and cap enforcement • session management • activity monitoring • payment status verification • manual handling of account status changes when required System Requirements The system does not need to support large scale at this stage but must be stable, well structured, and maintainable. Important priorities include: • reliable content lifecycle logic • accurate event logging • stable payment flow • clear admin control of the beta environment Developer Responsibilities • lead the engineering direction • build system architecture • Implement backend services and APIs • Implement frontend user interface • Design and maintain database models • Implement time-based content lifecycle logic • Integrate payment system • Implement user activity tracking and logging • Build admin tools required for operating the beta • Deploy and maintain the beta environment Ideal Candidate • Strong full stack development experience • Experience building production web applications • Comfortable implementing complex application logic • Experience with payment integration • Ability to translate system architecture into production code • Comfortable working on early stage product experiments Nice to Have • Experience building community platforms or discussion systems • Experience with event driven architectures • Experience implementing analytics or behavioral tracking To Apply Please include: • examples of platforms or systems you have built • your preferred technology stack for a system like this • a brief explanation of how you would approach building the core content lifecycle system Agencies won't be considered in any case. If you're not AI, include "Lead architecture" in the first of proposal.

title: Full-Stack Developer Needed to Build Domain Security Diagnostic Platform (WordPress + Node API)
description: We are an IT Managed Service Provider (MSP) building a domain diagnostic platform used in outbound email campaigns to help companies identify potential issues with their email infrastructure and domain security. The system will generate automated domain reports that check items like: -email deliverability -SPF / DMARC configuration -DNS records -website security headers -infrastructure indicators The reports will be accessed via personalized URLs sent in email campaigns. The goal is to provide useful public-facing insights and offer a free consultation CTA. This project combines: -WordPress frontend integration -a Node/TypeScript scan engine -DNS/email analysis modules -caching and API endpoints ------------------------ Project Architecture The system has two components: WordPress website WordPress will: -host marketing pages -route tool URLs -render the page template -load the scan results using JavaScript Example: /tools/email-deliverability/example.com A lightweight WordPress plugin will: -parse the route -load a template -inject configuration for the scan engine -Scan Engine (separate service) -The scan engine will run as a separate application. Recommended stack: -Node.js or Next.js -TypeScript -PostgreSQL -Redis caching It will provide APIs such as: /api/scan /api/event /api/request-review Scan Features The scan engine will analyze public domain configuration including: -Email authentication -SPF records -DMARC policy -DKIM selector checks -DNS configuration -A records -MX records -TXT records -NS records -Infrastructure detection -email providers (Google, Microsoft 365, etc.) -mail gateways -SaaS indicators Web security indicators -HTTPS -security headers -TLS certificate info -Additional modules (phase 2) -breach exposure checks -subdomain discovery -lookalike domain detection UI The UI will be rendered by a small JS loader (embed.js) that: -reads domain + tool profile -calls the scan API -renders results -displays findings and recommendations -shows a CTA such as: -Request a Free Security Review Deliverables -WordPress -custom router plugin -/tools/profile/domain routing -template for scan pages -embed loader integration -Scan Engine -scan orchestrator -DNS/email scanning modules -scoring engine -caching with Redis -PostgreSQL storage -API endpoints -Frontend -embed.js loader -results rendering -loading state -error handling -CTA integration -Tracking The system should track: -page views -scans -CTA clicks -campaign parameters -Campaign parameters include: -campaign_id -lead_id -email -company -first_name -last_name We plan to launch an MVP first, then expand the system. MVP Tools Initial tools: Email Deliverability Check Domain Security Snapshot Email Spoofing Risk Website Security Snapshot Security Scan (combined report) What we will provide Timeline Ideal timeline: 3–5 weeks When applying please include Examples of Node/Next.js API projects you’ve built Any experience with: DNS querying email deliverability domain security tools Your estimated hours for an MVP version Confirmation you are comfortable integrating with WordPress Long-Term Opportunity This platform will expand into a larger lead-generation security assessment tool, so strong performers may be invited for ongoing development work.

title: Compliance Audit Tracker - Full Stack Web App (SaaS PoC)
description: About the Project We're building a web-based SaaS platform that helps B2B software companies manage compliance across frameworks like SOC 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR. The goal is to give mid-sized teams a practical alternative to enterprise GRC tools — something purpose-built, easy to use, and audit-ready without the overhead. This engagement is scoped as a proof of concept. We want a working product we can test with real users, not a prototype held together with duct tape. What We Need Built - Multi-tenant web app with user authentication and workspace isolation - Control library with mappings to SOC 2 and at least one other framework - Evidence upload and versioning system linked to individual controls - Automated gap analysis view showing outstanding items per framework - Audit-readiness dashboard with completion tracking per framework Tech Stack Open to discussion, but we expect something modern and maintainable. React or Next.js on the front end, Node.js or Python on the back end, and a hosted relational database (Postgres preferred). Who We're Looking For - Proven experience building multi-tenant SaaS products - Strong full stack skills — you should be comfortable owning both front and back end - Clean, documented code that another developer can pick up without a guided tour - Bonus: prior experience with compliance, GRC tools, or document management systems Engagement Details - Type: Fixed price or milestone-based - Scope: Proof of concept — core features only, no scope creep - Timeline: Looking to move quickly with the right person To Apply Share relevant work (especially any SaaS or multi-tenant projects), your rough estimate for this scope, and your preferred stack. Applications without examples will not be reviewed.

title: Technical Lead | Full-Stack Developer | PWA (No Agencies)
description: We are looking for an experienced full-stack developer / technical lead to take ownership of an existing production product. The platform is live, has an active user base, and generates revenue. Our team consists of seasoned operators and designers who’ve scaled businesses to $150M+ in revenue, raised VC funding, and taken companies public. 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭 𝐎𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰 The product currently consists of four main components: • User application (PWA) • Moderator application • CMS / admin tooling • Marketing website and gamified sign-up funnel The system also relies heavily on third-party integrations. 𝐂𝐮𝐫𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐤 • Frontend: React-based PWA • Backend / CMS: WordPress, WooCommerce, plugins • Hosting / Infrastructure: Hostinger Cloud Enterprise • CDN: Bunny.net (Optimizer + Shield) • Real-time messaging: CometChat, Pusher • Notifications / Push: OneSignal • Payments: EMP, Verotel • Verification: AgeChecker, ZeroBounce • CDP / Email / SMS: Newsman • Ads: Meta Conversion API • Internal tools: GitHub, Slack Your role will be to understand the existing architecture, resolve technical issues, and implement new features. For example, WordPress currently powers parts of the backend. Over time we will migrate to a more dedicated backend architecture. New UX/UI work will be provided through Figma designs. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys taking ownership of a real product and improving it over time, not just building isolated features. This is a full-time opportunity for the right developer. 𝐑𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 • Proven experience building and maintaining stable production systems • Strong full-stack experience across frontend (React, HTML/CSS/JS) and backend systems • Experience building performant PWAs and delivering near-native app user experiences • Handle diverse 3rd-party integrations, some of which have poor or minimal documentation • Strong debugging skills and ability to identify root causes in complex systems • Build with security best practices in mind (authentication, API key management, access control) • Extremely disciplined approach to development, staging, and production environments We value developers who: • Act as a technical sparring partner to identify inefficiencies and evaluate tradeoffs • Ship code consistently and at speed while taking ownership of outcomes • Use AI tools to move faster, but you must fully understand code you ship. No vibe coders • Paranoid about reliability, monitoring, and eliminating silent bleed that churns users • Are comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment with high expectations • Huge bonus points if you have experience with TurtleOS ;-) As the product and revenue grow, you will have the opportunity to build and lead a development team. Initially, however, you will be the sole developer responsible for the product and its codebase. 𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬 If interested, submit a proposal including: • Summary of relevant experience • Examples of products you built • Your availability (hours per week) • Keyword “anime” so we know you read the JD Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 𝐩𝐚𝐢𝐝 𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬, which includes: • Interview with CEO and technical advisor • Test project • Final decision

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer for Sports Betting Trading Dashboard (React / Laravel / AWS / Claude)
description: We are looking for a sharp, fast, highly reliable full-stack developer to help build an internal sports betting trading dashboard. This is not a generic CRUD app with lipstick on it. We need a real operator who can move quickly, think clearly, and build production-ready systems using React on the frontend and Laravel/PHP + MySQL on the backend, deployed on AWS (EC2 / RDS). The main goal is to build a dashboard that integrates with the Pinnacle API to fetch games, odds, and market data, then allow a trader to review opportunities, adjust strategy parameters, and place bets programmatically through the system. We want someone who is also comfortable using Claude / AI-assisted coding workflows to move fast without turning the codebase into a haunted house. What we need built The system will include things like: Pinnacle API integration to fetch games, markets, lines, and odds Internal dashboard for traders to monitor betting opportunities Configurable strategy settings and parameters inside the dashboard Ability for trader/admin users to tweak values such as thresholds, sizing logic, timing delays, review conditions, and execution rules Programmatic bet placement after trader review/approval Logging, audit trail, and error handling for all actions Admin tools for managing settings and monitoring system health Clean database structure and scalable backend architecture Deployment and server setup on AWS A practical example of the type of logic we want represented in the dashboard: compare Pinnacle lines vs internal trading parameters set different execution modes/settings adjust betting thresholds and sizing control timing, review delays, and order behavior avoid blindly firing orders without trader oversight Tech Stack Must be comfortable with: React Laravel / PHP MySQL AWS EC2 / RDS API integrations Git / clean version control practices AI-assisted development with Claude or similar tools Nice to have Big plus if you have experience with any of the following: Sports betting, odds feeds, sportsbooks, or trading systems Pinnacle API specifically Queue systems / background jobs Real-time dashboards WebSockets Logging / monitoring / alerting Security best practices for execution systems Docker Nginx / Linux server management What kind of developer we want We are looking for someone who: writes clean, maintainable code can think like a product builder, not just a ticket taker moves fast and communicates clearly can suggest architecture improvements instead of just following orders blindly understands reliability matters when money is involved is detail-oriented and careful with edge cases is comfortable shipping in phases and iterating quickly Important This is an internal tool for a serious workflow. Accuracy, reliability, and good judgment matter a lot. Please do not apply if you: are junior and need heavy hand-holding disappear for days build messy code fast and call it “startup speed” have no experience with backend architecture or AWS deployment When applying, please include Your experience with React, Laravel/PHP, MySQL, and AWS Any experience with sportsbook APIs, trading dashboards, or execution systems Your experience using Claude, Cursor, or other AI coding tools in real workflows Examples of similar systems or dashboards you have built How you would structure this project from MVP to production Your availability and timezone Project scope This will likely start as an MVP and then expand into a longer-term working relationship if things go well. We care more about quality, speed, and judgment than fancy talk. If you’re the kind of developer who can actually build, think, and ship, apply.

title: Full Stack Developer for Consumer Website and App
description: We are developing a consumer web-based service and app focused on user reviews and recommendations. I have a website that is 90% done but still has a lot of issues. I am interested in either using what we have already or starting over and developing it from scratch. Current tech stack: React MongoDb I am also interested in a possible redesign of the UI/UX/Aesthetic. I am looking for any one: - Full stack development including front end UI/UX design - Back end developer to fix what I currently have - Back end devleoper to possibly develop the whole site from scratch - UI/UX developer Fluency in English a must.

title: Senior Full Stack Developer
description: No agencies. Only freelancers. We are building the operating system for mortgage brokers and lenders. We handle the compliance, document review, and communication workflows that today live across spreadsheets, email threads, and outdated portals. What the role actually looks like Some weeks you're heads-down shipping a feature alongside our lead engineer. Other weeks you're taking full ownership of a slice of the product from design to deploy, making the call on how something should work and why. There's no PM writing specs for you. There's no QA team catching your bugs. There's no tech lead doing the thinking and handing you tasks. If you need that structure to do your best work, this probably isn't the right fit. But if you get energized by that kind of ownership, read on. What we're looking for You think before you build. When you pick up a ticket, you're already asking what the edge cases are, what could break, what's missing from the spec. You raise those questions early and push back when something doesn't make sense. You care about the quality of what ships. Not in a perfectionist, never-ship way, but in a "I want this to actually work and look good" way. Clean code is the baseline, not the goal. You've worked in a codebase that was moving fast and you know how to make good decisions with incomplete information. You use AI tooling sensibly. You know when it's helping and when it's producing slop that needs to be cleaned up. The stack TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB. We also work with Claude/Anthropic APIs for AI features we're building into the product. You don't need to have used every tool here but you should be comfortable picking things up fast. What we offer $100k–$130k depending on experience, and we're willing to flex on that for the right person. Fully remote, work from wherever you are. You'll be an early and meaningful part of the team, not a headcount. How we hire First, a take-home test. It's designed to take 3-4 hours and involves a small, well-defined task in a real slice of our codebase. The scope is intentionally narrow and the expectations are clearly laid out. We're looking for clean code and quality of thought. Because the task is so tightly scoped, how you perform here is a strong signal of how you'd perform in the role. If that goes well, a work trial (~20 hours). You'll pick up a real feature that touches multiple parts of the platform. What we care about most here is not how much you shipped. It's how well you thought through the problem. - Did you consider the edge cases? - Did you think about how this feature interacts with the rest of the product? - Did you ask the right questions along the way? A feature that's 30% complete but thoughtfully built and well-reasoned tells us more than something that's "100% done" but patchy. We're not looking for order takers. We're looking for engineers who can genuinely think for themselves. We've tried to make this process feel less like a hiring gauntlet and more like a genuine look at how we'd actually work together. If you think you're a good fit, please reply to this job posting with ONLY the answer from this question here: https://loanscape-hiring.netlify.app/ All other responses will be rejected for lack of not paying attention to detail.

title: Senior AI Engineer — Agentic Systems, Full-Stack (Claude / LLM / TypeScript)
description: Job Description: I'm the founder of a multi-company portfolio spanning energy efficiency, software, direct sourcing, EV infrastructure, and AR inspection technology. I'm building a suite of 17+ proprietary AI-powered tools to run and scale my operations — and I need a senior engineer who can be a true long-term technical partner across this entire ecosystem. This is not a one-off project. This is a foundational engagement with significant runway. The right person grows with the portfolio. The Ecosystem: BC1 / BetterCity One All tools are built under a unified platform ecosystem called BC1 (BetterCity One) — shared infrastructure, auth, and data layers across my companies. This includes BetterCity Energy (30-year NYC energy efficiency firm, 3,000+ projects), Solvur Labs (software & design), Trovi Direct Sourcing, EV Surge, and PROVA (AR inspection tech). The 17+ tool pipeline includes: Ava — AI executive assistant platform (first build, immediate start) BC1 CRM — Full client relationship management (Next.js / PostgreSQL / Prisma / Vercel, existing codebase) BC1 Lighting Catalogue — Product database web app (111 products, indoor/outdoor, Playwright test suite) BC1 Invest — EV charging financial calculator (21-phase model, deployed) SpeakSharp — Communication coaching platform (Read.ai transcript analysis) Sales intake & pipeline tooling for LinkedIn/cold outreach operations ClickUp automation layer (n8n workflows, custom rules engine) PROVA Site Intelligence — AR-adjacent AI inspection tools for the built environment EV charging proposal generator Operational reporting dashboards Additional CRM, marketing, and sourcing tools across portfolio companies This is a living pipeline. New tools are scoped and added regularly. Project 1: Ava / DCC System (Immediate Start) Ava is a personal AI executive assistant built on a two-component architecture: DCC (web platform) — The interface I interact with daily. Built with TypeScript / Fastify / Prisma / PostgreSQL / Redis / BullMQ / React. DCC owns all OAuth tokens and exposes internal API endpoints. OpenClaw (agent layer) — The AI agent runtime. Accesses all external APIs only through DCC endpoints — never directly. A complete documentation suite is already written and ready for handoff: CLAUDE.md, SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TASK.md, and a full DCC API spec. The 16-week build plan is locked with MVP at Week 10. 35 behavioral sections, 18 subsystems. Key integrations: Microsoft Graph, Gmail, ClickUp, Twilio, ElevenLabs, YouTube, Voyage AI, Google Maps, Anthropic API (Claude Haiku for classification, Sonnet for generation) Testing protocol (three-layer): Claude Code → logic validation | OpenClaw / Puppeteer → browser automation | Dan → visual/UX sign-off What I'm Looking For: Must-haves: 5+ years software engineering, 2+ years in production AI/LLM systems Deep hands-on experience with the Anthropic Claude API — tool use, multi-turn conversations, agentic loops Strong TypeScript / Node.js backend (Fastify or Express) PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM Redis + BullMQ task queuing OAuth 2.0 integration (Microsoft Graph and Gmail API required) React frontend — functional and clean (not design-heavy) Git discipline: versioned commits, clean PRs, documented handoffs Comfortable working from detailed architecture docs, SOPs, and spec-driven development Clear async communicator — Loom updates, written status logs, no chasing required Strong bonus: Experience with Claude Code CLI Playwright / Puppeteer automated testing n8n workflow automation ElevenLabs or voice pipeline integration ClickUp API Next.js / Vercel deployment Experience auditing and inheriting existing codebases Working Style & Expectations: I run a documentation-first operation. Every system has a spec. Every build has a task log. You will be expected to update docs as you build — not as an afterthought. You'll work closely with me (the founder/CEO) and coordinate with a Colombia-based team (systems, ClickUp, QA) and a Systems Architect. You are the senior technical lead — you own architecture decisions, code quality, and build sequencing across the ecosystem. I've terminated a prior agency relationship due to poor execution. title: Full Stack Developer Needed to Build Beta Version of a Membership Platform (End-to-End)
description: We are building the beta version of a new digital membership platform designed to test a specific interaction model and economic behavior within a small, controlled community. This beta platform will operate with a limited number of members (approximately 200 users) and includes a few core mechanics centered around time-limited content, member interaction that can extend content lifespan, and periodic paid sessions hosted by guest contributors. The goal of the beta is not to build a full social network, but to create a reliable, production-quality system that allows us to observe member behavior and measure participation in paid sessions. We are looking for a strong full stack developer who can take the defined system architecture and implement the platform end to end. This role includes backend development, frontend implementation, database design, and deployment of the beta environment. Project Overview The platform will support a small membership room where users can post short text contributions that remain visible for a limited period of time. Other members can interact with those contributions in a way that extends their lifespan. The system will also support scheduled paid sessions where members can participate in discussions or content hosted by invited practitioners. The platform must track behavioral activity across the system so we can evaluate engagement and participation patterns during the beta phase. The system will operate with strict constraints such as a fixed membership cap and inactivity rules that affect account status. Core System Features User Identity System • User registration and authentication • Fixed membership cap (approximately 200 active users) • Unique identity assignment for each member • Identity status management (active, inactive, pending transfer, etc.) • Activity tracking to determine member engagement Content Contribution System • Members can post short text contributions • Each contribution has a defined lifespan (time-based expiration) • Contributions automatically expire unless extended by interaction • Interaction mechanism that extends the lifespan of contributions • Tracking of interaction counts and timestamps Activity Feed • Chronological display of active contributions • Visibility of contribution lifespan and remaining time • Ability for members to interact with contributions • Ability to create new contributions Paid Session Module • System for hosting scheduled paid sessions • Members can view upcoming sessions • Payment required to access paid portions of sessions • Stripe payment integration • Access control for session participants Inactivity and Account Status Management • System tracks meaningful user activity • Warning triggers for inactivity • Account status changes after extended inactivity • Administrative ability to manage member status and transfers Behavioral Data Logging The platform must track and log key system events such as: • contribution creation • interaction events • contribution expiration • session participation • payment events • user activity and retention patterns This data will be used to evaluate the success of the beta experiment. Admin and Operations Tools Administrative tools are required for managing the beta environment, including: • member management • identity issuance and cap enforcement • session management • activity monitoring • payment status verification • manual handling of account status changes when required System Requirements The system does not need to support large scale at this stage but must be stable, well structured, and maintainable. Important priorities include: • reliable content lifecycle logic • accurate event logging • stable payment flow • clear admin control of the beta environment Developer Responsibilities • build system architecture • Implement backend services and APIs • Implement frontend user interface • Design and maintain database models • Implement time-based content lifecycle logic • Integrate payment system • Implement user activity tracking and logging • Build admin tools required for operating the beta • Deploy and maintain the beta environment Ideal Candidate • Strong full stack development experience • Experience building production web applications • Comfortable implementing complex application logic • Experience with payment integration • Ability to translate system architecture into production code • Comfortable working on early stage product experiments Nice to Have • Experience building community platforms or discussion systems • Experience with event driven architectures • Experience implementing analytics or behavioral tracking To Apply Please include: • examples of platforms or systems you have built • your preferred technology stack for a system like this • a brief explanation of how you would approach building the core content lifecycle system

title: Full-Stack Developer for GIS-Enabled Client Data Portal
description: We are seeking a skilled full-stack developer to create a GIS-enabled client data portal. The project involves building a React frontend integrated with Leaflet maps for interactive mapping features, as well as processing shapefiles and developing Recharts dashboards for data visualization. The ideal candidate will have experience in both frontend and backend technologies, ensuring a seamless user experience and efficient data handling. We have a fully functional interactive prototype built in React that demonstrates the complete user experience, page layouts, data flows, and feature set. This is not a wireframe — it's a working front-end with real component logic, role-based access, charts, an interactive map, and an embedded AI assistant. The prototype covers: - Login & role-based access (Admin, Internal, Client) - Multi-client switching - Dashboard with KPIs, filters, charts, trend indicators, and activity feed - Interactive leak map (Leaflet/OpenStreetMap) - inspection register with search, filters, expandable rows, and CSV/PDF export - project schedule with Gantt timeline - project results comparison charts - Weekly performance analytics (stacked bar, area, composed charts) - Daily locations with employee-level tracking - Photo gallery - Document library with download buttons - Alert & report rule management (threshold alerts, daily/weekly/monthly scheduled reports) - Module configuration per client (toggle features on/off) - Embedded AI chatbot (Claude API) scoped to active client data The prototype uses sample/hardcoded data.** The production build needs to connect to our live data sources. | Layer | Preference | Notes | |---|---|---| | Frontend | React (Next.js preferred) | Prototype is already in React | | UI Library | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Clean, modern, consistent with prototype aesthetic | | Charts | Recharts | Already used in prototype | | Maps | Leaflet + OpenStreetMap | Already working in prototype; Mapbox acceptable | | Backend | Node.js / Next.js API routes | Or Python/FastAPI if preferred | | Database | PostgreSQL or Supabase | For user accounts, alert rules, config; Airtable remains source of truth for leak data | | Auth | NextAuth.js, Clerk, or Supabase Auth | Must support role-based access | | File Storage | AWS S3 or Google Cloud Storage | For photos and documents | | Email/SMS | SendGrid or AWS SES + Twilio | For alert and report delivery | | Hosting | Vercel, AWS, or similar | Must support Australian region for latency | | AI | Anthropic Claude API (Sonnet) | Already integrated in prototype |

description: I am building a regional senior care network platform that helps families find assisted living, memory care, and board & care facilities. The project needs to include a web app and the ability to convert it into a mobile app (iOS initially, Android later). I am looking for a developer who can build a clean, scalable MVP with strong backend structure. This is not a complex AI project, but it must be built professionally with a solid database and admin system. Core Features: Public User Side Search facilities by city / county Filter by categories (Assisted Living, Board & Care, Memory Care) Facility profile pages with photos, description, amenities, pricing range Map view of facility locations “Check Availability” / “Schedule a Tour” call-to-action Lead capture form that routes inquiries to admin dashboard Click-to-call button (using call tracking numbers) Admin Dashboard Manage facilities (add/edit/remove listings) Lead management pipeline (new lead → contacted → tour scheduled → move-in → closed) Track referral source Track which facility the lead was matched with Revenue tracking field Basic analytics (lead count, placements, etc.) Additional Requirements Clean and scalable database (Postgres or similar) Ability to export all data Fast search performance Mobile-friendly responsive design Hosting recommendations Mobile App Requirement After the web app is complete, we want to convert it into an iOS app. This can be done via: React Native Flutter PWA wrapper or another clean approach that Apple will approve. Please explain how you would approach this. Tech Stack (Preferred but flexible) We are open to recommendations but prefer modern scalable tools such as: React / Next.js Node.js PostgreSQL Supabase / Firebase Map integration (Google Maps) If you recommend something different, please explain why. Project Scope This is an MVP build focused on: functionality reliability clean backend structure We are not looking for unnecessary complexity or expensive enterprise architecture. Future phases may include additional features once the platform is live. Timeline We would like a working MVP in 5–8 weeks. Budget $8,000 – $15,000 depending on experience and proposed architecture. To Apply Please include: Examples of web apps or directories you’ve built The tech stack you recommend Your approach to building both web and mobile versions Confirmation that we will own the full source code Estimated timeline Ideal Candidate Strong full-stack developer Experience building marketplaces or directories Clean database architecture Experience submitting apps to the Apple App Store is a plus Long-term collaboration is possible if the project goes well.

title: RFP: Full-Stack Automation Expert (n8n + Supabase + Anthropic) VIBE CODER SPECIALTY
description: Project Overview We are building Prolinkhub.ai, an AI-driven lead generation and recruitment platform for the home services industry (specializing in HVAC). We have the core architecture started in n8n and Supabase, but we need an expert to finalize the backend "plumbing," specifically regarding AI memory, database schema, and API integrations. Scope of Work AI Agent Memory Setup: Configure Postgres Chat Memory in n8n using our Supabase instance. This includes creating the correct jsonb tables and session_id logic so the agent remembers homeowner interactions. API Troubleshooting: Resolve 401/404 errors with Anthropic (Claude) API credentials within the n8n environment. Lead Data Pipeline: Finalize the "Create a Row" logic to extract lead details (Name, Phone, HVAC issue) from the AI conversation and inject them into a structured Supabase leads table. Synta.io Integration: Optimize the workflow using Synta.io to ensure the connection between n8n and Supabase is high-performance and error-resistant. Error Handling: Implement robust error-handling nodes so the workflow doesn't stall if a user provides incomplete information. Required Technical Stack n8n: Advanced workflow design, including AI Agent nodes and custom expressions. Supabase/PostgreSQL: Database schema design, Row Level Security (RLS), and jsonb data handling. LLMs: Expertise in Anthropic Claude 3 (Haiku/Sonnet) and prompt engineering for lead qualification. Synta.io: Experience using Synta as a bridge for n8n/Supabase is a major plus. Timeline Goal: Fully functional backend within 3–5 business days.

The project includes front-end and back-end development, AI product recommendations, and AI customer service integration. Let us know your availability and how you’d tackle the project. We are seeking a skilled developer to create a comprehensive ecommerce website capable of handling around 200 products. The site should incorporate AI-driven solutions for customer service, sales, and marketing to enhance user experience and streamline operations. The ideal candidate will have a robust understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies. Please share your previous work examples and how you plan to implement AI in this project.
